translation_verse: Kyɛɛ hʋ nala aa sɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ, baala bɩl bɩ sɩ paa haana, haana mɛ bɩl bɩ sɩ yal balama. Ba buloŋ sɩ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ malɩkasɩ aa we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
translation_verse: Ʋ baa, ‘Mɩyaŋ nɛ yaa Wɩɩsɩ hʋ Abɩraham abee Azɩkɩ abee Gyeekɔbɩ buloŋ gyɩ aa tɩŋa. Wɩɩ no ʋ aa basa, ʋ daga anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ yaa niweye Wɩɩsɩ rɛ, ʋ bɩ yaa sʋʋnɩ Wɩɩsɩ.”’

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: Nɩgyamaa hʋ aa nɩɩ gɛɛ ʋ aa daga nala, ba nyʋʋsɩ fɩyɛlɩ. Wɩɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ tɩyaŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩɩ Maakɩ

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Farasiima hʋ aa nɩya anɩɩ Yesu basɩ wɩya lo Sadusiima ba nyʋʋsɩ gyʋʋ kpʋlɩya gɛɛ, ba ko yaa nyʋʋdɩgɩ a sii kpa mʋ Yesu lee.

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: a baa, “Nɩhɩyawʋ, Wɩɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, ba kɩbee rɛ yaa ba buloŋ tɩyaŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩɩ?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ buloŋ wɩbal abee ba tɩyaŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩɩ rɛ gɛɛ.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Wɩlɩya tɩɩna mɛ aa yaa sɩfɩyaŋ wɩɩ rɛ nyɛ: ‘Kyo ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna anɩɩ ɩ aa kyo ɩ tɩɩ gɛɛ.”’

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: Farasiima hʋ ha aa laŋŋa gɛɛ, Yesu pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa,

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: Beewɩya Deviti fa baa: ‘Ŋ Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ basɩ tɩya ŋ Tɩɩna a baa, hɔŋ ŋ noduu logiŋ nɩhɔnɩɩ, a mʋ pele saŋa hʋ ŋ aa sɩ kpa ɩ dɔmɔ we ɩ nosi tɩyaŋ.’

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Ɛɛ rɛ ma bɩɩnɩ Krisita, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya wɩya? Kɩbee doho nal lɛ ʋ yaa?” Ɛɛ rɛ ba baa dɩ ʋ yaa Deviti nihii rɛ.

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Dɩ Deviti tɩɩ rɛ yaŋ yɩrɩ Krisita hʋ ʋ Tɩɩna, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sɩ yaa a yaa ʋ nihii.”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Yesu aa basɩ gɛɛ, nal buloŋ bɩl bɩ wuwo laa wɩɩ basɩ. A lɩɩ haŋ kyɛɛ hʋ a kaŋ kɩ mʋ, nal buloŋ mɛ bɩl bɩ kaŋ nyuduwo ʋ aa sɩ wuwo lɩɩ sɩŋ a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ wɩɩ buloŋ.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Nal hʋ buloŋ aa kpa ʋ tɩɩ yohuŋ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kana ʋ hʋwɔlɩ, ka nal hʋ mɛ aa kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ hʋwɔlɩ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kpa ʋ yohuŋ.

english_verse: And now your temple will be deserted.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ baanɩ ma bee tɩya ma dɩ ʋ pɩŋ gyikpiye.

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: Ʋ maga dɩ ma tɩyaŋ sɩlaal bɩrɩmɩ haŋbiye rɛ a tʋŋ tʋma kɩ tɩya ma buloŋ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: Mamaa Gyerusalɛm nala, Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋ ʋ tɩŋdaala ma lee rɛ, ma kpʋ badɔmɔŋ a paa bʋwa yaga badɔmɔŋ mɛ. Saŋa buloŋ nɛ ŋ fa kyo dɩ ŋ puŋ tɔ ma buloŋ anɩɩ gyimii aa puŋ tɔ ʋ balɩya gɛɛ, ka ma bɩ laa nyʋwa.

english_verse: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, dɩ nal lɛ ŋmɩyɛsɩ wɩɩsʋllɩ-kɩna kogu hʋ, ʋ ŋmɩyɛsɩ kogu hʋ bee kɩŋ hʋ buloŋ aa dɔbɔ ʋ a tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya nɩgyamaa hʋ abee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa,

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: “Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ abee Farasiima hʋ, ba aa rɛ kaŋ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ba kɩ daga nala Wɩɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ Moosi aa saba biŋ.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ma maga dɩ ma kɩ tɩŋ wɩya hʋ buloŋ ba aa baa dɩ ma kɩ yaa rɛ, amɛ ma ta kɩ kyese ba wɩyaalɩya, beewɩya ba tɩɩ bee tɩŋ wɩya hʋ ba aa daga ma.

english_verse: They pile heavy burdens on people's shoulders and won't lift a finger to help.
translation_verse: Ba aa vʋwa kyʋgɩ yoho rɛ a kpa kyʋgɩ nala, amɛ ba tɩɩ bɩ sɩ laa nyʋwa pɛ ba tɩyaŋ mʋhʋ mɛ dɩ ba wuwo kyʋŋ kyʋgɩsɩ hʋ.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: Ba aa yaa wɩɩ buloŋ a kɩ kyɛ dɩ nala na ba rɛ. Ba aa sabɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya a we dagibiisi tɩyaŋ nɛ a kpa vʋwa ba gannɩ nosi tɩyaŋ abee ba nyupugini tɩyaŋ, aŋ leŋ ba gannɩ nyʋʋgyʋwasa mɛ doliye doliye a te ɛɛ hʋ nɩwaasɩma kɩna aa kɩya.

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: Ba aa kɩ kyɛ dɩ nala kɩ kyʋwalɩ ba rɛ abee gyɩrɩma gyamaa tɩyaŋ aŋ kɩ yɩrɩ ba nɩhɩyasɩ.

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: Amɛ ma kɛ ta leŋ dɩ nala kaŋ ma kɩ yɩrɩ nɩhɩyasɩ, beewɩya ma buloŋ yaa nɩmmabalɩya rɛ, aŋka ma nɩhɩyawʋ mɛ yaa nɩdɩgɩ dʋŋ.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Ʋ bɩl bɩ maga dɩ ma yɩrɩ nal buloŋ ma kuwo dʋnɩya no tɩyaŋ, beewɩya ma Kuwo yaa nɩdɩgɩ dʋŋ nɛ a we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
translation_verse: Ma ta bɩl kaŋ nal buloŋ mɛ yɩrɩ ma sɩlaal, beewɩya ma sɩlaal yaa nɩdɩgɩ dʋŋ nɛ, ʋ rɛ yaa Krisita, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩmma! Ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Beewɩya ma aa kaŋ lohaana kɩna kɩ fasɩ rɛ, aŋ aa fugo kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɩ pɩɩsɩ. Ɛɛwɩya Wɩɩsɩ sɩ dɔgɩsɩ ma a te nal buloŋ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩmma! Ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Ma aa sii duwori nɩɩ rɛ a mʋ paalʋʋ dʋŋsɩ a kɩ basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya. Dɩ nɩdɩgɩ rɛ ko laa di, ma aa leŋ ʋ marɩ bɔmɔ rɛ, a maga dɩ ʋ tɩɩna gyʋʋ diŋ a na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ anɩɩ ma mɛ aa sɩ na gɛɛ.

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: Mamaa nyʋlʋŋ-sɩlaala, ma sɩ na tʋwara! Ma aa baa, “Dɩ nal lɛ ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ, ʋ bɩ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ amɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ ko ŋmɩyɛsɩ salma kɩna hʋ aa we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ rɛ.”

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Ɛɛdɔŋ nyʋlʋŋbambugo! Salma kɩna hʋ aa we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ abee dɩya hʋ aa leŋ salma hʋ bɩ kaŋ bisiŋ Wɩɩsɩ sɩya tɩyaŋ, kɩna no balɩya, kɩbee rɛ yaa sɩfɩyaŋ kɩŋ?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Ma bɩl basa anɩɩ dɩ nal lɛ ŋmɩyɛsɩ wɩɩsʋllɩ-kɩna kogu hʋ tɩyaŋ ba aa kpa kɩna kɩ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ, ʋ bɩ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ, amɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ ŋmɩyɛsɩ kɩŋ hʋ ba aa kpaa dɔbɔ wɩɩsʋllɩ-kɩna kogu hʋ nyuu a sʋla Wɩɩsɩ, ʋ yaa ŋmɩyɛsʋʋ rɛ.

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: Dɩ nal lɛ bɩl ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ, ʋ ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩsɩ dɩya hʋ abee Wɩɩsɩ buloŋ nɛ, beewɩya Wɩɩsɩ we dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: Dɩ nal lɛ bɩl ŋmɩyɛsɩ wɩɩsɩbee, ʋ ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩsɩ kuworikpasa hʋ abee nal hʋ aa hɔŋ ʋ tɩyaŋ buloŋ nɛ gɛɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩmma! Ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Paaponni hʋ buloŋ ma aa kpa we kɩdiiliye tɩyaŋ ba sʋwala sʋma, ma aa kaŋ ba kpaa leye fi fi rɛ, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ. Ma aa yaa wɩmʋlla no buloŋ nɛ aŋ vɩya dɩ ma tɩŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩya hʋ Moosi fa aa biŋ tɩya ma anɩɩ ma kaŋ wɩtɩɩ wɩɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, a kɩ fá nala sikii, a kɩ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ abee wɩtɩɩ. Ma aa kaŋ ma kɩna kpaa leye fi fi, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ gɛɛ, ʋ yaa wɩwelii rɛ, amɛ ɛɛ bɩ daga dɩ ma vɩya wɩkaalɩya hʋ kɛ ta.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Mamaa nyʋlʋŋ sɩlaala! Ma aa kyuwori ma nɩɩ rɛ, a lɩɩ naŋkyʋwa ta, aŋ kpa kokumo lile.

english_verse: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
translation_verse: Ɛɛdɔŋ nyʋlɩma! Ma bɩ gyɩma anɩɩ dɩ mamaa rɛ laa sɩya sasɩ nɩɩnyʋwagbaŋbiisi no bee ma kɩdigbaŋsɩ no tɩsɩ ba hala mɛ sɩ kyaanɩ weliŋ?

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩmma! Ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Beewɩya ma kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ bilni ba aa kpaa tʋwɔl bʋgɩ. Ba hala aa weliye rɛ, aŋka ba tɩsɩ buloŋ su abee sʋʋnɩ hogo abee kɩpʋwanɩ iriŋ kɛ buloŋ.

english_verse: That's what you are like. Outside you look good, but inside you are evil and only pretend to be good.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ma mɛ kɩɩ nala sɩya tɩyaŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ na ma, ba aa bɩɩnɩ nɩɩ ma yaa nɩtɩɩma rɛ, ka ma tɩsɩ su abee wɩbɔmɔ abee minaafigi wɩya.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ! Ma yaa minaafigi tɩmma rɛ, beewɩya ma aa saa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala abee wɩtɩɩ tɩmma bilni rɛ a marɩ ba mɛ weliŋ.

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: Ma wɩbasɩya kɔnɩ daga anɩɩ ma yaa nala hʋ aa kpʋʋ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala hʋ doho nala rɛ.

english_verse: So keep on doing everything they did.
translation_verse: Wɩbɔmɔ hʋ ma naabaala aa piile, ma mɛ kaŋ teŋsi.

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: Na ba aa bɔmɔ gɛɛ anɩɩ dʋma! Ma bɩɩna anɩɩ ma sɩ wuwo fá gɛ diŋ gyʋʋyɩ rɛ koo?

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, nala no ba aa kpʋwa, ba haakye buloŋ dɔbɔ ma gyɩnaŋ nala nyuu tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, ma bɩl bɩ sɩ na ŋ, see saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ ma aa sɩ lɔllɩ ŋ a baa, ‘Nyusʋŋtɩɩna rɛ yaa nal hʋ aa ko Wɩɩsɩ feŋ tɩyaŋ.”’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Ma tɩɩ naa gɛɛ ba aa weliye rɛ, amɛ wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ba sɩ maakyiye ŋmaa dɩɩsɩ no buloŋ lo. Bʋdɩgɩ mɛ gba bɩl bɩ sɩ ka a dɔbɔ ʋ dɔŋtɩɩna tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Beewɩya, nala yʋga aa sɩ ko kpa ŋ feŋ anɩɩ ba aa rɛ yaa Krisita hʋ, a mʋrɩ nɩgyamaa.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Ma sɩ nɩɩ yuwo dɔmɔ lee buloŋ tɩyaŋ. Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko nɩɩ gɛɛ, ma ta leŋ dɩ ma bembiye kɩ sii. Beewɩya wɩya no buloŋ sɩ yaa, amɛ ɛɛ bɩ daga anɩɩ dʋnɩya tenii pele rɛ.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu gyɩ mʋ hɔŋ Olivi dogimo hʋ nyuu tɩyaŋ, doŋ nɛ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ dʋŋ sii ko ʋ lee a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Saŋa bee tɩyaŋ nɛ wɩya no sɩ yaa? Bekiŋ wɩmagɩl lɛ á sɩ na a gyɩŋ saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ ɩ aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko, abee saŋa hʋ dʋnɩya aa sɩ teŋ?”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Yuwo sɩ we dʋnɩya logiŋ buloŋ tɩyaŋ. Losi mɛ sɩ gyʋʋ, ka dɩ taŋha mɛ gyigili lɩgyamaa tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Haŋ saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ, haluwokanna bee biifɩllɩ nɩmma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ.

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Wɩya no buloŋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ haluwokannɩ luwol aa piile kɩ wɩɩ.

english_verse: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ ba sɩ kasɩ ma a kpa ma we nala nosi tɩyaŋ dɩ ba dɔgɩsɩ ma aŋ kpʋ ma. Dʋnɩya nala buloŋ sɩ kuu ŋ wɩya a haasɩ ma.

english_verse: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
translation_verse: Haŋ saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ nɩgyamaa sɩ kpa ba Wɩɩsɩ laadii ta. Ba sɩ kaŋ gyamba bee dɔmɔŋ a haasɩ dɔmɔŋ.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ aa nyɩya wɩya a baa ba yaa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala rɛ, ba sɩ yʋga a ko a mʋrɩ nɩgyamaa.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ nal buloŋ nɛ ko basɩ tɩya ɩ a baa, ‘Na Krisita hʋ we daha, koo ʋ we lee kɩdɩgɩ, ta laa ʋ tɩɩna di.’

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Ma kɩ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ dɩ kyɛ hʋ ma aa si fá ta ko yaa puwo saŋa koo Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko na kɩbɔŋlɔrɩ hʋ aa yaa sɩna hʋ wɩya Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ aa tɩŋ ʋ tɩŋdaal Danɩyɛl tɩyaŋ a basɩ, dɩ ʋ sɩŋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ maga dɩ ma nala hʋ aa karɩmɩ wɩya no lʋga rɛ aŋ gyɩŋ ba memii.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko na gɛɛ, ʋ maga dɩ nala hʋ aa we Gyudɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ fá gyɩŋ dogiŋsi nyuni rɛ a faŋa.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Dɩ nal buloŋ nɛ we ʋ baga tɩyaŋ, ʋ ta bɩl baa ʋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ dɩya a paa ʋ gannɩ.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Dɩ Wɩɩsɩ rɛ fa bɩ kere kyɛyɛ hʋ wɩya no aa sɩ yaa ta, nɩdɩgɩ mɛ fa bɩ sɩ ka beewɩya tʋwara hʋ aa sɩ ko sɩ lɔrɩ rɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Amɛ akuu nala hʋ ʋ aa lɩya wɩya, ʋ sɩ kere kyɛyɛ hʋ ta.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ dɩ mamaa rɛ ko na wɩya no buloŋ aa yaa, ma gyɩma anɩɩ mɩyaŋ nihuwobiŋ Biye koyi mɛ kɔnɩ kpaga rɛ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko, ʋ sɩ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ wɩya hʋ gyɩ aa yaa saŋa hʋ Nowa gyɩ aa we ʋ mɩɩbol tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: Nal buloŋ bɩ gyɩŋ kyɛɛ hʋ wɩya no aa sɩ yaa. Malɩkasɩ aa we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ mɛ gba paalɩ bɩ gyɩma, see ŋ Kuwo Wɩɩsɩ dʋŋ.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Ka dɩ duwoŋ hʋ gyɩ na nɩɩ a su dʋnɩya buloŋ, nala gyɩ aa di kɩdiiliye rɛ, a kɩ nyʋwa sɩŋ. Baala kɩ paa haana, haana mɛ kɩ yala balama, a mʋ pele kyɛɛ hʋ Nowa gyɩ aa gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ.

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ bɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ aa yaa see saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ nɩɩ hʋ aa ko paa ba buloŋ mʋ. Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ sɩ yaa saŋa hʋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ baala balɩya sɩ kɩ pala baga tɩyaŋ dɩ Wɩɩsɩ ko kpa kɩdɩgɩ aŋ leŋ kɩdɩgɩ ta.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya ma yaŋ gyɩŋ tɩya ma tɩɩ weliŋ, beewɩya ma bɩ gyɩŋ kyɛɛ hʋ ma Tɩɩna aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Ma mɛ yaŋ yaa siri pɔ, beewɩya mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ ma aa bɩ yɩyɛla.” Tʋŋtʋnnɩ buloŋ maga ʋ kaŋ wɩtɩɩ rɛ Luka

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Amɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ yaa tʋŋtʋnnɩbɔŋ, ʋ aa basɩ tɩya ʋ tɩɩ rɛ a baa, ‘Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ bɩ sɩ ko lagɩlagɩ no. Ʋ sɩ pɩɩsɩ.’

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ʋ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ sɩ kpa ʋ kɩna buloŋ we tʋŋtʋnnɩ no nosi tɩyaŋ dɩ ʋ kɩ deŋ.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: Ka dɩ ʋ yaŋ piili kɩ dɔgɩsɩ ʋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ-dɔŋtɩŋsɩ hʋ aŋ kɩ di kɩdiiliye hʋ yʋga yʋga, aŋ gɔllɩ tɩŋ sɩnyʋwala hal a nyʋwa sɩŋ bugo kɩ baŋ,

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: wɩya no tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ sɩ we kɩ yaa dɩ ʋ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ mɩɩgɩ ko pirigi ʋ tɩyaŋ dɩ ʋ bɩ yɩyɛla.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩ dɔgɩsɩ ʋ weliŋ aŋ leŋ dɩ ʋ mɛ na tʋwara hʋ nala aa fugo baa ba aa tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa naa. Haŋ tʋwara hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba sɩ we a kɩ wii aŋ kɩ kyaŋ ba nyɩŋa.”

english_verse: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
translation_verse: Bambugo hʋ gyɩ bɩ lɔgɔ nʋʋ kolo, dɩ ba fɩŋtɩŋsɩ hʋ rɛ ko dɩsɩ dɩ ba fa kpa we.

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: Ka wɩgyʋnna hʋ kɛ gyɩ lɔgɔ nʋʋ we bumbuŋiye tɩyaŋ a kolo.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: Ʋ tekere vɩʋ̃ʋ̃, nal kɩdɩgɩ heeli a baa, “Hafalɩɩ bala hʋ rɛ aa ko! Ma ko lɩɩ mʋ kyeme ʋ.”

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ hatoliye hʋ buloŋ sii a kaŋ ba fɩŋtɩŋsɩ ŋmasɩ turo ta ba gyɩŋ.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ bambugo hʋ basɩ tɩya wɩgyʋnna hʋ a baa, “Á fɩŋtɩŋsɩ hʋ aa kyɛ dɩ ba dɩsɩ rɛ, ma tɩya ma nʋʋ hʋ mʋhʋ mʋhʋ.”

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ wɩgyʋnna hʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Aayɩ, nʋʋ hʋ bɩ sɩ pele á beel-ma buloŋ. Ma mʋ kyɛ yɔbɔ ma tɩɩ nʋʋ.”

english_verse: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ŋ bɩ gyɩŋ ma.”

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Yesu aa maga namaga no ko teŋ ʋ baa, “Ma yaŋ gyɩŋ tɩya ma tɩɩ weliŋ beewɩya ma bɩ gyɩŋ kyɛɛ hʋ koo saŋa hʋ mɩyaŋ nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko.” Yesu maga tʋŋtʋnna boto namaga Luka

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ pɩɩsa rɛ weliŋ ka ba nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ gyɩ na ko a yɩrɩ ba dɩ ʋ na ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ aa kpaa ʋ molbiye yaa gɛɛ.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: ʋ fa maga dɩ ɩ kpa ŋ molbiye hʋ mʋ biŋ lee hʋ tɩyaŋ ba aa biŋ molbiye rɛ. Ŋ fa aa mɩɩgɩ ko nyɛ, ŋ fa sɩ laa molbiye hʋ abee ŋ tɔnɔ buloŋ.

english_verse: As soon as the man had gone, the servant with the
translation_verse: Tʋŋtʋnnɩ hʋ aa laa molbiye tusi bɔnɔŋ hʋ kaŋ mʋ a tʋŋ tʋma a bɩl na tusi bʋnɔŋ tɔnɔ pɛ.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ ʋ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Gyaaŋbiye, ɩ yaa tʋŋtʋnnɩtɩɩ rɛ. Ɩ kɔnɩ daga nɩɩ ɩ yaa wɩtɩɩ tɩɩna rɛ. Ɛɛwɩya ɩ aa wuwo deŋ kɩbiye nyuu weliŋ nyɛ ŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ɩ kɩ deŋ kɩgyamaa. Ŋ teŋ fɩyɛla rɛ weliŋ abee wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa yaa. Ɛɛwɩya ŋ aa kyɛ dɩ ɩ mɛ teŋ fɩyɛlɩ rɛ a beel ŋ.’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ mɛ a baa, ‘Gyaaŋbiye, ɩ yaa tʋŋtʋnnɩtɩɩ rɛ. Ɩ kɔnɩ daga nɩɩ ɩ yaa wɩtɩɩ tɩɩna rɛ. Ɩ aa wuwo deŋ kɩbiye nyuu weliŋ nyɛ ŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ɩ kɩ deŋ kɩgyamaa. Ŋ teŋ fɩyɛla rɛ weliŋ abee wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa yaa. Ɛɛwɩya ŋ aa kyɛ dɩ ɩ teŋ mɛ fɩyɛlɩ rɛ a beel-ŋ.’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ kambɩŋ kana ŋ, ŋ hul bʋwa a kpa molbiye hʋ a hogo. Na ɩ molbiye. Laa ɩ kɩna.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Ɩ yaa tʋŋtʋnnɩbɔŋ nɛ a kaŋ teŋwɩɩ mɛ. Ɩ aa gyɩma anɩɩ ŋ aa kɩ kʋŋ kɩna ŋ aa bɩ doho,

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: Ma kaŋ molbiye hʋ laa ʋ lee, a kpa tɩya nal hʋ aa kaŋ molbiye tusi fi hʋ.

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Nal hʋ aa kaŋ kɩna yʋga, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ marɩ tɩya ʋ pɛ, amɛ nal hʋ aa bɩ kaŋ kɩna yʋga, mʋhʋ hʋ mɛ ʋ aa kana, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kaŋ laa ʋ lee.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Ma kaŋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ tɔɔ no lɩɩ yuwo ta dɩ ʋ we bilhuu tɩyaŋ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ nala hʋ aa vɩya Wɩɩsɩ sɩ we a kɩ wii aŋ kɩ kyaŋ ba nyɩŋa.”’ Dʋnɩya buloŋ sarɩya kyɛdiilii

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: Saŋa hʋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko abee gaŋdarɩ, ŋ bee malɩkasɩ buloŋ nɛ sɩ ko. Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ sɩ hɔŋ ŋ gaŋdaa-kuworikpasa tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: Aŋka dɩ dʋnɩya nala buloŋ ko laŋŋɩ ŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ. Ŋ sɩ kaŋ ba porigi leye balɩya anɩɩ pedaal aa kaŋ ʋ piyese porigi bʋʋna tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: Ŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩmma hʋ sɩŋ ŋ noduu, ka dɩ nɩbɔmɔ hʋ mɛ sɩŋ ŋ nogɔbɔ.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ sɩ basɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa sɩŋ ŋ noduu a baa, “Ma ko, ma nala hʋ ŋ Kuwo Wɩɩsɩ aa yaa wɩwelii tɩya. Ma ko di wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ ʋ aa mara biŋ ma a lɩɩ dʋnɩya piili tɩyaŋ buloŋ.

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: Beewɩya, losi gyɩ aa kana ŋ nɛ, ma yaa kɩdiiliye tɩya ŋ, nɩɩnyʋwasɩ gyɩ kana ŋ ma yaa nɩɩ tɩya ŋ, ŋ nyʋwa. Ŋ gyɩ yaa nɩhʋwal lɛ, ma kana ŋ weliŋ.

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: Ŋ gyɩ kaŋ teŋbil lɛ, ma kpa gal laalɩ ŋ. Ŋ gyɩ aa wɩɩlɩ lɛ, ma deŋ ŋ lee weliŋ. Ba gyɩ kana ŋ tɔ dɩyaŋ nɛ, ma ko na ŋ.”

english_verse: Then the ones who pleased the Lord will ask, “When did we give you something to eat or drink?
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩmma hʋ sɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Á Tɩɩna, kyɛbee rɛ á naa ɩ dɩ losi kana ɩ, á tɩya ɩ kɩdiiliye? Kyɛbee rɛ nɩɩnyʋwasɩ kana ɩ, á yaa nɩɩ tɩya ɩ?

english_verse: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
translation_verse: Kyɛbee rɛ á naa ɩ dɩ ɩ yaa nɩhʋwal, á kana ɩ weliŋ, koo kyɛbee rɛ á naa ɩ dɩ ɩ kaŋ teŋbil á kpa gal laalɩ ɩ?

english_verse: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Kyɛbee rɛ á naa ɩ dɩ ɩ aa wɩɩlɩ, koo dɩ ba kana ɩ tɔ dɩyaŋ, á ko na ɩ?”

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ ŋ sɩ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, saŋa hʋ buloŋ ma aa yaa wɩwelii a tɩya ŋ naabalɩya no tɩyaŋ nɩbiye, mɩyaŋ nɛ ma yaa tɩya gɛɛ.”

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ sɩ basɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa sɩŋ ŋ nogɔbɔ a baa, “Ma fasɩ lɩɩ ŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ! Wɩɩsɩ vɩya ma wɩya rɛ. Ma mʋ gyʋʋ diŋ hʋ aa bɩ sɩ maakyiye dɩsɩ, diŋ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa wee biŋ Sɩtaanɩ bee ʋ noŋ nala.

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Beewɩya saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ losi gyɩ aa kana ŋ, ma bɩ yaa kɩdiiliye tɩya ŋ, nɩɩnyʋwasɩ gyɩ kana ŋ nɛ ma bɩ yaa nɩɩ tɩya ŋ.

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: Ŋ gyɩ yaa nɩhʋwal lɛ ma bɩ kana ŋ weliŋ. Ŋ gyɩ kaŋ teŋbil lɛ, ma bɩ kpaa gal laalɩ ŋ. Ŋ gyɩ aa wɩɩlɩ rɛ ma bɩ deŋ ŋ, ba gyɩ kana ŋ tɔ dɩyaŋ mɛ, ma bɩ ko na ŋ.”

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ ba sɩ pɩyɛsɩ ŋ a baa, “Á Tɩɩna, kyɛbee rɛ losi kana ɩ, koo nɩɩnyʋwasɩ kana ɩ, koo ɩ gyɩ yaa nɩhʋwal koo, ɩ gyɩ kaŋ teŋbil koo, ɩ gyɩ kɩ wɩɩlɩ koo, ba kana ɩ tɔ dɩyaŋ aŋ ka á bɩ kyiyeli ɩ?”

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: Ŋ yaŋ sɩ baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, saŋa hʋ buloŋ ma aa vɩya dɩ ma kyiyeli nala no tɩyaŋ nɩbiye, mɩyaŋ nɛ ma vɩya kyiyelii gɛɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ sɩ leŋ dɩ nala no mʋ gyʋʋ lee hʋ ba aa sɩ na tʋwara a kaŋ kɩ mʋ deŋdeŋ, aŋka dɩ tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩmma hʋ mɛ kaŋ mɩɩbol hʋ aa bɩ tenii.”

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Ma tɩɩ gyɩma anɩɩ kyɛyɛ balɩya rɛ kaa ka dɩ Dɩɩsɩ-parɩ-banɩɩ Kyɛbal hʋ pele. Saŋa no tɩyaŋ nɛ ba sɩ kaŋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye a kpa ŋ tɩya ŋ dɔmɔ dɩ ba kpaasɩ ŋ mal daagarɩɩ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ bɩl mɩɩgɩ ko ʋ hatɩnna hʋ lee a pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Ma ha pɩŋ kɩ pɩŋ doo rɛ? Ma deŋ na, saŋa hʋ ba aa sɩ kaŋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye a mʋ tɩya wɩbɔŋyaala pele rɛ.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba vʋʋlɩ gɛɛ hʋ ba aa sɩ yaa a lʋwɔlɩ kaŋ Yesu kpʋ.

english_verse: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
translation_verse: Ba baa dɩ ʋ bɩ maga ba kana ʋ kyɛbal hʋ kyɛɛ, beewɩya dɩ ba aa rɛ yaa gɛɛ, gyamaa hʋ sɩ laa taawɛɛ. Ba kpaa tʋranɩɩ tɩsɩ Yesu Bɛɛtani tɩyaŋ Maakɩ

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
translation_verse: Deŋ naa, dɩ ba aa rɛ fa kpaa tʋranɩɩ no yallɩ na ba aa na mogyamaa rɛ dɩ ba fa kyiyeli nyaaba.”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aa naa gɛɛ, ba baaŋ sii. Ba gʋrɩmɩ wɩya kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ haaŋ no kaŋ tʋranɩɩ no kyogi ta nyɛ?

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba yɩɩ wɩɩkyʋwalyɩɩl aŋ sii lɩɩ a mʋ gyɩŋ Olivi dogimo hʋ. Yesu baa dɩ Piita sɩ tʋwa baa ʋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ Maakɩ

english_verse: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
translation_verse: A lɩɩ haŋ saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Gyudasɩ piili kɩ kyɛ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa sɩ tɩŋa a kpa Yesu we ba nosi tɩyaŋ. Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ dii Dɩɩsɩ-parɩ-banɩɩ Kyɛbal hʋ kɩdiiliye Maakɩ

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
translation_verse: Ba aa di kɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ma kɩdɩgɩ rɛ sɩ kpa ŋ yallɩ.”

english_verse: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
translation_verse: Dɩɩsɩ-Parɩ-banɩɩ kyɛbal hʋ tɩyaŋ ba aa kyaŋ paanʋʋ hʋ ba aa bɩ wee sɩbʋl hʋ, ʋ kyɛbuŋbuŋ kyɛɛ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ ko pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Lee rɛ ɩ kɩ kyɛ dɩ á mʋ yaa Dɩɩsɩ-parɩ-banɩɩ Kyɛbal hʋ kɩdiiliye dɩ ɩ ko di?”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Ba aa hɔnɔ kɩ di kɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu kpa paanʋʋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ aŋ kaŋ lɔgɔ lɔgɔ a kpa tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aŋ baa, “Ma laa kyaŋ. Ŋ teŋbii rɛ nyɛ.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: beewɩya ŋ kyal lɛ gɛɛ. Kyal no rɛ Wɩɩsɩ kpa we nyʋwa hʋ tɩya ma. Ŋ kyal sɩ gyaasɩ nɩgyamaa wɩya, dɩ Wɩɩsɩ kpa ba wɩbɔmɔ kyɛ ba.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, ŋ bɩl bɩ sɩ maakyiye nyʋwa sɩŋ no, see kyɛɛ hʋ ŋ beel-ma aa sɩ nyʋwa sɩfalɩɩ ŋ Kuwo koro hʋ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Tebine no tɩɩ ma buloŋ sɩ fá pɩsa aŋ vɩya ŋ ta akuu wɩɩ hʋ aa sɩ puu ŋ wɩya. Beewɩya ba sabɩ Wɩɩsɩ teŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ a baa, Wɩɩsɩ baa, ‘Ŋ sɩ kpʋ pedaal hʋ aŋ ka dɩ piyese hʋ fá pɩsa.”’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ɩ, gyɩnaŋ tebine no tɩɩ ɩ Piita sɩ tʋwa naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋto a baa ɩ bɩ gyɩma ŋ, ka dɩ gyibal na wii.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ sii mʋ gyʋʋ lee kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Gesamani. Doŋ nɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma hɔŋ daha ka dɩ ŋ yɩya mʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋ ko.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ŋ tɩya buloŋ nɛ kyogo ŋ bembii kɩ kyɛ dɩ ʋ bori. Ma hɔŋ daha kɩ gyegili ŋ?”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Haalɩ dɩ ŋ beel-ɩ sɩ paalɩ sʋba mɛ, ka ŋ bɩ sɩ maakyiye baa ŋ bɩ gyɩma ɩ.” Nɩkaalɩya hʋ buloŋ mɛ basɩ gɛɛ tɩɩ. Yesu kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ Gɛsamani tɩyaŋ Maakɩ

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Ʋ bɩl mʋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ lɩya tɩɩna a bɩl baa, “Ŋ kuwo, dɩ ŋmanɩɩ buloŋ tuwo a maga dɩ tʋwara no parɩ ŋ baŋ, leŋ dɩ ɩ kyɛrɩ yaa.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Ba hɔŋ ka ʋ fasɩ mʋ sɩya mʋhʋ a tuu pɩŋ kyige taŋha a sʋla Wɩɩsɩ a baa “Ŋ kuwo, dɩ ŋmanɩɩ we doŋ maga dɩ tʋwara no parɩ ŋ baŋ, leŋ dɩ ɛɛ yaa. Amɛ ŋ tɩɩ kyɛrɩ tɩyaŋ daa, ɩ kyɛrɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Ma sii dɩ á mʋ. Na nal hʋ aa kpaa ŋ yallɩ rɛ aa ko.” Ba kaŋ Yesu Maakɩ

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter and the two brothers, James and John. He was very sad and troubled,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka ʋ kaŋ Piita abee Zebedi biisi hʋ balɩya ba mʋ. Ʋ tɩya gyɩ kyogo rɛ kɩŋkaŋ, ka ʋ hakɩla buloŋ gyarɩ.

english_verse: He came back and found his disciples sleeping. So he said to Peter, “Can't any of you stay awake with me for just one hour?
translation_verse: Ʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ hʋ teŋ aŋ sii ko ʋ hatɩnna hʋ boto lee dɩ ba pɩŋ doo. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ pɩyɛsɩ Piita a baa, “Ma aa pɩŋ doo rɛ? Ɛɛ kɛ ma bɩ sɩ wuwo bara doo a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ hawadɩgɩ mɛ aŋ kɩ gyegili ŋ?

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Ma kɩ bara doo a kɩ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ aŋ kɩ pɔ dɩ Sɩtaanɩ ta wuwo mʋrɩ ma. Ma tɩɩsɩ tɩyaŋ ma kyo dɩ ma tɩŋa ŋ nyʋwa rɛ, amɛ ma tɩɩ bɩ kaŋ ɛɛ dee.”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu leŋ ba aŋ bɩl mʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ bʋto tɩɩna, a bɩl kyʋwalɩ gɛɛ tɩɩ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋ bagyʋwa, kɩ yaa wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa ko dɩ ɩ yaa.” Nyɛ rɛ ba mʋ pele Yesu a gurigi ʋ.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: Yesu ha gyɩ aa basɩ wɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ dɩ ʋ hatɩnna fi abee balɩya hʋ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Gyudasɩ rɛ mʋ kɩ ko gɛɛ, nɩgyamaa paa tɩŋa ʋ hal, badɔmɔŋ kolo takoobiye abee daŋgulo. Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee ba bee nɩhɩyasɩ rɛ gyɩ tɩŋ ba.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
translation_verse: Minaafigi tɩɩna hʋ gyɩ tɩya nala hʋ magɩl lɛ a baa, “Nal hʋ ŋ aa sɩ mʋ guwori, ʋ rɛ gɛɛ. Ma kana ʋ.”

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyudasɩ vala degini Yesu a mʋ pele ʋ aŋ baa, “Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ, ŋ aa kyʋwalɩ ɩ, aŋ guwori ʋ.”

english_verse: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ lɩɩ ʋ takoobii a ŋmaŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ dɩgɩŋ kyɔl. Baal no fa yaa Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ dɩya tʋŋtʋnnɩ rɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Mɩɩgɩ kpa ɩ takoobii hʋ we ʋ tʋʋl tɩyaŋ. Nal hʋ buloŋ aa kyuri takoobii kyɔgɔ nal, takoobii rɛ ba sɩ kpa kpʋ ʋ tɩɩna mɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Ka Yesu tallɩ ʋ nyʋwa. Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋmɩyɛsɩ Wɩɩsɩ hʋ aa weye feŋ tɩyaŋ, a basɩ tɩya ma, ɩ rɛ yaa Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya? Ɩ rɛ yaa Wɩɩsɩ Biye hʋ?”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Piita aa basɩ gɛɛ, ʋ sii lɩɩ sɩŋ gyaŋwuwo hʋ paga nyʋwa tɩyaŋ. Doŋ nɛ tʋŋtʋnnɩ-toluu kɩdɩgɩ mɛ bɩl na ʋ a basɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa sɩŋ kpaga doŋ a baa, “Baal no mɛ yaa Yesu aa lɩɩ Nazarɛti hatɩnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ rɛ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala hʋ aa kaŋ Yesu kana ʋ mʋ wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ Kayifasɩ dɩya. Dɩ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee ba bee nɩhɩyasɩ buloŋ laŋŋa doŋ.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
translation_verse: Ka Piita mɛ daasɩ kɩ tɩŋ ba hal a tɩŋa gyʋʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ gyaŋwuwo. Ʋ aa gyʋʋ gyaŋwuwo hʋ, ʋ mʋʋ hɔŋ nala hʋ aa pɔ gyaŋwuwo hʋ lee dɩ ʋ na wɩɩ hʋ ba aa sɩ yaa Yesu.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee nala hʋ buloŋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya, kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba nyɩya wɩya a tɩya Yesu dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ.

english_verse: But they could not find any, even though many did come and tell lies. At last, two men came forward
translation_verse: Amɛ ba gyɩ bɩ naa wɩɩ buloŋ ba aa sɩ nyɩya tɩya ʋ. Haalɩ nala yʋga gyɩ ko nyɩya wɩya tɩya ʋ, amɛ ba bɩ naa wɩɩ buloŋ ʋ aa yaa kyogi a maga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ.

english_verse: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ baala balɩya ko lɩɩ a baa, “Baal no baa dɩ ʋ sɩ wuwo ŋmaa Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ lo aŋ mɩɩgɩ saa ʋ kyɛyɛ boto paga tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ɛɛ rɛ ɩ basɩ. Amɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma buloŋ nɛ, a lɩɩ saŋa no tɩyaŋ a kaŋ kɩ mʋ, ma sɩ na mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye hʋ aa hɔŋ Wɩɩsɩ noduu logiŋ tɩyaŋ dɩ ŋ ko kɩ tuu taala tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba tʋ naŋtʋwɔl we ʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ aŋ ŋmaa ʋ. Ba badɔmɔŋ aa ŋmaa ʋ rɛ, aŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa,

english_verse: and said, “You think you are the Messiah! So tell us who hit you!” Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: “Ɩ Krisita, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya, na ɩ yaa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal lɛ, daga nal hʋ aa ŋmaa ɩ.” Piita tʋwa anɩɩ ʋ bɩ gyɩŋ Yesu Maakɩ

english_verse: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Piita bɩl tʋwa a baa, “Ŋ ŋmɩyɛsa, ŋ bɩ gyɩŋ baal no.”

english_verse: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ Piita mɛ gyɩ hɔŋ gyaŋwuwo hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ gyaŋwuwo hʋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ-toluu kɩdɩgɩ ko lɩɩ a baa, “Na ɩ mɛ fa aa tɩŋ Yesu aa lɩɩ Galili hal lɛ.”

english_verse: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ Piita tʋwa ba buloŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ a baa, “Ŋ kɛ bɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ no ɩ aa basɩ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Mʋhʋ sʋwa nala hʋ aa sɩŋ doŋ mɛ sii kpa mʋ Piita lee a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ, ɩ mɛ yaa ba kɩdɩgɩ rɛ, beewɩya ɩ wɩbasɩɩ daga gɛɛ rɛ.”

english_verse: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Piita liisi wɩɩ hʋ Yesu gyɩ aa basɩ tɩya ʋ anɩɩ ʋ sɩ tʋwa naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋto a baa dɩ ʋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ, ka dɩ gyibal na wii. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sii lɩɩ a wii weliŋ.

english_verse: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Pilato gyɩ gyɩma rɛ anɩɩ Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ tɩsɩ gyɩ aa hɔllɩ Yesu wɩyaalɩya rɛ, ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ba kana ʋ ko ʋ lee.

english_verse: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
translation_verse: Ba aa kana ʋ lɩɩ kɩ mʋ dɩ ba kpaasɩ mal daagarɩɩ hʋ nyuu gɛɛ rɛ, ba kyeŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Siimɔŋ aa lɩɩ Sariini. Ɛɛ rɛ laalyuwolo hʋ fɩla ʋ, ʋ laa Yesu daagarɩɩ hʋ a baasɩ.

english_verse: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.

english_verse: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa mɩɩgɩ lɩɩ nɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ ko dɩ ʋ lɩɩ, a wil dɩ ʋ deŋ, dɩ ʋ naa wɩɩsɩnyuu pirigi lɔgɔ suri. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ aa kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ kokomo ko tuu saga ʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ gbege kɩdɩgɩ mɛ sii ko Yesu lee a tuu gbinni a sʋla ʋ a baa, “Dɩ ɩ rɛ aa kyɛ, ɩ sɩ wuwo leŋ dɩ ŋ deye.”

english_verse: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
translation_verse: a baa, “Saŋa hʋ pele rɛ. Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ pele rɛ. Ma bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ ma wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ aŋ laa Wɩɩsɩ wɩweliye hʋ di.”

english_verse: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Ʋ aa ko na ba, ʋ yɩrɩ ba, ba leŋ ba kuwo hʋ abee nala hʋ buloŋ fa aa tʋma kɩ tɩya ba aŋ lɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ, a mʋ kɩ tɩŋa ʋ hal. Yesu kile gyɩŋbɔŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ Luka

english_verse: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ ko gyʋʋ a kaŋ haaŋ hʋ noŋ tɩyaŋ a kaŋ tarɩ ʋ sii sɩŋ. Ʋ teŋnɩma hʋ pirigi nyʋgɩsɩ, ka ʋ sii yaa kɩdiiliye tɩya ba.

english_verse: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
translation_verse: Ba aa ko na ʋ, ba basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Nala buloŋ nɛ ko kɩ kyɛ ɩ.”

english_verse: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ dɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ gyɩŋbɔŋ aa kana rɛ gɛɛ, a ko gyʋʋ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya hʋ a kɩ kyɩɩrɩ a baa,

english_verse: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: “Yesu aa lɩɩ Nazarɛti, bee rɛ ɩ kɩ kyɛ ɩ yaa ma? Ɩ ko dɩ ɩ kpʋ ma rɛ? Ŋ gyɩma ɩ rɛ. Ɩ rɛ yaa Wɩɩsɩ Tɩpʋlʋŋ Tɩɩna hʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
translation_verse: Yesu aa nɩɩ wɩya no baal hʋ aa basa, ʋ nɔsɩ gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ a baa, “Leŋ kyagɩŋsɩ aŋ ko lɩɩ baal hʋ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
translation_verse: Gyɩŋbɔŋ hʋ kaŋ baal hʋ kɩ kyele aŋ kyɩɩrɩ yʋga aŋ lɩɩ baal hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ mɩɩgɩ hirigi a kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ a baa, “Bee wɩɩ rɛ nyɛ? Wɩfalɩɩ rɛ ʋ kɩ daga. Ʋ paalɩ kaŋ gyɩŋbɔmɔ dee rɛ, a basɩ wɩya tɩya ba, ba kɩ tɩŋa ʋ nyʋwa.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma leŋ dɩ á mʋ tɔnɩ hʋ mɛ aa kpaga daha, dɩ ŋ basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya doŋma mɛ, beewɩya ɛɛ wɩya rɛ ŋ ko.”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ, sikii kaŋ Yesu. Ʋ lɩɩ ʋ noŋ a dige ʋ aŋ baa, “Ŋ aa kyɛ rɛ, deye!”

english_verse: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii lɩɩ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ haŋ lagɩ lagɩ hʋ, ʋ bee Gyemsi abee Gyɔɔŋ, a kpa mʋ Siimɔŋ abee Aŋduru dɩya.

english_verse: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Ba aa mʋwa dɩ Siimɔŋ hɩɩlhaaŋ pɩna kɩ wɩɩlɩ, ʋ teŋ buloŋ nɩma. Yesu aa mʋʋ ko, ba basɩ tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩdʋsɩya saŋa, ba kaŋ nala hʋ buloŋ aa wɩɩlɩ abee gyɩŋsɩ aa kaŋ nala hʋ buloŋ a ko Yesu lee.

english_verse: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
translation_verse: Bee hʋ tɩmma buloŋ giri a ko laŋŋɩ Siimɔŋ dɩya dimbee nyʋwa tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
translation_verse: Yesu gyɩ tɩɩbɩ nala hʋ yʋga aa wɩɩlɩ, a kile gyɩŋbɔmɔ mɛ yʋga ta nala tɩyaŋ. Gyɩŋsɩ hʋ gyɩ gyɩŋ nal hʋ Yesu aa yaa rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ʋ gyɩ bɩ leŋ ba basɩ wɩɩ buloŋ. Yesu basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya Galili paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ Luka

english_verse: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩgballɩnyʋwa, Yesu sii lɩɩ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ a mʋ lee hʋ nala aa tuwo dɩ ʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: Simon and the others started looking for him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka Siimɔŋ bee ʋ kyaŋsɩ hʋ mɛ sii ko a kɩ kyɛ ʋ.

english_verse: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Ʋ yaŋ sii a mʋ gbɛ Galili paalʋʋ buloŋ, a gyʋʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩsɩ a kɩ basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya aŋ pɛ kile gyɩŋsɩ mɛ nala tɩyaŋ a ta. Yesu tɩɩbɩ gbege Matiwu

english_verse: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ gbegisi hʋ pirigi leŋ baal hʋ, ʋ deye.

english_verse: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ abee sɩfɩyaŋ a baa,

english_verse: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
translation_verse: “Kɩ mʋ aŋ ta basɩ wɩɩ no tɩya nal buloŋ. Amɛ kaŋ ɩ tɩɩ mʋ daga wɩɩkywal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ, dɩ ʋ lɩɩ wɩɩsʋlɩɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ, a pɩɩsɩ ɩ bisiŋ ta anɩɩ Moosi fa aa daga ma gɛɛ. Dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, nal buloŋ sɩ na anɩɩ ɩ bɩl bɩ kaŋ bisiŋ.”

english_verse: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
translation_verse: Aŋka baal hʋ vɩya aŋ kaŋ wɩɩ hʋ lɩɩ basɩ a taalɩ lee buloŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ Yesu bɩl bɩ wuwo dɩ ʋ gyʋʋ tɔɔ buloŋ tɩyaŋ. Amɛ ʋ aa hɔŋ beye samanɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ka nala lɩɩ lee kɛ buloŋ a kɩ ko ʋ lee.

english_verse: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala yʋga giri ko su lee hʋ buloŋ, a tɔ dimbeenyʋwa hʋ, ka ʋ basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya kɩ tɩya ba.

english_verse: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ dɩ nala badɔmɔŋ mɛ rɛ gɛɛ. Ba banaa kpa gbarɩgɩ kɩdɩgɩ aa pɩŋ ʋ wasa tɩyaŋ a kaŋ ko.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
translation_verse: Nala aa tɔɔ lee hʋ buloŋ gɛɛ, ba bɩ gyɩŋ lee hʋ ba aa sɩ tɩŋa a gyʋʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba gyɩŋ dɩya hʋ nyuu, a luri lee, a kpa gbarɩgɩ hʋ abee ʋ wasa buloŋ a tɩŋ doŋ ta, ʋ tuu pɩŋ Yesu sɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: “Bee rɛ tɩŋ baal no kɩ basɩ gɛɛ? Bee rɛ tɩŋ ʋ kɩ tʋʋsɩ Wɩɩsɩ nyɛ? Aŋnɛ rɛ sɩ wuwo kpa nal wɩbɔmɔ kyɛ ʋ, dɩ Wɩɩsɩ dʋŋ daa?”

english_verse: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
translation_verse: Ŋ sɩ daga ma anɩɩ mɩyaŋ, Nihuwobiŋ biye, kaŋ dee rɛ dʋnɩya tɩyaŋ dɩ ŋ kpa nala wɩbɔmɔ kyɛ ba.”

english_verse: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya gbarɩgɩ hʋ a baa, “Sii kpa ɩ wasa a kɩ mʋ dɩya.”

english_verse: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii doŋ a mɩɩgɩ tuu fuwo hʋ nyʋwa. Nɩgyamaa ko gol-ʋ, ʋ kɩ daga ba.

english_verse: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ lɩɩ Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ nihuwobisi wɩya rɛ, amɛ Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ wɩya daa ʋ marɩ nihuwobiŋ.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu mʋ gyʋʋ Levi dɩya, ʋ bee ʋ hatɩnna buloŋ, a hɔŋ kɩ di kɩna. Lampolaala badɔmɔŋ abee wɩbɔŋyaala mɛ ko, ba buloŋ laŋŋɩ kɩ di kɩna hʋ. Lampolaala abee wɩbɔŋyaala fa yʋga rɛ weliŋ a kɩ tɩŋa Yesu hal.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla badɔmɔŋ fa we doŋ a yaa Farasiima. Ɛɛ rɛ ba naa ba, a ko pɩyɛsɩ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ Yesu bee wɩbɔŋyaala abee lampolaala buloŋ laŋŋɩ hɔŋ kɩ di kɩna?”

english_verse: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Saŋa kɩdɩgɩ, Gyɔɔŋ Wɩɩsɩnɩɩfool hʋ hatɩnna abee Farasiima gyɩ aa vʋwa nyʋwa rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ nala badɔmɔŋ sii ko pɩyɛsɩ Yesu a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ Gyɔɔŋ hatɩnna abee Farasiima kɩ vʋwa nyʋwa, aŋka ɩ hatɩnna kɛ bee vʋwa nyʋwa?”

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Amɛ saŋa kɩdɩgɩ rɛ sɩ ko pele dɩ ba kaŋ baal hʋ mʋ lee kɩdɩgɩ. Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ kyaŋsɩ hʋ yaŋ sɩ vʋwa nyʋwa.”

english_verse: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Nal buloŋ bɩ sɩ kpa taŋfalɩɩ a ala tɔ gabɩnɩɩ bol? Dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, taŋfalɩɩ hʋ sɩ kʋʋrɩ, dɩ gabɩnɩɩ hʋ marɩ kɩyasɩ pɛ.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
translation_verse: Dɩ ɩ rɛ kaŋ pulbɩnɩɩ, ɩ sɩ yaa sɩhuwo a we dɩ ʋ wii? Aayɩ, dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, pulbɩnɩɩ hʋ sɩ pɔsɔ dɩ sɩŋ hʋ buloŋ ta. Ba aa kpa sɩhuwo we pulfalɩɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ.” Ba basɩ Kyɛwiyesii hʋ Kyɛɛ wɩya Matiwu

english_verse: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
translation_verse: Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ, Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ sii a mʋ tɩŋ nyoŋso baga kɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ a kɩ mʋ. Ʋ hatɩnna hʋ bori nyoŋso hʋ a piisi kɩ kyaŋ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima badɔmɔŋ pɩyɛsɩ Yesu a baa, “Ɩ bɩ naa? Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ sɩna daa ɩ hatɩnna hʋ kɩ kyogi gɛɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu mɩɩgɩ pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Ma ha bɩ maakyiye karɩmɩ wɩɩ hʋ Deviti gyɩ aa yaa? Losi gyɩ kaŋ ʋ bee ʋ hatɩnna rɛ,

english_verse: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Mɩyaŋ, Nihuwobiŋ biye rɛ yaa Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ tɩɩna.”

english_verse: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
translation_verse: Gyamaa hʋ gyɩ aa ko yʋga gɛɛ, ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ dɩ ba mʋ kpa nɩɩduworiboro ko sɩgɩ nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa dɩ ʋ gyʋʋ, dɩ nala hʋ ta ko yige ʋ lo.

english_verse: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya nosʋʋ hʋ tɩɩna dɩ ʋ sii ko lɩɩ sɩŋ ba sɩya tɩyaŋ. Ʋ sii lɩɩ.

english_verse: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu na baaŋ ba nyuu tɩyaŋ. Ʋ tɩya buloŋ gyɩ kyogo rɛ akuu ba dɩgɩna aa bee nɩɩ wɩya nyuniŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya nosʋʋ hʋ tɩɩna a baa, “Kaŋ ɩ noŋ tannɩ.” Ʋ kaŋ tannɩ, ʋ bɩrɩmɩ noduwo.

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ gyɩ sii tuu fuwobal hʋ nyʋwa. Nɩgyamaa aa lɩɩ Galili paalʋʋ sii giri tɩŋ ba hal.

english_verse: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ fi abee balɩya ʋ gyɩ aa lɩya, ba fene rɛ nyɛ: Siimɔŋ, Yesu fa kana ʋ kɩ yɩrɩ Piita.

english_verse: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii lɩɩ doŋ, a mʋ gyɩŋ dogimo kɩdɩgɩ, a yɩrɩ nala hʋ buloŋ ʋ aa kyɛ, ba mʋ laŋŋɩ ʋ lee.

english_verse: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
translation_verse: Zebedi biisi hʋ balɩya, Gyemsi abee ʋ naabiibaal Gyɔɔŋ. Yesu fa kaŋ ba mɛ kɩ yɩrɩ Buwanagyisi. Feŋ no yaa namaga feŋ nɛ. Ʋ memii rɛ yaa gaduwo-kyagɩŋsɩyaala anɩɩ duwoŋ nɔsɩɩ.

english_verse: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: Ba nɩkaalɩya hʋ mɛ rɛ nyɛ: Aŋduru abee Fɩlɩpɩ abee Batolomi abee Matiwu abee Tɔmaasɩ abee Gyemsi, Alfiyasɩ biibaal hʋ, abee Tadiyo abee Siimɔŋ, nal hʋ aa kyɩl ʋ nala wɩya kɩŋkaŋ,

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla badɔmɔŋ mɛ gyɩ we doŋ. Ba gyɩ lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm nɛ a ko, a mɛ baa dɩ gyɩŋbɔmɔ buloŋ kuwori ba aa yɩrɩ “Bɛlizebul” lɛ kana ʋ. Dɩ ʋ rɛ tɩya ʋ dee hʋ ʋ kɩ kile gyɩŋsɩ nala tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
translation_verse: dɩ nɩgyamaa hɔŋ gol-ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba laa basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ɩ naa abee ɩ naabalɩya rɛ sɩŋ gyaŋhal tɩyaŋ a kɩ kyɛ ɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu yɩrɩ ba, a maga namaga no tɩya ba a baa, “Ɛɛ rɛ Sɩtaanɩ sɩ yaa a kile ʋ tɩɩtɩɩ noŋ nala?

english_verse: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
translation_verse: Yesu aa ko lɩɩ nala no mʋ teŋ, ʋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ dɩya. Ɛɛ rɛ nala bɩl ko gol ʋ bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ, ba paalɩ bɩ naa sɩnɩɩ dɩ ba di kɩdiiliye mɛ gba.

english_verse: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
translation_verse: Dɩ bee nala rɛ we doŋ aŋ bɩ kaŋ nyʋʋdɩgɩ, ba bee hʋ sɩ sɩŋ?

english_verse: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
translation_verse: Dɩ Sɩtaanɩ bee ʋ noŋ nala mɛ yaŋ ko bɩ kaŋ nyʋʋdɩgɩ, ba bee mɛ bɩ sɩ sɩŋ. Ʋ sɩ tele teŋ.

english_verse: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
translation_verse: Nal tuwo ʋ aa sɩ wuwo gyʋʋ gaduwogi dɩya a gaa ʋ kɩna, see ʋ aa laa sɩya kaŋ gaduwogi hʋ vʋwa biŋ, aŋ na wuwo gyʋʋ paa ʋ kɩna.”

english_verse: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya nala hʋ a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, dɩ nal lɛ yaa wɩbɔmɔ mɔɔgɛɛ buloŋ, aŋ pɛ tʋʋsɩ Wɩɩsɩ mɛ gba, ka Wɩɩsɩ ha sɩ wuwo kpa ba buloŋ kyɛ ʋ.

english_verse: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
translation_verse: Amɛ dɩ nal lɛ tʋʋsɩ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ, Wɩɩsɩ bɩ sɩ maakyiye kpa kyɛ ʋ buloŋ. Sʋʋkpʋŋ wɩbɔŋ aa bɩ kaŋ marɩ layɩ rɛ ʋ tɩɩna yaa gɛɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
translation_verse: Nala hʋ badɔmɔŋ aa basɩ baa dɩ gyɩŋbɔŋ nɛ kaŋ ʋ hʋ, ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ʋ basɩ nyɛ. Yesu nɩɩna abee ʋ naabalɩya ko ʋ lee Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
translation_verse: Mʋhʋ ko pɛ dɩ Yesu nɩɩna abee ʋ naabalɩya ko a sɩŋ gyaŋhal tɩyaŋ aŋ tɩŋ nal dɩ ʋ gyʋʋ yɩrɩ ʋ. Tɩŋdaal hʋ aa gyʋʋ

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu mɩɩgɩ pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Aŋnɛ rɛ yaa ŋ naa abee ŋ naabalɩya?”

english_verse: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
translation_verse: Aŋ mɩɩgɩ deŋ nala hʋ aa hɔŋ gol-ʋ, aŋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma na, ŋ naa abee ŋ naabalɩya rɛ nyɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Nal hʋ buloŋ aa yaa wɩya hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa kyo, ʋ rɛ yaa ŋ naabiibaal, abee ŋ naatoluu, abee ŋ naa.”

english_verse: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl mɩɩgɩ mʋ hɔŋ Galili fuwobal hʋ nyʋwa a kɩ daga nala. Nɩgyamaa sii giri ko kyige ʋ. Ʋ aa ko na gyamaa hʋ yʋga gɛɛ, ʋ sii mʋ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro, a hɔŋ nɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, ka nala hʋ sɩŋ fuwo hʋ nyʋwa.

english_verse: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa gyaasɩ kɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, biye hʋ badɔmɔŋ tele nɩɩbʋʋsɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Digibiisi ko tuu tʋwasɩ di.

english_verse: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ kpa namagɩsɩ a daga ba wɩya yʋga. Ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa,

english_verse: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: “Ma gyegili nɩɩ daha! Baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ sii a kaŋ ʋ kɩdoho mʋ dɩ ʋ gyaasɩ.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Badɔmɔŋ mɛ tele bʋnyɩŋ tɩyaŋ. Bʋnyɩŋ hʋ fa bɩ kaŋ taŋha yʋga. Ɛɛ wɩya, kɩna hʋ teŋ pesi nyʋ. Amɛ ba naaliliye bɩ wuwo gyʋʋ taŋha.

english_verse: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ wɩɩsɩ nʋŋ kpʋ ba, beewɩya ba naaliliye bɩ gyʋʋ taŋha.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
translation_verse: Biye hʋ badɔmɔŋ mɛ tele sɔsɩ tɩyaŋ a nyʋ. Amɛ sɔsɩ hʋ sii gɩrɩgɩ ba, ba nʋŋ pʋgɩmɩ kɩna hʋ, ba bɩ wuwo we biye.

english_verse: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Bekiŋ nɛ á sɩ kpa Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ magɩsɩ? Namaga bee rɛ á sɩ kpa daga ʋ memii?

english_verse: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
translation_verse: Saŋa hʋ gyamaa hʋ buloŋ aa mʋwa, aŋka Yesu bee ʋ kpambɩsɩ fi abee balɩya hʋ, abee ʋ hatɩnna badɔmɔŋ dʋŋ, ba pɩyɛsɩ ʋ namagɩsɩ hʋ memii.

english_verse: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: dɩ gɛɛ leŋ, ‘ba aa gyegili, gyegili aŋ bɩ wuwo kɩ gyɩŋ ba memii, a bɩl deŋ deŋ mɛ aŋ bɩ wuwo kɩ na kɩŋ buloŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ fa gyɩŋ ba memii, ba fa sɩ bɩrɩmɩ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ, dɩ ʋ kpa ba wɩbɔmɔ kyɛ ba.”’ Yesu daga kɩdohil namaga hʋ memii Matiwu

english_verse: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
translation_verse: Nala badɔmɔŋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ kɩdoho hʋ aa tele nɩɩbʋʋsɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Nala no aa nɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩweliye hʋ rɛ, ka haŋ lagɩlagɩbiye hʋ, Sɩtaanɩ teŋ ko kaŋ wɩɩ hʋ a lɩɩ ba tɩsɩ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ wɩweliye hʋ rɛ kɩdohil hʋ kaŋ lɩɩ dɩ ʋ basɩ tɩya nala.

english_verse: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Dɩ ɩ rɛ aa kyɛ pʋlʋŋ a kyol fɩŋtɩna ko gyʋʋ dɩya, ɩ sɩ kpa gbaha a kyige tɔ? Koo, ɩ sɩ kpa sɩgɩ ɩ godo memii? Koo, ɩ aa kpa ɩ saga kɩŋ nyuu tɩyaŋ nɛ dɩ ʋ kyaanɩ?

english_verse: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
translation_verse: Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ nal aa siye a mʋ gyaasɩ kɩdoho ʋ baga tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
translation_verse: Ba nyʋwa rɛ ba tɩɩ, a waa, a we biye, a hɩla ba tɩɩ.

english_verse: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba aa ko hɩla ko teŋ, ʋ leŋ ba mʋ kʋma.” Yesu maga kɩdoho kɩdɩgɩ ba a yɩrɩ Masɩtadɩ namaga Matiwu

english_verse: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
translation_verse: Ʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ anɩɩ kɩdoho bii kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ masɩtadɩ. Masɩtadɩ bii rɛ muro te tɩya buloŋ bii.

english_verse: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
translation_verse: Amɛ dɩ ʋ ko nyʋ ʋ aa waa tɩɩbal lɛ a te kɩdohii kɛ buloŋ. Ʋ aa lɩɩ naasɩ yʋga rɛ digibiisi aa ko saga ʋ tɩyaŋ a sʋwa ba gyannɩ doŋ.”

english_verse: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
translation_verse: Ʋ aa bɩ laa nyʋwa basɩ wɩɩ buloŋ polli, see dɩ ʋ kaŋ maga namaga. Amɛ dɩ ʋ bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ dʋŋ nɛ ko hɔŋ, ʋ aa daga namagɩsɩ hʋ memiye rɛ a tɩya ba. Yesu leŋ nɩɩ tɩyaŋ puwoduwo sige Matiwu

english_verse: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
translation_verse: Haŋ kyɛɛ hʋ tɩɩtɩɩ dɩdaana, ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa, “Ma leŋ dɩ a kyol fuwo hʋ kyolo.”

english_verse: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
translation_verse: Yesu maga namagɩsɩ no iriŋ nɛ yʋga a kɩ daga nala Wɩɩsɩ wɩya. Ʋ aa kɩ daga ba a maga gɛɛ hʋ ba aa sɩ wuwo kpa a we ba nyuni tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
translation_verse: Ba ta haŋ nɩgyamaa hʋ buloŋ ba mʋ, aŋ ko gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ ʋ fa aa hɔnɔ hʋ a kpa kɩ mʋ. Nɩɩduworiborisi badɔmɔŋ mɛ fa we doŋ nɛ a kɩ mʋ.

english_verse: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ dɩ dopuwo kɩdɩgɩ piili kɩ yaa, a ko ŋmaa nɩɩ kɩ we nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ kɩ kyɛ dɩ ʋ su.

english_verse: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
translation_verse: Amɛ Yesu fa pɩŋ doo rɛ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ hal tɩyaŋ, a yelli nyuyellii. Ʋ hatɩnna hʋ ko kyisi ʋ a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Á Tɩɩna, ɩ sɩya tuwo koo? Ɩ bɩ gyɩma dɩ á aa kyɛ á sʋba rɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sii doo hʋ tɩyaŋ a nɔsɩ puwo hʋ dɩ ʋ ta kɩ yaa, aŋ basɩ tɩya fuwo hʋ mɛ dɩ ʋ fɩyɛlɩ pɩŋ. Puwo hʋ sige, ka lee hʋ buloŋ yaa siyee.

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu pɩyɛsɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ kambɩŋ kaŋ ma? Ma ha bɩ laa ŋ di?”

english_verse: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ kambɩŋ marɩ kaŋ ba weliŋ, ba kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ a baa, “Nɩgɛɛ doho paalɩ yaa baal no? Puwoni abee fuwoni buloŋ kɩ tɩŋa ʋ nyʋwa.”

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: “Dɩ ŋ yaŋ wuwo dige ʋ gal mɛ, ŋ sɩ deye.” Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ pɩyɛgɩ nala hʋ a mʋ sɩŋ Yesu hal a kaŋ noŋ a dige ʋ gal.

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu mɩɩgɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋ toluu, ɩ laadii rɛ leŋ ɩ deye. Kɩ mʋ abee laaŋfɩya. Wɩɩl hʋ bɩl bɩ sɩ kana ɩ.”

english_verse: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ gɔllɩ yaa luliye kɩŋkaŋ, a na tʋwara weliŋ, a paalɩ kyogi ʋ molbiye buloŋ ta waasʋ, ka wɩɩl hʋ gba marɩ gyʋʋ ʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Ʋ mʋ gyʋʋ a pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ ma kɩ yaa kyagɩŋsɩ no buloŋ? Biye hʋ bɩ sʋba. Ʋ ta kɩ pɩŋ doo rɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ sii kyol Galili fuwobal hʋ kyolo a mʋ pele Gɛrasa tɩmma paalʋʋ a lɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ lɩɩ bilni tɩyaŋ nɛ a kɩ ko dɩ ʋ kyem-ʋ. Bilni hʋ nyuni tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ gyɩ aa gɔllɩ we.

english_verse: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ aa kaŋ kyɔrʋmɔ vʋwa ʋ rɛ, a mʋ lɔlɔ, beewɩya ba aa ko vʋwa ʋ buloŋ, ʋ aa kaŋ tɔrɩ rɛ. Ba aa kana ʋ we kpɩyasɩ mɛ, ʋ ŋmaa kpɩyasɩ hʋ bori. Nal buloŋ tuwo aa sɩ wuwo kana ʋ.

english_verse: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
translation_verse: Wɩɩhaa abee tebine buloŋ, ʋ gyɩ aa we bilni abee dogiŋsi nyuu tɩyaŋ nɛ a kɩ gbɛ, a kɩ kyɩɩrɩ aŋ paa bʋwa kɩ paasɩ ʋ tɩɩ.

english_verse: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa naa Yesu dɩ ʋ we libolii, ʋ fá mʋ kyem-ʋ, a tuu gbinni ʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sʋla Yesu kɩŋkaŋ dɩ ʋ ta leŋ dɩ gyɩŋsɩ hʋ lɩɩ doŋ paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Dɩ porikulaal mɛ sɩŋ doŋ, a kɩ di kɩna dogimo hʋ nyuu.

english_verse: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
translation_verse: Gyɩŋsɩ hʋ sʋla Yesu dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ ba mʋ gyʋʋ porikusi hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ porikudaala hʋ fá mʋ heeli tɩya bagɩdaala abee tɔɔ hʋ nala buloŋ. Ba buloŋ sii ko lɩɩ Yesu lee.

english_verse: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: Ba aa ko dɩ baal hʋ gyɩŋsɩ fa aa kana hʋ, dɩ ʋ hɔnɔ a laalɩ gal a kaŋ laaŋfɩya. Ba aa naa gɛɛ, kambɩŋ gyʋʋ ba.

english_verse: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ fa aa we doŋ a naa wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa baal hʋ abee porikusi hʋ, ba basɩ wɩɩ hʋ tɩya nala hʋ buloŋ.

english_verse: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Ba aa nɩɩ gɛɛ rɛ, ba ko sʋla Yesu dɩ ʋ lɩɩ ba paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
translation_verse: Yesu kaŋ vɩya, aŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ dɩya a basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩya nala wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ á Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ aa yaa ʋ tɩyaŋ, abee ʋ aa fáá ʋ sikii maga gɛɛ.

english_verse: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: Baal hʋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ tɔnɩ fi hʋ aa we doŋ paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a basɩ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ Yesu a yaa tɩya ʋ. Nala nɩɩ, ʋ yaa ba wɩkperii. Gyerusi toluu abee haaŋ hʋ aa dige Yesu gal Matiwu

english_verse: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
translation_verse: Ba buloŋ mɩɩgɩ sɩŋ kɩ mʋma ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ kile ba ta ba lɩɩ, aŋka ʋ kaŋ biye hʋ kuwo, abee ʋ naa, abee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ boto, ba gyʋʋ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ biye hʋ aa pɩna.

english_verse: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu mɩɩgɩ mʋ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ a kyol fuwo hʋ kyolo a mʋ hɔŋ fuwo hʋ nyʋwa. Nala giri ko kyige ʋ.

english_verse: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ dɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ we doŋ, ba kɩ yɩrɩ Gyerusi. Ʋ fa yaa Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya nɩhɩyawʋ rɛ. Ʋ aa naa Yesu, ʋ tuu tele ʋ naasɩ tɩyaŋ,

english_verse: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
translation_verse: a sʋla ʋ a baa, “Ŋ toluu rɛ aa wɩɩlɩ, a pɩŋ kɩ kyɛ dɩ ʋ sʋba. Ŋ aa sʋla ɩ dɩ ɩ ko kpa ɩ nosi dɔbɔ ʋ tɩyaŋ, dɩ ʋ deye.”

english_verse: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
translation_verse: Yesu sii tɩŋa ʋ hal ba kɩ mʋ. Nɩgyamaa mɛ sii kɩ tɩŋa ʋ hal a gol-ʋ kyige.

english_verse: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ nɩɩ Yesu wɩya rɛ, a mɛ ko kɩ tɩŋ nala hʋ tɩyaŋ, aŋ kɩ bɩɩnɩ ʋ tɩya tɩyaŋ a baa,

english_verse: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa dige ʋ gal hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ʋ wɩɩl hʋ pirigi teŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ tɩɩ gyɩma anɩɩ ʋ wɩɩl hʋ tene rɛ.

english_verse: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
translation_verse: Ka Yesu mɛ gyɩma ʋ tɩya tɩyaŋ anɩɩ dee kɩdɩgɩ lɩɩ ʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ʋ bɩrɩmɩ nala hʋ lee a pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Aŋnɛ rɛ dige ŋ gal gɛɛ?”

english_verse: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ hatɩnna baa, “Ɩ bɩ naa nala aa yʋga gɛɛ a gol-ɩ kyige? Ka ɩ bɩl kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɩ aŋnɛ rɛ dige ɩ gal?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
translation_verse: Aŋka Yesu ha vɩya aŋ kɩ deŋ dɩ ʋ na nal hʋ a dige ʋ.

english_verse: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
translation_verse: Amɛ haaŋ hʋ gyɩ aa gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa ʋ tɩyaŋ wɩya, ʋ ko a kɩ kyele, a tuu tele Yesu naasɩ tɩyaŋ aŋ basɩ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Yesu ha aa basɩ wɩya haaŋ hʋ lee gɛɛ, dɩ tɩŋdaala lɩɩ Gyerusi dɩya, a ko basɩ tɩya Gyerusi a baa, “Ɩ toluu hʋ sʋba rɛ, ta bɩl kɩ dɔŋ Tɩɩna hʋ.”

english_verse: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
translation_verse: Yesu aa nɩɩ ba aa basɩ gɛɛ, ka ʋ basɩ tɩya Gyerusi a baa, “Ta leŋ dɩ ɩ bembii kɩ sii, laa ŋ di.”

english_verse: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii, a bɩ leŋ nal buloŋ tɩŋa ʋ hal see Piita, abee Gyemsi, abee ʋ naabiye Gyɔɔŋ.

english_verse: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba ko pele Gyerusi dɩya. Ba aa ko dɩ ba kaŋ kyagɩŋsɩ a kɩ ŋmaa nyʋʋsɩ bagɩbagɩ, a kɩ wii lee buloŋ.

english_verse: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
translation_verse: Biye hʋ nyagɩ sii a kɩ vala, nala hʋ buloŋ nyʋʋsɩ fɩyɛlɩ. Biye hʋ fa yaa bɩsɩ fi abee balɩya rɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba abee sɩfɩyasɩ, dɩ ba ta leŋ dɩ nal buloŋ nɩɩ wɩɩ no, aŋ basɩ tɩya ba dɩ ba kyɛ kɩdiiliye tɩya biye hʋ dɩ ʋ di.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba kyol fuwo hʋ kyolo, a ko pele tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Gyɛnɛsarɛtɩ, a lɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ aŋ kaŋ vʋwa nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal kɛ buloŋ, ʋ aa kaŋ yohuŋ nɛ lee buloŋ tɩyaŋ, amɛ ʋ bee nala abee ʋ dɩya nala tɩɩ kɛ sɩɩ aa bee su ʋ.”

english_verse: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
translation_verse: Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ aa nɩɩ ʋ wɩya, ʋ baa, “Gyɔɔŋ nɛ gɛɛ. Ʋ rɛ yaa nal hʋ ŋ gyɩ aa leŋ ba keri ʋ nyuu hʋ. Ʋ rɛ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.” Wɩɩ hʋ aa tɩŋ Hɛrɔtɩ gyɩ leŋ ba keri Gyɔɔŋ nyuu rɛ nyɛ:

english_verse: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Kyɛwiyesii hʋ kyɛɛ, ʋ sii mʋ gyʋʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya a kɩ daga nala. Ba aa nɩɩ ʋ wɩbasɩɩ hʋ, ba gyeniŋ buloŋ hirige rɛ, a baa “Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yaa a gyɩŋ wɩya no? Aŋnɛ rɛ tɩya ʋ wɩgyʋŋ no?

english_verse: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yaa a wuwo kɩ yaa wɩmagɩla no? Á gyɩma ʋ rɛ! Kapiŋta hʋ daa gɛɛ? Mɛɛrɩ biibaal daa gɛɛ. Gyemsi abee Gyoose abee Gyuda abee Siimɔŋ daa yaa ʋ naabalɩya? Ʋ naatoliye we daha.” Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii bee su ʋ.

english_verse: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “A lɩɩ ma daatɩgɩya hal, ma ta bɩl kpaa kɩŋ buloŋ pɛ a vala ŋmanɩɩ hʋ. Ma ta kaŋ kɩdiiliye mʋ, a ta kpaa diiku mɛ kolo, a ta kpaa molbii mɛ.

english_verse: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
translation_verse: Hɛrɔtɩ gyɩ aa tɩya Gyɔɔŋ gyɩrɩma rɛ, beewɩya ʋ naa dɩ ʋ yaa tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩɩna rɛ a kɩ yaa wɩya hʋ aa kpaa ŋmanɩɩ. Gyɔɔŋ wɩbasɩɩ buloŋ gyɩ aa kperi Hɛrɔtɩ rɛ, ka abee gɛɛ buloŋ, ʋ ha kyo dɩ ʋ gyegili nɩɩ wɩya hʋ ʋ aa basɩ rɛ.

english_verse: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
