translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ piili kɩ gʋrɩmɩ wɩya a baa, “Baal no leŋ wɩbɔŋyaala kɩ ko ʋ lee rɛ, haalɩ ʋ beel-ba laŋŋɩ kɩ di kɩna.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them this story:
translation_verse: Yesu aa nɩɩ gɛɛ, ʋ maga namaga no tɩya ba a baa,

english_verse: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
translation_verse: Dɩ ʋ rɛ ko na ʋ, ʋ aa kpa ʋ rɛ ʋ baasɩ mʋ dɩya abee teŋfɩyɛlʋʋ.

english_verse: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Dɩ ʋ ko pele dɩya, ʋ aa yɩrɩ ʋ kyaŋsɩ abee ʋ dɔŋtɩŋsɩ rɛ, ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, ‘Ma leŋ dɩ á buloŋ tenni fɩyɛlɩ. Beewɩya, ŋ naa ŋ piyesi hʋ aa nyʋgɩsa hʋ rɛ.’

english_verse: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
translation_verse: “Koo ma leŋ á kpa anɩɩ haaŋ kɩdɩgɩ rɛ kaŋ molbinʋʋ kɩna fi, dɩ kɩdɩgɩ nyʋgɩsɩ, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sɩ yaa? Ʋ aa kyol diŋ nɛ, dɩ ʋ tige ʋ dɩya buloŋ, a deŋ weliŋ a ko mʋ na ʋ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.” Two Sons
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, ‘Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, nyɛ tɩɩ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ malɩkasɩ mɛ tenni aa fɩyɛlɩ, dɩ wɩbɔŋyaaldɩgɩ rɛ ko bɩrɩma lɩɩ ʋ wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ.”’ Yesu maga biye hʋ aa nyʋgɩsa namaga

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Yesu bɩl maga namaga kɩdɩgɩ a baa, “Baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ a kaŋ ʋ biibaala balɩya.

english_verse: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
translation_verse: Ʋ bɩ pɩɩsa, ka haŋbiye hʋ tɩɩna sii kpa kɩna hʋ buloŋ ʋ aa laa, a yallɩ, a sii kpa mʋ we paalʋʋ bolii kɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ. Doŋ nɛ ʋ kaŋ molbiye hʋ mʋ, a kpa yaa wɩvɩyagyɩmɩya, ba buloŋ teŋ.

english_verse: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ sii mʋ na tʋma tɔɔ hʋ tɩmma baal kɩdɩgɩ lee. Baal hʋ kana ʋ mʋ dɩ ʋ kɩ deŋ ʋ porikusi ʋ baga tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa deŋ porikusi hʋ mɛ, ka nal buloŋ bee tɩya ʋ kɩdiiliye. Ʋ aa hɔŋ deŋ anɩɩ dɩ ʋ rɛ fa naa porikusi hʋ kɩdiiliye badɔmɔŋ mɛ gba, ʋ aa dii rɛ.

english_verse: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
translation_verse: Ŋ bɩl bɩ maga dɩ ɩ kana ŋ yaa ɩ biye. Kpa ŋ dɩ ŋ yaa ɩ tʋŋtʋnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ á tʋma kɩ tɩya ɩ.’ Ɛɛ rɛ ka ʋ sii kpa kɩ mʋ ʋ kuwo lee.

english_verse: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Ʋ ha gyɩ mʋʋ we libolii rɛ a kɩ ko, ka ʋ kuwo hʋ na ʋ. Sikii buloŋ kana ʋ. Ʋ sii fá mʋ guwori ʋ.

english_verse: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ biye hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ kuwo a baa, ‘Ŋ yaa kyogi Wɩɩsɩ rɛ, a yaa kyogi ɩ mɛ. Ŋ bɩl bɩ maga dɩ ɩ kana ŋ yaa ɩ biye.’

english_verse: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
translation_verse: Amɛ ʋ kuwo basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩya tʋŋtʋnna a baa, ‘Ma mʋ lagɩlagɩ, a paa gaweliye kaŋ ko laalɩ ʋ. Ma kpa nonipiri a we ʋ noŋ aŋ paa naatɔbɔ mɛ a we ʋ.

english_verse: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
translation_verse: Ma lɩɩ kaŋ nɔfara ko gyʋʋ kpʋ, dɩ á buloŋ kyaŋ, dɩ á tenni fɩyɛlɩ.

english_verse: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
translation_verse: Ʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ ŋ biye no fa sʋba rɛ aŋ sii. Ʋ fa nyʋgɩsa rɛ, ka á bɩl na ʋ nyɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba kɩ di kɩna, a kɩ gbɩyalɩ abee teŋfɩyɛlʋʋ.”’

english_verse: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ biye hʋ maana mɛ we baga tɩyaŋ. Ʋ bee koyi saŋa aa ko pele, ʋ sii kɩ ko bee. Ʋ ko kɩ kpaga dɩya, dɩ ʋ nɩɩ ba aa ŋmaa kɩna a kɩ gʋwa.

english_verse: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yɩrɩ ʋ kuwo dɩya tʋŋtʋnna kɩdɩgɩ a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Bekine wɩya rɛ ba kɩ yaa daha?”

english_verse: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ɩ naabiye hʋ rɛ mɩɩgɩ ko. Ɛɛ rɛ ɩ kuwo kaŋ nɔfara kpʋ a tɩya ʋ, beewɩya ʋ mɩɩgɩ ko dɩya abee laaŋfɩya rɛ.”

english_verse: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ biye hʋ maana hʋ na baaŋ kɩŋkaŋ, aŋ vɩya dɩya gyʋʋyɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ kuwo ko lɩɩ, a sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ ko gyʋʋ dɩya.

english_verse: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ kuwo a baa, “Bɩsɩ no buloŋ tɩyaŋ ŋ tʋŋ tʋma rɛ weliŋ a tɩya ɩ anɩɩ yoŋ. Ŋ ha bɩ maakyiye vɩya ɩ nyʋwa kyɛdɩgɩ mɛ. Ba buloŋ tɩyaŋ, bekiŋ nɛ ɩ yaa tɩya ŋ? Ɩ ha bɩ kaŋ bʋʋŋ mɛ gba tɩya ŋ dɩ ŋ bee ŋ kyaŋsɩ mɛ kpʋ kyaŋ.

english_verse: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
translation_verse: Ka ɩ biye no kaŋ ɩ kɩna buloŋ mʋ kyogi ta gyaŋtɩrɩsɩ tɩyaŋ, aŋ mɩɩgɩ ko dɩya, ɩ ha bɩl sii kaŋ nɔfara kpʋ tɩya ʋ.”

english_verse: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ biye hʋ kuwo basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋ biye, ɩ kɛ we dɩya tɩyaŋ nɛ saŋa buloŋ. Kɩŋ buloŋ ŋ aa kana, ɩ rɛ tɩŋa ʋ.

english_verse: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
translation_verse: Amɛ ʋ maga dɩ á leŋ á tenni fɩyɛlɩ rɛ dɩ á gbɩyalɩ, beewɩya ʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ ɩ naabiye no fa sʋba rɛ aŋ sii. Ʋ fa nyʋgɩsa rɛ, ka á bɩl na ʋ nyɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
translation_verse: Yesu gyɩ maga namaga no tɩya ʋ hatɩnna rɛ a baa, “Kɩna tɩɩna kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ, a kaŋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ kɩdɩgɩ, a leŋ ʋ kɩ deŋ ʋ kɩna. Ɛɛ rɛ nala ko basɩ tɩya kɩna tɩɩna hʋ anɩɩ ʋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ hʋ kaŋ ʋ dʋwa buloŋ kyogi kɩ ta rɛ.

english_verse: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
translation_verse: Ʋ aa nɩɩ gɛɛ, ʋ leŋ ba mʋ yɩrɩ ʋ ko, ʋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, ‘Deŋ na ɩ wɩkanɩya hʋ ŋ aa nɩɩ. Ɩ bɩl bɩ maga dɩ ɩ yaa ŋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ-hɩyawʋ. Ɛɛwɩya, leŋ dɩ ŋ gyɩŋ ɛɛ bee ɛɛ buloŋ ɩ aa kpaa ŋ molbiye tʋma.’

english_verse: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ baal hʋ bɩɩnɩ ʋ tɩya tɩyaŋ a baa, ‘Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ kile ŋ tʋma tɩyaŋ nyɛ, ɛɛ rɛ ŋ sɩ yaa? Ŋ bɩ kaŋ dee ŋ aa sɩ pala di. Ŋ bɩl bɩ sɩ wuwo gɔllɩ kɩ sʋla kɩna mɛ. Ŋ sɩ kɩ fá hɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
translation_verse: Ŋ gyɩŋ gɛɛ ŋ aa sɩ yaa a wuwo kaŋ kyaŋsɩ rɛ, dɩ ŋ tʋma no rɛ ko kyogi mɛ, dɩ ba kana ŋ weliŋ ba dɩɩsɩ tɩyaŋ.’

english_verse: “A hundred barrels of olive oil,” the man answered. So the manager said, “Take your bill and sit down and quickly write ‘
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ baa dɩ nʋʋ aŋkʋrɩsɩ kɔɔ rɛ ʋ kyɩma. Ɛɛ rɛ tʋŋtʋnnɩ-hɩyawʋ hʋ kpaa teŋ hʋ aa daga ʋ kyɩŋ a tɩya ʋ aŋ baa, ‘Teŋ no daga anɩɩ ɩ kyɩŋ yaa nʋʋ aŋkʋrɩsɩ kɔɔ rɛ. Amɛ mɩɩgɩ kaŋ sabɩ aŋkʋrɩsɩ mɔllɩbalɩya abee fi.’

english_verse: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ bɩl yɩrɩ kɩdɩgɩ tɩɩna mɛ a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, ‘Ka ɩ mɛ?’ Ʋ mɛ baa dɩ ʋ kyɩŋ yaa mɩbʋwara tusi rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ tʋŋtʋnnɩ-hɩyawʋ hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Teŋ hʋ aa daga ɩ kyɩŋ nɛ nyɛ. Amɛ mɩɩgɩ kaŋ sabɩ bʋwara kɔɔsɩ kyori.

english_verse: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ kɩna tɩɩna hʋ bigisi sɩŋgɩrɩŋtɩɩna no aŋ baa dɩ ʋ yaa kuwokeri rɛ abee wɩgyʋŋ no ʋ aa kana a kaŋ kɩ yaa wɩbɔmɔ. Beewɩya nala hʋ aa kyo dʋnɩya kɩna kɩŋkaŋ, ba kaŋ kɩ kyɛ wɩgyʋŋ nɛ weliŋ, a kɩ pɛ dɔmɔŋ tɩyaŋ a te nala hʋ aa tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ.’

english_verse: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa, ‘Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, ma kpa ma dʋnɩya kɩna a kyɛ kyaŋsɩ. Dɩ kɩna hʋ rɛ ko teŋ, dɩ Wɩɩsɩ laa ma we ʋ lee, dɩ ma we doŋ a kaŋ kɩ mʋ deŋdeŋ.’

english_verse: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Abɩraham bɩl basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Dɩ ba aa rɛ bɩ sɩ gyegili nɩɩ wɩya hʋ Moosi abee Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala hʋ aa saba, ɛɛ daga anɩɩ dɩ nal lɛ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ a mʋ basɩ tɩya ba mɛ, ba ha bɩ sɩ laa di.”’

english_verse: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
translation_verse: Farasiima hʋ aa nɩɩ wɩya hʋ Yesu aa basa, ba kɩ kyʋʋsɩ lee lee buloŋ, beewɩya ba fa kyo molbiye rɛ weliŋ.

english_verse: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Na ma aa kyɛ dɩ nala bɩɩnɩ anɩɩ ma kaŋ wɩtɩɩ rɛ, amɛ Wɩɩsɩ kɛ gyɩŋ ma tɩsɩ rɛ. Kɩna hʋ aa yaa sɩfɩyaŋ kɩna nihuwobisi lee, ba aa rɛ yaa kɩtɔɔnɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɛ lee.”

english_verse: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩnyuu abee taŋha buloŋ aa sɩ pirigi dɩɩgɩ, ʋ rɛ sɩ yaa mɔl a te wɩbiibiye mɛ aa sɩ dɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ Moosi aa saba tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: Baal hʋ buloŋ aa vɩya ʋ haaŋ, aŋ bɩl mʋ kpa hadɩma, ʋ yaa sɔŋsɔnnɩ rɛ. Baal hʋ mɛ buloŋ aa kpaa haaŋ aa vɩya ʋ bala, ʋ mɛ yaa sɔŋsɔnnɩ.” Yesu maga kɩna tɩɩna kɩdɩgɩ abee Laazʋrɔsɩ namaga

english_verse: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl maga namaga a baa, “Kɩna tɩɩna kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ, a gyɩ kɩ laalɩ gabala dʋŋ, aŋ we sʋma dʋŋ tɩyaŋ kyɛɛ bee ʋ nyʋwa buloŋ.

english_verse: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nyaabʋ hʋ sii sʋba. Malɩkasɩ ko kpa ʋ a kaŋ mʋ holli Abɩraham logiŋ Wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ, ʋ kɩ nɩɩ sʋma.

english_verse: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
translation_verse: Ka kɩna tɩɩna hʋ mɛ sii sʋba, ba kpa ʋ hogo. Ʋ mʋ gyʋʋ diŋ a we tʋwara tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ deŋ mʋ na Abɩraham dɩ ʋ hɔŋ libolii, ka Laazʋrɔsɩ mɛ hɔŋ ʋ logiŋ.

english_verse: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yɩrɩ Abɩraham a baa, ‘Ŋ kuwo Abɩraham, fá ŋ sikii aŋ tɩŋ Laazʋrɔsɩ dɩ ʋ kaŋ ʋ nonii taga nɩɩ, a kaŋ ko lulo we ŋ naŋdeliŋ tɩyaŋ. Ŋ aa na tʋwara rɛ weliŋ diŋ no tɩyaŋ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Abɩraham basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Ŋ biye, liisi mʋ pele saŋa hʋ ɩ gyɩ aa we ɩ mɩɩbol tɩyaŋ, ɩ fa aa nɩɩ sʋma rɛ weliŋ, aŋka Laazʋrɔsɩ fa we tʋwara tɩyaŋ. Amɛ lagɩlagɩ no, Wɩɩsɩ leŋ Laazʋrɔsɩ bembii fɩyɛlɩ rɛ daha, aŋka ɩ mɛ we tʋwara tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
translation_verse: Ŋ naabalɩya bɔnɔŋ ha we doŋ. Leŋ dɩ ʋ mʋ kʋrɩsɩ ba dɩgɩna dɩ ba mɛ ta maakyiye ko gyʋʋ tʋwara no tɩyaŋ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Abɩraham basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Wɩya hʋ Moosi abee Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala hʋ aa saba biŋ, ɩ naabalɩya hʋ gyɩŋ ba rɛ. Leŋ dɩ ba gyegili nɩɩ ba aŋ kɩ tɩŋ ba.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ kɩna tɩɩna hʋ bɩl baa, ‘Ŋ kuwo, ɛɛ dʋŋ bɩ maga. Amɛ dɩ nal lɛ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ a mʋ ba lee, ɛɛ kɛ ba sɩ bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ ba wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Bee rɛ tɩŋ baal no aa bɩ yaa Gyuu dʋŋ mɩɩgɩ ko dɩ ʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ á Tɩɩna Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Dɩ mamaa rɛ fa laa Wɩɩsɩ di mʋhʋ tɩɩ mɛ, ma sɩ wuwo basɩ tɩya tɩya no dɩ ʋ bee ʋ naaliliye buloŋ sii kyuri mʋ pɔ mpʋwɔnʋʋ tɩyaŋ, dɩ ʋ kɔnɩ tɩŋ ma nyʋwa.” Tʋŋtʋnnɩ-welii wɩyaalɩya

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl baa, “Dɩ ma kɩdɩgɩ rɛ kaŋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ dɩ ʋ kɩ pala ɩ baga, koo ʋ kɩ daa ɩ piyese, dɩ ʋ rɛ lɩɩ baga koo pedaa ko, ɩ sɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa dɩ ʋ yaa lagɩ a di ʋ kɩdiiliye koo?

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ we ma tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ɛɛwɩya, nal buloŋ bɩ sɩ wuwo baa, ‘Ma deŋ na, ʋ we daha, koo ʋ we lee kɩdɩgɩ dɔŋ.”’

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ Yesu mɩɩgɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa, “Saŋa kɩdɩgɩ sɩ pele dɩ ma kɩ kyɛ dɩ ma hɔŋ Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye lee kyɛdɩgɩ dʋŋ mɛ gba, amɛ ma bɩ sɩ na gɛɛ.”

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Ɩ sɩ laa nyʋwa kyʋwalɩ ɩ tʋŋtʋnnɩ akuu ʋ aa tʋŋ tʋma hʋ ɩ aa daga ʋ wɩya koo?

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Yesu gyɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ rɛ a baa, “Wɩya hʋ aa sɩ leŋ dɩ nala yaa wɩbɔmɔ, ba sɩ we doŋ. Amɛ nal hʋ aa sɩ leŋ dɩ nala yaa wɩbɔmɔ, ʋ tɩɩna sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ.

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Ma pɔ ma tɩɩ ma weliŋ. Dɩ ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna ko yaa ɩ wɩbɔŋ, leŋ dɩ ʋ gyɩma. Dɩ ʋ rɛ ko baa ʋ kyogo rɛ, kpa kyɛ ʋ.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Dɩ ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna rɛ villi yaa wɩɩ kyogi ɩ kyɛdɩgɩ a maga gɛɛ buloŋ mɛ, ka dɩ ʋ ko yaa buloŋ, ʋ mɩɩgɩ ko a baa dɩ ʋ kyogo rɛ, kpa kyɛ ʋ.” Wɩɩsɩ laadii

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Á Tɩɩna, leŋ dɩ á marɩ laa Wɩɩsɩ di yʋga.”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: Aayɩ, ɩ sɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ sii yaa kɩdiiliye tɩya ɩ, aŋ yaa siri a ko tɩga tɩya ɩ, aŋ yaa wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ ɩ aa kyɛ a tɩya ɩ. Dɩ ɩ rɛ ko di teŋ, dɩ ʋ mɛ yaŋ na di.

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ma mɛ yaŋ kɩɩ! Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko tʋŋ tʋma hʋ ba aa tɩya ma dɩ ma tʋma, ʋ maga dɩ ma baa, ‘Á yaa tʋŋtʋnna rɛ, á bɩ maga dɩ ba lɔllɩ ma, beewɩya á tʋma rɛ á tʋma.”’ Yesu tɩɩbɩ gbegisi fi

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu gyɩ bɩl sii kɩ mʋ Gyerusalɛm, a gyɩ mʋ tɩŋ Samaarɩya abee Galili paalʋʋ paga a kɩ mʋ.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: Ʋ mʋʋ dɩ ʋ gyʋʋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ, dɩ gbegisi fi rɛ ko kɩ kyeme ʋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: Ba tɩyaŋ kɩdɩgɩ aa yaa Samaarɩya tɩɩna aa naa dɩ ʋ deye gɛɛ, ʋ mɩɩgɩ ko Yesu lee a kɩ dannɩ Wɩɩsɩ yʋga.

english_verse: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
translation_verse: A mʋ tuu gbinni Yesu sɩya tɩyaŋ aŋ kyʋwalɩ ʋ aŋgyʋwasʋ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Ma nala fi hʋ buloŋ daa ŋ tɩɩbɩ? Lee rɛ nibi hʋ kɛ we?

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya baal hʋ a baa, “Sii kɩ mʋ, ɩ aa laa Wɩɩsɩ di rɛ tɩŋ ɩ deye.” Yesu basɩ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ koyi wɩya Matiwu

english_verse: The day of the Son of Man will be like lightning flashing across the sky.
translation_verse: Kyɛɛ hʋ Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko, nal buloŋ sɩ na ŋ anɩɩ duwoŋ aa lɩgɩsɩ a kyaanɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu nal nal buloŋ na ʋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Á Tɩɩna, lee tɩyaŋ nɛ wɩya no sɩ yaa?” Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Lee hʋ kɩpʋwa aa pɩna, doŋ nɛ duuno kɩ laŋŋɩ.”

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Amɛ ŋ sɩ laa sɩya na tʋwara, ka dɩ ma gyɩnaŋ nala ha vɩya ŋ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Saŋa hʋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko, ʋ sɩ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ saŋa hʋ Nowa gyɩ aa we ʋ mɩɩbol tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Nowa saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ, nal nal buloŋ gyɩ we doŋ nɛ, a di aŋ kɩ nyʋwa, haana kɩ yala balama, baala mɛ kɩ paa haana, a mʋ pele kyɛɛ hʋ Nowa gyɩ aa gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ, ka nɩɩ ko kpʋ nɩkaalɩya hʋ buloŋ.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: Ka kyɛɛ hʋ Lɔɔtɩ aa lɩɩ Sodɔm tɩyaŋ, diŋ abee gyɩrɩbʋyɩ gyɩ lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu rɛ, a ko tuu kpa nɩkaalɩya hʋ buloŋ aa we doŋ di.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ba sɩ kɩɩ kyɛɛ hʋ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye mɛ aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: Haŋ kyɛɛ hʋ dɩ nal lɛ we salnyuu, ʋ ta bɩl kɩ lʋga dɩ ʋ tuu gyʋʋ ʋ dɩya a paa ʋ kɩna. Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ, dɩ nal mɛ rɛ we baga tɩyaŋ, ʋ ta bɩl kɩ lʋga dɩ ʋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ dɩya a kpa kɩŋ buloŋ.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Ma liisi mʋ na wɩɩ hʋ gyɩ aa yaa Lɔɔtɩ haaŋ.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, haŋ kyɛɛ hʋ, nala balɩya sɩ pɩŋ doo gododɩgɩ tɩyaŋ tebine, dɩ ba kpa kɩdɩgɩ aŋ leŋ kɩdɩgɩ ta.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl maga namaga tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ, a daga ba anɩɩ ba aa yaa ba kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ saŋa buloŋ, aŋ ta leŋ dɩ ba tenni kɩ yʋwɔrɩ.

english_verse: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
translation_verse: Ʋ baa, “Sarɩdiil kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ. Ʋ gyɩ bee fá Wɩɩsɩ, a bee tɩya nihuwobisi mɛ gyɩrɩma.

english_verse: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
translation_verse: Lohaaŋ kɩdɩgɩ mɛ rɛ gyɩ we tɔɔ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Saŋa buloŋ, ʋ gyɩ aa sii mʋ sarɩdiil no lee, a kɩ sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ kyiyeli ʋ, a di sarɩya lo ʋ dɔŋtɩɩna kɩdɩgɩ tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: I will help this widow because she keeps on bothering me. If I don't help her, she will wear me out.”
translation_verse: Amɛ lohaaŋ no aa vala kɩ dɔmɔ ŋ nyɛ, ŋ sɩ pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ, a di sarɩya no tɩya ʋ. Dɩ gɛɛ daa, ʋ ha sɩ vɩya aŋ vala kɩ dɔmɔ ŋ dɩ ŋ lɔgʋ mʋ lɩɩ.’

english_verse: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
translation_verse: Ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma rɛ, ʋ sɩ di ba sarɩya lagɩlagɩ, a kpa wɩsʋma tɩya ba. Amɛ kyɛɛ hʋ Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ ko, ŋ sɩ na nala aa laa Wɩɩsɩ di?” Yesu maga Farasii kɩdɩgɩ abee lampolaal namaga

english_verse: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl maga namaga no a tɩya nala hʋ aa laa ba tɩɩ di anɩɩ ba aa rɛ kaŋ tɩpʋlʋŋ a te nal buloŋ, aŋ kaŋ ba dɔŋtɩŋsɩ kɩ kʋwasɩ.

english_verse: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasii hʋ sii sɩŋ, a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ ʋ tɩya tɩyaŋ a baa, ‘Ŋ Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ, ŋ aa kyʋwalɩ ɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ rɛ, beewɩya ŋ bɩ kɩɩ nala hʋ aa gɔllɩ kaŋ nala kɩna kɩ fasɩ, koo nala hʋ aa bɩ kaŋ wɩtɩɩ, koo nala hʋ aa gɔllɩ kyɛ nala haana kɩ baŋ. Ŋ bɩ kɩɩ lampolaal no mɛ aa we daha.

english_verse: I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.”
translation_verse: Ŋ aa vʋwa nyʋwa bʋlɩya rɛ yɔbɔ buloŋ tɩyaŋ. Kɩŋ hʋ buloŋ mɛ ŋ aa naa, ŋ aa kaŋ kpaa leye fi rɛ, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya ɩ.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, saŋa hʋ ba aa kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ hʋ teŋ, ba balɩya tɩyaŋ, lampolaal hʋ rɛ yaa tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ sɩya tɩyaŋ. Nal hʋ buloŋ aa kpa ʋ tɩɩ yohuŋ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kana ʋ fʋŋɩsɩ. Amɛ nal hʋ buloŋ aa kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ kɩ hʋwɔlɩ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kpa ʋ yohuŋ.” Yesu sʋla Wɩɩsɩ tɩya biibiisi Matiwu

english_verse: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Nala badɔmɔŋ mɛ rɛ gyɩ sii kaŋ ba biibiisi ko Yesu lee dɩ ʋ kpa ʋ nosi dɔbɔ ba tɩyaŋ, a sʋla Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ba. Yesu hatɩnna hʋ na ba, a nɔsɩ ba.

english_verse: will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ sɩ tɩya ʋ tɩɩna kɩna yʋga a te kɩna hʋ ʋ aa leŋ ta. Dɩ ʋ rɛ ko sʋba mɛ, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ tɩya ʋ mɩɩbol hʋ aa bɩ kaŋ tenii.” Yesu bɩl basɩ ʋ sʋʋ wɩya Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu kaŋ ʋ hatɩnna fi abee balɩya hʋ gɩɩgɩ lɩɩ, a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma gyegili nɩɩ daha. Á aa mʋ Gyerusalɛm nɛ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ, Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye wɩya hʋ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala hʋ aa saba biŋ, ba buloŋ sɩ ko su ba bʋʋsɩ.

english_verse: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma nɩhɩyawʋ kɩdɩgɩ ko Yesu lee a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Nɩhɩyawelii, ɛɛ rɛ ŋ sɩ yaa a kaŋ mɩɩbol aa bɩ kaŋ tenii?”

english_verse: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ baal hʋ baa, “Ŋ aa tɩŋ wɩya no kɛ buloŋ nɛ a lɩɩ ŋ biibiri tɩyaŋ buloŋ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ, Yesu bɩl basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Wɩdɩgɩ rɛ kaa ɩ ha aa sɩ yaa. Mʋ kpa ɩ kɩŋ kɩŋ buloŋ yallɩ, a kpa molbiye hʋ tɩya nyaaba, aŋ ko kɩ tɩŋa ŋ. Dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, ɩ sɩ kaŋ kɩna wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Baal hʋ aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, ʋ tɩya buloŋ kyogi, beewɩya ʋ gyɩ kaŋ kɩna rɛ weliŋ.

english_verse: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Yesu aa naa dɩ ʋ tɩya kyogo gɛɛ, ʋ baa, “Ʋ yaa wɩduwo rɛ anɩɩ kɩna tɩmma aa sɩ wuwo gyʋʋ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Nyɔgɩmaa aa sɩ tɩŋ gahaŋ sɩɩ lɩɩ, ʋ rɛ yaa mɔl a te gɛɛ kɩna tɩɩna aa sɩ yaa a gyʋʋ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Nala hʋ aa nɩɩ Yesu aa basɩ gɛɛ, ba pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Kɩbee rɛ yaŋ Wɩɩsɩ sɩ laa ta?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Wɩɩ hʋ aa kperi nihuwobiŋ, ʋ bɩ kpere Wɩɩsɩ kɛ.”

english_verse: He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him.
translation_verse: Ba sɩ kpa ŋ we nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma nosi tɩyaŋ dɩ ba yaa ŋ sɩya. Ba sɩ tʋʋsɩ ŋ aŋ pɛ tʋ naŋtʋwɔl mɛ we ŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
translation_verse: Ba sɩ vɩɩrɩ ŋ aŋ kpʋ ŋ, amɛ ŋ sɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ ʋ kyɛtoo kyɛɛ.”

english_verse: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
translation_verse: Nyʋlʋŋ hʋ aa nɩɩ nɩgyamaa hʋ aa baŋ dɔmɔ, ʋ pɩyɛsɩ a baa, “Bee rɛ aa yaa?”

english_verse: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba basɩ tɩya ʋ anɩɩ Yesu aa lɩɩ Nazarɛti rɛ aa baŋ.

english_verse: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yɩrɩ Yesu a baa, “Yesu, Deviti nihii, fá ŋ sikii.”

english_verse: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Nala hʋ aa we ʋ sɩya nɔsɩ ʋ, dɩ ʋ leŋ ʋ kyagɩŋsɩ hʋ. Amɛ ʋ marɩ yɩrɩ yʋga a te gɛɛ a baa, “Yesu, Deviti nihii, fá ŋ sikii.”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sɩŋ aŋ baa dɩ ba kaŋ nyʋlʋŋ hʋ ko ʋ lee.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ, “Ɩ sɩya suro rɛ. Ɩ aa laa Wɩɩsɩ dɩ rɛ rɛ tɩŋ ɩ deye.”

english_verse: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ ʋ sɩya pirigi suri, ʋ sii kɩ tɩŋ Yesu hal a kɩ dannɩ Wɩɩsɩ. Nɩgyamaa hʋ buloŋ aa naa gɛɛ, ba buloŋ mɛ kɩ dannɩ Wɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ parɩ nala hʋ buloŋ ta, aŋ fá mʋ sɩya, a gyɩŋ tɩya dɩ ʋ wuwo na Yesu. Ʋ gyɩ gyɩma anɩɩ Yesu sɩ ko tɩŋ tɩya hʋ memii baŋ nɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
translation_verse: Yesu aa ko tɩŋ tɩya hʋ memii kɩ baŋ, ʋ wil deŋ tɩya hʋ nyuu a na Zakiyo, a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Zakiyo, ko tuu lagɩ, beewɩya ɩ dɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ sɩ pɩŋ gyɩnaŋ.”

english_verse: Everyone who saw this started grumbling, “This man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.”
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ aa we doŋ aa naa gɛɛ, ba piili kɩ gʋrɩmɩ wɩya a baa, “A! Ma na baal no kɔnɩ aa mʋ dɩ ʋ pɩŋ wɩbɔŋyaal no dɩya tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
translation_verse: Saŋa hʋ á Tɩɩna Yesu aa we Zakiyo dɩya tɩyaŋ, Zakiyo sii sɩŋ a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ, ŋ sɩnɩɩ no tɩyaŋ, ŋ sɩ kaŋ ŋ kɩna buloŋ kpaa leye balɩya, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya nyaaba. Ka dɩ mɩyaŋ nɛ fa firimi nal buloŋ mɛ, ŋ sɩ tuŋ ʋ tɩɩna maga gɛɛ ŋ aa firimi ʋ naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋnaa.”

english_verse: Jesus said to Zacchaeus, “Today you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ laa ɩ bee ɩ dɩya nala buloŋ ta rɛ gyɩnaŋ. Beewɩya ɩ mɛ yaa Abɩraham doho nal lɛ.

english_verse: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye ko dɩ ŋ kyɛ na nala hʋ aa nyʋgɩsa rɛ, a laa ba ta.” Yesu maga salma molbinʋʋ namaga Matiwu

english_verse: But they said, “Sir, he already has ten times as much!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Nɩhɩyawʋ, na ʋ kɛ foŋ kaŋ molbiye fi rɛ.’

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
translation_verse: Amɛ nɩbal no bee nala gyɩ haasɩ ʋ rɛ weliŋ. Ʋ gyɩ aa mʋ ko teŋ, ba mɛ gyɩ sii tɩŋ tɩŋdaala dɩ ba kile ʋ lugo, a mʋ basɩ tɩya kuworibal hʋ lee ʋ aa mʋ dɩ ʋ holli ʋ koro hʋ, anɩɩ ba bɩ kyo dɩ baal hʋ yaa ba kuwori.

english_verse: He told them, “Go into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here.
translation_verse: “Ma mʋ tɔɔ hʋ aa we ma sɩya. Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko mʋ, ma sɩ na kokubiye dɩ ba kaŋ vʋwa sɩgɩ. Nal buloŋ ha bɩ maakyiye gyɩna ʋ. Ma puri a kaŋ ko.

english_verse: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ buŋbuŋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ ko basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ, molbii hʋ ɩ aa kpaa tɩya ŋ hʋ, ŋ mɛ kaŋ tʋma rɛ a na maga gɛɛ fi a pɛ.’

english_verse: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ tʋŋtʋnnɩlɩya hʋ tɩɩna mɛ ko ʋ lee a baa, ‘Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ, molbii hʋ ɩ aa kpaa tɩya ŋ hʋ, ŋ mɛ kaŋ tʋma rɛ a na maga gɛɛ bʋnɔŋ a pɛ.’

english_verse: Another servant came and said, “Sir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ tʋŋtʋnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ mɛ ko a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ, na ɩ molbii hʋ ɩ aa kpaa tɩya ŋ. Ŋ kaŋ mʋ gulo gakɔsʋŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ a kpa faŋa.

english_verse: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
translation_verse: Bee rɛ yaŋ tɩŋ ɩ fa bɩ kaŋ ŋ molbii hʋ mʋ biŋ bangi tɩyaŋ? Ŋ fa aa mɩɩgɩ ko nyɛ, ŋ fa sɩ laa molbii hʋ abee ʋ tɔnɔ buloŋ.’

english_verse: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ dɔŋtɩŋsɩ hʋ fa aa sɩŋ doŋ a baa, ‘Ma laa salɩŋbidɩgɩ hʋ ʋ lee, a kpa tɩya nal hʋ aa kaŋ kɩna fi hʋ.’

english_verse: The king replied, “Those who have something will be given more. But everything will be taken away from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, ‘Nal hʋ aa kaŋ kɩna yʋga, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ marɩ kpa kɩna yʋga a pɛ a tɩya ʋ, amɛ nal hʋ aa bɩ kaŋ kɩna yʋga, mʋhʋ hʋ mɛ ʋ aa kana, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kaŋ ba laa ʋ lee.

english_verse: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Ka nala hʋ mɛ aa haasɩ ŋ, a bɩ kyo dɩ ŋ yaa ba kuwori hʋ, ma kasɩ ba ko ŋ sɩya daha, a kpʋ ba ŋ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ.”’ Yesu gyʋʋ Gyerusalɛm abee gandarɩ Matiwu

english_verse: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yesu aa basɩ wɩya no ko teŋ, ʋ sii kpa ŋmanɩɩ, a laa ʋ hatɩnna hʋ sɩya, ba kɩ mʋ Gyerusalɛm.

english_verse: As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead.
translation_verse: Ba aa ko kpaga Bɛtifage abee Bɛɛtani, a ko pele Olivi dogimo hʋ lee, ɛɛ rɛ Yesu lɩɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ tɩyaŋ nala balɩya a tɩŋ ba a baa,

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
translation_verse: Dɩ nal buloŋ nɛ ko pɩyɛsɩ ma a baa dɩ bee rɛ tɩŋ ma kɩ puri, ma baa dɩ Tɩɩna hʋ rɛ baa dɩ ma puri kaŋ ko.”

english_verse: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
translation_verse: Ba aa mʋʋ doŋ, wɩya hʋ buloŋ kɔnɩ yaa rɛ anɩɩ gɛɛ Yesu aa basɩ tɩya ba.

english_verse: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
translation_verse: Ba aa puri kokubiye hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ʋ tɩmma pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ ma kɩ puri kokubiye hʋ?”

english_verse: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba kaŋ kokubiye hʋ mʋ tɩya Yesu, a wuri ba gabala a dɔbɔ kokubiye hʋ tɩyaŋ, aŋ tɩga tɩya Yesu ʋ gyɩŋ.

english_verse: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa gyɩŋ kokubiye hʋ kɩ mʋ tɩyaŋ, nala kpa ba gabala a gyarɩ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ kɩ vala ba tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen.
translation_verse: Yesu aa mʋʋ kpaga Gyerusalɛm, lee hʋ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ a lɩɩ Olivi dogimo hʋ nyuu tuu, nɩgyamaa hʋ fa aa tɩŋ ʋ hal piili kɩ gyʋwɔsɩ, a kɩ dannɩ Wɩɩsɩ yʋga akuu wɩmagɩla hʋ buloŋ ba aa naa wɩya.

english_verse: They shouted, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory to God.”
translation_verse: Ba baa, “Wɩɩsɩ sɩ pɛ kuwori hʋ aa ko á Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ feŋ tɩyaŋ. Fɩyɛlʋʋ sɩ we Wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ, ka dɩ Wɩɩsɩ feŋ mɛ yɩrɩ.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, make your disciples stop shouting!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Farasiima badɔmɔŋ fa aa we nɩgyamaa hʋ tɩyaŋ basɩ tɩya Yesu a baa, “Nɩhɩyawʋ, basɩ tɩya ɩ hatɩnna hʋ dɩ ba leŋ kyagɩŋsɩ.”

english_verse: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
translation_verse: “Ŋ fa kyo dɩ ma bee no nala gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ ma aa sɩ yaa a hɔŋ abee laaŋfɩya rɛ gyɩnaŋ, amɛ lagɩlagɩ no ʋ yaa wɩfaŋɩɩ rɛ ma lee.

english_verse: Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side.
translation_verse: Kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ sɩ pele dɩ ma dɔmɔ ko kyige ma bee no, a tɔ ma we, dɩ ma kyɛ lee lɔlɔ dɩ ma lɩɩ fá.

english_verse: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba sɩ kpʋ bee no nal nal buloŋ, aŋ kyeŋŋi bee hʋ mɛ buloŋ. Bʋdɩgɩ mɛ bɩ sɩ ka dɔbɔ ʋ dɔŋtɩɩna tɩyaŋ. Beewɩya, saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa ko dɩ ʋ laa ma ta, ma vɩya ʋ nyʋwa rɛ.” Yesu gyʋʋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ Matiwu

english_verse: When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu sii mʋ gyʋʋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ, a na dɩ nala aa yallɩ kɩna doŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ piili kil ba kɩ ta ba kɩ lɩɩ,

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: aŋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ba saba Wɩɩsɩ teŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ a baa dɩ Wɩɩsɩ baa: ‘Ŋ dɩya sɩ yaa lee hʋ nala aa sɩ kɩ kyʋwalɩ ŋ nɛ’. Aŋka ma kɛ mɩɩgɩ kaŋ bɩrɩmɩ gaala dɩya.”

english_verse: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
translation_verse: Kyɛɛ kɛ buloŋ nɛ Yesu gyɩ aa kɩ daga nala Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ.

english_verse: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala hʋ ba aa turo we hʋ sii mʋ Yesu lee, a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Nɩhɩyawʋ, á gyɩma anɩɩ wɩya hʋ ɩ aa basɩ abee wɩya hʋ ɩ daga ma buloŋ yaa wɩtɩɩ rɛ. Á gyɩma anɩɩ ɩ aa daga Wɩɩsɩ ŋmanɩɩ tɩɩ rɛ, aŋ bee fá nal buloŋ mɛ sɩɩ.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sadusiima badɔmɔŋ mɛ sii ko Yesu lee. Nala no gyɩ laa di rɛ anɩɩ nala hʋ aa sʋba bɩl bɩ sɩ wuwo sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba pɩyɛsɩ Yesu a baa,

english_verse: When God raises people from death, whose wife will this woman be? All seven brothers had married her.
translation_verse: Kyɛɛ hʋ nala aa sɩ mɩɩgɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ, baala no bapɛ tɩyaŋ kɩbee rɛ sɩ tɩŋ haaŋ hʋ? Beewɩya ba buloŋ bapɛ rɛ ʋ yala ko baŋ parɩ.”

english_verse: asked him, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: A pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Degɛɛ rɛ ɩ kana a wuwo kɩ yaa wɩya no? Kɩbee rɛ tɩya ɩ ŋmanɩɩ ɩ kɩ yaa wɩya no buloŋ?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Aŋnɛ rɛ gyɩ tɩya Gyɔɔŋ ŋmanɩɩ ʋ kɩ fo nala wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ gɛɛ, Wɩɩsɩ rɛ koo nihuwobisi rɛ?”

english_verse: And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.”
translation_verse: Dɩ ámaa rɛ bɩl baa dɩ nihuwobisi rɛ tɩya ʋ gɛɛ ŋmanɩɩ, gyamaa no aa laŋŋa daha sɩ paa bʋwa yaga ma, beewɩya ba buloŋ laa di anɩɩ Gyɔɔŋ fa yaa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal lɛ.”

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Á bɩ gyɩŋ nal hʋ aa tɩya ʋ gɛɛ ŋmanɩɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ Yesu mɛ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Tɔɔ, ŋ mɛ bɩ sɩ daga ma nal hʋ aa tɩya ŋ ŋmanɩɩ ŋ kɩ yaa wɩya no.” Yesu maga baga tʋŋtʋnna badɔmɔŋ namaga Matiwu

english_verse: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: “Ma pɔ ma tɩɩ ma weliŋ abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla no wɩya. Ba aa gɔllɩ kɩ baŋ abee ba gadoliye rɛ gyiyegyiye, a kɩ kyɛ dɩ nala yʋwɔgɩ kɩ kyʋwalɩ ba. Dɩ ba aa rɛ ko mʋ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya koo laŋŋɩbal buloŋ, ba aa kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba hɔŋ nɩbala lɩhɔnɩya rɛ.

english_verse: When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ tɩɩsɩ hʋ ko nɔnɔ a yaa tɔrɩɩ. Baga hʋ tɩɩna sii tɩŋ ʋ dɩya tʋŋtʋnna kɩdɩgɩ dɩ ʋ mʋ baga tʋŋtʋnna hʋ lee a laa ʋ tɩɩnɔnɔ tol ko tɩya ʋ. Ʋ sii mʋ, ba kana ʋ a ŋmaa ʋ weliŋ, aŋ kile ʋ ta, ʋ mɩɩgɩ kaŋ nohɩllɩ mʋ baga hʋ tɩɩna lee.

english_verse: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Tʋŋtʋnna hʋ aa naa biye hʋ dɩ ʋ aa ko, ɛɛ rɛ ba basɩ tɩya dɔmɔŋ a baa, ‘Baga hʋ tɩɩna biye tɩɩ rɛ nyɛ. Dɩ baga hʋ tɩɩna rɛ ko sʋba, biye no rɛ sɩ di ʋ kɩna hʋ buloŋ kyaŋ. Ma leŋ dɩ á kpʋ ʋ, aŋka dɩ baga hʋ bɩrɩmɩ á kɩŋ.’

english_verse: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩ ko kpʋ tʋŋtʋnna hʋ buloŋ, aŋ kpa baga hʋ a tɩya nɩdʋŋsɩ.’ Nala hʋ aa nɩɩ wɩya hʋ Yesu aa basa, ba baa dɩ Wɩɩsɩ ta laa nyʋwa dɩ gɛɛ yaa.

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ kɩ lʋga dɩ ba kaŋ Yesu doŋ tɩyaŋ, beewɩya ba gyɩma anɩɩ ba aa rɛ ʋ maga namaga hʋ kɩ daga. Amɛ ba gyɩ aa fá nala hʋ aa we doŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Then he told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Ɛɛ kɛ, kɩŋ hʋ aa yaa Roma paalʋʋ kuworibal hʋ kɩŋ, ma kpa tɩya ʋ, ka kɩŋ hʋ mɛ aa yaa Wɩɩsɩ kɩŋ, ma kpa tɩya Wɩɩsɩ.”

english_verse: Jesus' enemies could not catch him saying anything wrong there in front of the people. They were amazed at his answer and kept quiet. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ, ba bɩl bɩ naa wɩɩ buloŋ ba aa sɩ wuwo tɩŋ gɛɛ tɩyaŋ kana ʋ nala aa we doŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ. Wɩya hʋ ʋ aa basa yaa ba wɩkperii rɛ, ba nyʋʋsɩ buloŋ mɛ fɩyɛlɩ. Ba pɩyasɩ Yesu sʋʋnɩ mɩɩgɩ siiyi wɩya Matiwu

english_verse: and said: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Á nɩhɩyawʋ, Wɩɩsɩ teŋ tɩyaŋ, Moosi daga ma anɩɩ dɩ baal lɛ kpaa haaŋ, dɩ ʋ bee haaŋ hʋ bɩ kaŋ biye, aŋka dɩ baal hʋ sʋba, dɩ ʋ maga ʋ naabiye kpa lohaaŋ hʋ rɛ a lʋl biisi tɩya ʋ maana hʋ aa sʋba.

english_verse: There were once seven brothers. The first one married, but died without having any children.
translation_verse: Tɔɔ, nɩmmabalɩya bapɛ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ, ba buloŋ yaa baala. Ba kɩhɩyawʋ tɩɩna kpa haaŋ. Ʋ bee haaŋ hʋ bɩ lʋl biye, ka ʋ sʋba.

english_verse: The second one married his brother's widow, and he also died without having any children.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ katɩyal kpa haaŋ hʋ. Ʋ beel-ʋ mɛ bɩ lʋl biye, aŋ ka ʋ mɛ sʋba.

english_verse: The same thing happened to the third one. Finally, all seven brothers married this woman and died without having any children.
translation_verse: Too hʋ tɩɩna mɛ kpa haaŋ hʋ. Ɛɛ tɩɩ puu ʋ mɛ. Nyɛ rɛ nɩmmabalɩya no bapɛ buloŋ kpa haaŋ hʋ, a sʋba mʋ teŋ dɩ ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bee haaŋ hʋ bɩ lʋl biye.

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ haaŋ hʋ mɛ sii sʋba.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses said, “Teacher, you have given a good answer!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla badɔmɔŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Nɩhɩyawʋ, ɩ bee nyɩya! Ɩ basɩ ba bʋʋsɩŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Why do people say that the Messiah will be the son of King David?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Ɛɛ rɛ ba sɩ wuwo baa dɩ Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya, sɩ yaa Deviti nihii rɛ?

english_verse: In the book of Psalms, David himself says, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right side
translation_verse: Deviti tɩɩ basa Wɩɩsɩ teŋ logiŋ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Yɩɩla tɩyaŋ nɛ a baa, “Ŋ Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ basɩ tɩya ŋ Tɩɩna rɛ a baa, ‘hɔŋ ŋ noduu logiŋ,

english_verse: until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: ka dɩ ŋ yuwo di ɩ dɔmɔ buloŋ teŋ’.

english_verse: David spoke of the Messiah as his Lord, so how can the Messiah be his son?” Jesus and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: Deviti tɩɩ yɩrɩ nal no ʋ Tɩɩna rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yaŋ sɩ yaa a yaa Deviti nihii dʋŋ?” Yesu basɩ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ wɩya Matiwu

english_verse: While everyone was listening to Jesus, he said to his disciples:
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ gyɩ aa gyegile kɩ nɩɩ Yesu wɩbasɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ʋ mɩɩgɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ a baa,

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and then pray long prayers just to show off. These teachers will be punished most of all.
translation_verse: Ba aa fugo kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɩ pɩɩsɩ rɛ, a kaŋ lohaana kɩna kɩ fasɩ. Saŋa hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa sɩ di dʋnɩya sarɩya, ba tʋwara bɩ sɩ kyallɩ.”

english_verse: Before all this happens, you will be arrested and punished. You will be tried in your synagogues and put in jail. Because of me you will be placed on trial before kings and governors.
translation_verse: Aŋka dɩ wɩya no buloŋ na yaa, ba sɩ kasɩ ma, a dɔgɩsɩ ma. Ba sɩ kaŋ ma gyʋʋ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩsɩ, a di ma sarɩya aŋ kaŋ ma tɔ dɩɩsɩ tɩyaŋ. Ba sɩ kuu ŋ wɩya, a kaŋ ma mʋ kuworo abee nɩbala sɩya.

english_verse: Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had.” The Temple Will Be Destroyed (Matthew
translation_verse: Beewɩya, nala hʋ buloŋ aa kpaa molbiye a sʋla Wɩɩsɩ, ba lɩya rɛ aŋ ka. Amɛ haaŋ no kɛ, abee ʋ nyaabʋsɩ hʋ buloŋ, ʋ dɩya abee ʋ bee buloŋ nɛ ʋ kpa sʋla Wɩɩsɩ gɛɛ.” Yesu basɩ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ telii wɩya Matiwu

english_verse: Some people were talking about the beautiful stones used to build the temple and about the gifts that had been placed in it. Jesus said,
translation_verse: Yesu hatɩnna hʋ badɔmɔŋ gyɩ aa basɩ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ wɩya rɛ, a baa dɩ ʋ weliye, dɩ bʋwa hʋ ba aa kpaa saa ʋ mɛ weliye, abee kɩweliye hʋ iriŋ nala aa kpaa sʋla Wɩɩsɩ ba kpa marɩ dɩya hʋ tɩya, dɩ ba buloŋ nɛ leŋ dɩya hʋ weliye gɛɛ.

english_verse: “Do you see these stones? The time is coming when not one of them will be left in place. They will all be knocked down.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Dɩya no bee ʋ kɩweliye hʋ buloŋ ma aa naa nyɛ, saŋa kɩdɩgɩ sɩ pele dɩ ba ŋmaa ba buloŋ lo. Bʋdɩgɩ mɛ paalɩ bɩ sɩ ka a dɔbɔ ʋ dɔŋtɩɩna tɩyaŋ.” Yesu basa ba aa sɩ dɔgɩsɩ nala hʋ aa tɩŋa ʋ wɩya Matiwu

english_verse: Some people asked, “Teacher, when will all this happen? How can we know when these things are about to take place?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba pɩyɛsɩ Yesu a baa, “Nɩhɩyawʋ, saŋa bee tɩyaŋ nɛ wɩya no sɩ yaa? Bekiŋ wɩɩ rɛ sɩ yaa dɩ á na a gyɩma anɩɩ wɩya no kpaga yayɩ rɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: Don't be fooled by those who will come and claim to be me. They will say, “I am Christ!” and “Now is the time!” But don't follow them.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma kɩ gyɩma weliŋ dɩ nal buloŋ ta ko mʋrɩ ma. Nala yʋga sɩ ko a kpa ŋ feŋ anɩɩ ba aa rɛ yaa Krisita hʋ, a baa dɩ saŋa hʋ dʋnɩya aa sɩ teŋ kpaga rɛ. Amɛ ma ta ko kɩ tɩŋ ba hal.

english_verse: When you hear about wars and riots, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but this isn't the end.
translation_verse: Ma sɩ nɩɩ yuwo abee kaasɩ dɔmɔ lɩkpagɩsɩ abee liboliye tɩyaŋ. Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko nɩɩ ba wɩya, ma ta leŋ dɩ ma bembiye kɩ sii. Wɩya no buloŋ sɩ laa sɩya yaa, amɛ gɛɛ bɩ daga nɩɩ dʋnɩya hʋ tenii pele rɛ.”

english_verse: There will be great earthquakes, and in many places people will starve to death and suffer terrible diseases. All sorts of frightening things will be seen in the sky.
translation_verse: Taŋha sɩ gyigili kɩŋkaŋ, dɩ losi mɛ gyʋʋ. Wɩɩla sɩ gyʋʋ nala lee buloŋ tɩyaŋ, wɩbɔŋlɔrɩnɩ abee wɩmagɩla buloŋ sɩ yaa wɩɩsɩnyuu tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: You will be betrayed by your own parents, brothers, family, and friends. Some of you will even be killed.
translation_verse: Ma kuwoma abee ma nɩmmabalɩya abee ma lʋlla tɩɩ abee ma kyaŋsɩ sɩ kpa ma yallɩ tɩya ma dɔmɔ, dɩ ba kpʋ ma badɔmɔŋ.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be hated by everyone.
translation_verse: Nal nal buloŋ sɩ haasɩ ma ŋ wɩya.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at this time, run to the mountains. If you are in the city, leave it. And if you are out in the country, don't go back into the city.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ, ma leŋ dɩ nala hʋ aa we Gyudɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ fá mʋ gyɩŋ dogiŋsi nyuni. Nala hʋ mɛ aa we bee hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ maga dɩ ba lɩɩ bee hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Nala hʋ mɛ aa we ba bagɩsɩ tɩyaŋ, ba mɛ ta mɩɩgɩ gyʋʋ bee hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: When you see Jerusalem surrounded by soldiers, you will know that it will soon be destroyed.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl baa, “Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko na dɩ laal tɔɔ Gyerusalɛm, ma gyɩma anɩɩ bee hʋ kyeŋŋii pele rɛ.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children! Everywhere in the land people will suffer horribly and be punished.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ, haluwokanna abee biifɩllɩ nɩmma sɩ na tʋwara rɛ weliŋ. Wɩɩsɩ sɩ na baaŋ paalʋʋ no nala nyuu tɩyaŋ, a dɔgɩsɩ ba kɩŋkaŋ.

english_verse: Then Jesus told them a story: When you see a fig tree or any other tree
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu maga namaga no tɩya ba a baa, “Ma deŋ na tutogo abee tɩɩsɩ buloŋ.

english_verse: So, when you see these things happening, you know that God's kingdom will soon be here.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ, dɩ mamaa rɛ ko na wɩya no buloŋ ŋ aa daga ma piile kɩ yaa, ma gyɩma anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ kpaga rɛ.

english_verse: Then the Son of Man will be seen, coming in a cloud with power and great glory.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ ma sɩ na mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye dɩ ŋ we taala tɩyaŋ ko kɩ tuu, a kaŋ dee abee gaŋdarɩ.

english_verse: Strange things will happen to the sun, moon, and stars. The nations on earth will be afraid of the roaring sea and tides, and they won't know what to do.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu bɩl baa, “Wɩmagɩla sɩ yaa wɩɩpɔsɩɩ abee pene abee kyɩŋwʋlɩya tɩyaŋ. Dʋnɩya buloŋ tɩyaŋ, nala tɩsɩ sɩ kyogi dɩ ba bɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ tɩyaŋ ba aa wee. Beewɩya mpʋwɔnʋʋ sɩ dɔŋ, dɩ kambɩŋ gyʋʋ nala.

english_verse: People will be so frightened that they will faint because of what is happening to the world. Every power in the sky will be shaken.
translation_verse: Kambɩŋ sɩ gyʋʋ nala dɩ ba yiyegi, akuu ba aa lurimi na wɩɩ hʋ aa sɩ yaa dʋnɩya buloŋ tɩyaŋ wɩya. Beewɩya ba sɩ kile wɩɩsɩnyuu kɩna buloŋ aa kaŋ dee dɩ ba lɩɩ ba liweeliye tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all of this takes place.
translation_verse: Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ma nala no aa we ma mɩɩbol tɩyaŋ gyɩnaŋ, ma buloŋ bɩ sɩ sʋba, aŋka dɩ wɩya no yaa.

english_verse: like a trap. This day will surprise everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Beewɩya kyɛɛ hʋ aa puu nal buloŋ aa we dʋnɩya logiŋ buloŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: putting out leaves, you know that summer will soon come.
translation_verse: Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko na tɩɩsɩ piile kɩ tɔsɔ, ma aa gyɩma anɩɩ yʋʋ kpaga rɛ.

english_verse: Watch out and keep praying that you can escape all that is going to happen and that the Son of Man will be pleased with you.
translation_verse: Ma gyɩŋ tɩya ma tɩɩ, a kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ saŋa buloŋ dɩ ma wuwo gyʋʋ wɩya no buloŋ tɩyaŋ a lɩɩ bee laaŋfɩya, aŋ wuwo maakyiye sɩŋ Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye sɩya tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Jesus taught in the temple each day, and he spent each night on the Mount of Olives.
translation_verse: Yesu fa aa we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ kyɩkyʋwala abee wɩhaa buloŋ a kɩ daga nala. Dɩ dɩdaana rɛ ko pele buloŋ, ʋ aa mʋ Olivi dogimo hʋ nyuu rɛ a kyʋwa doŋ.

english_verse: Everyone got up early and came to the temple to hear him teach.
translation_verse: Sɩgballɩya buloŋ, nala aa mʋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ rɛ dɩ ba gyegile nɩɩ wɩya hʋ ʋ aa basɩ.

english_verse: They were very pleased and offered to pay Judas some money.
translation_verse: Ba tenni gyɩ fɩyɛlɩ weliŋ, ba laa nyʋwa anɩɩ ba sɩ tɩya ʋ molbiye.

english_verse: I tell you I will not eat another Passover meal until it is finally eaten in God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Beewɩya, ŋ bɩl bɩ sɩ maakyiye di kɩdiiliye no iriŋ, see saŋa hʋ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ aa sɩ ko. Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ ma sɩ gyɩŋ kɩdiiliye no dii memii.”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were looking for a way to get rid of Jesus, because they were afraid of what the people might do.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ kɩ lʋga dɩ ba na ŋmanɩɩ hʋ ba aa sɩ tɩŋa a lʋwɔlɩ kpʋ Yesu, beewɩya ba gyɩ aa fá nala hʋ rɛ. Gyudasɩ laa nyʋwa dɩ ʋ kpa Yesu yallɩ Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus told them, “As you go into the city, you will meet a man carrying a jar of water. Follow him into the house
translation_verse: Ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Dɩ mamaa rɛ ko mʋ kɩ gyʋʋ bee hʋ, ma sɩ na baal kɩdɩgɩ dɩ ʋ baasɩ vii abee nɩɩ. Ma tɩŋa ʋ hal mʋ gyʋʋ dɩya hʋ ʋ aa sɩ gyʋʋ.

english_verse: Jesus got up from praying and went over to his disciples. They were asleep and worn out from being so sad.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ hʋ teŋ, ʋ sii a mɩɩgɩ mʋ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ lee, dɩ ba pɩŋ doo, beewɩya tɩkyogi hʋ gyɩ leŋ lɔl gyʋʋ ba rɛ weliŋ.

english_verse: Judas went to talk with the chief priests and the officers of the temple police about how he could help them arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ sii mʋ, ʋ bee Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee nala hʋ aa pɔ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ nɩhɩyasɩ vʋʋlɩ gɛɛ ʋ aa sɩ yaa a kpa Yesu yallɩ tɩya ba.

english_verse: He agreed and started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus when the crowds were not around. Jesus Eats with His Disciples (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyudasɩ mɛ laa nyʋwa, aŋ piili kɩ kyɛ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa sɩ tɩŋa a kpa Yesu yallɩ tɩya ba dɩ nala bɩ gyɩma. Yesu bee ʋ hatɩnna dii Dɩɩsɩ-parɩ-banɩɩ Kyɛbal hʋ kɩdiiliye Matiwu

english_verse: Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and gave thanks to God. Then he told the apostles, “Take this wine and share it with each other.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu kpa gbaŋbiye bee sɩŋ, a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ, aŋ kpa tɩya ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aŋ baa, “Ma laa gbaŋbiye bee sɩŋ no a kaŋ kpaa dɔmɔŋ nyʋwa.

english_verse: The one who will betray me is here at the table with me!
translation_verse: Amɛ ma naa rɛ? Nal hʋ aa sɩ kpa ŋ yallɩ, ʋ we á tɩyaŋ daha. Ŋ beel-ʋ rɛ laŋŋa kɩ di kɩna hʋ nyɛ.

english_verse: You will eat and drink with me in my kingdom, and you will each sit on a throne to judge the twelve tribes of Israel. Jesus' Disciples Will Be Tested (Matthew
translation_verse: Dɩ ŋ beel-ma buloŋ maakyiye wuwo di kɩdiiliye ŋ koro hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ma sɩ hɔŋ kuworikpasɩnɩ nyuni a kɩ deŋ Iziral tɩmma bekyige fi abee balɩya hʋ nyuu.” Yesu baa dɩ Piita sɩ tʋwa a baa ʋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ Matiwu

english_verse: The day had come for the Festival of Thin Bread, and it was time to kill the Passover lambs.
translation_verse: Dɩɩsɩ-parɩ-banɩɩ Kyɛbal hʋ tɩyaŋ, kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ rɛ we doŋ ba aa kyaŋ paanʋʋ ba aa bɩ wee sɩbʋl. Kyɛɛ no tɩɩ mɛ rɛ ba aa kpʋ pepɔrɔ hʋ ba aa sɩ kpa marɩ Kyɛbal hʋ kɩdiiliye.

english_verse: So Jesus said to Peter and John, “Go and prepare the Passover meal for us to eat.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu tɩŋ Piita abee Gyɔɔŋ, a baa dɩ ba mʋ marɩ Dɩɩsɩ-parɩ-banɩɩ Kyɛbal hʋ kɩdiiliye dɩ ba di.

english_verse: But they asked, “Where do you want us to prepare it?”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Lee tɩyaŋ nɛ ɩ kɩ kyɛ dɩ á mʋ marɩ kɩdiiliye hʋ?”

english_verse: The Son of Man will die in the way that has been decided for him, but it will be terrible for the one who betrays him!”
translation_verse: Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye, ŋ sɩ sʋba anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ aa daga gɛɛ, amɛ nal hʋ aa sɩ kpa ŋ yallɩ, ʋ sɩ na tʋwara yʋga.”

english_verse: Then the apostles started arguing about who would ever do such a thing. An Argument about Greatness
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ piili kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ anɩɩ ba tɩyaŋ kɩbee rɛ sɩ yaa wɩɩ no? Yesu hatɩnna kaŋ nɩbalɩŋ wɩya kɩ kpa dɔmɔŋ

english_verse: he said to them, “I have very much wanted to eat this Passover meal with you before I suffer.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ŋ fa paalɩ kyo dɩ ŋ beel-ma di kɩna no rɛ, aŋka dɩ ŋ na na tʋwara.

english_verse: After the meal he took another cup of wine in his hands. Then he said, “This is my blood. It is poured out for you, and with it God makes his new agreement.
translation_verse: Ba aa dii kɩna hʋ teŋ, ʋ bɩl kpa gbaŋbiye hʋ abee sɩŋ a tɩya ba aŋ baa, “Gbaŋbiye no sɩŋ nɛ yaa nyʋʋfalɩɩ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa wee tɩya ma. Ŋ kyal hʋ aa gyaasa ma wɩya rɛ Wɩɩsɩ kpa we nyʋwa no.

english_verse: You have stayed with me in all my troubles.
translation_verse: Ŋ tʋwara nayɩ tɩyaŋ, mamaa rɛ yaa nala hʋ aa sɩna ŋ hal, a bɩ vɩya ŋ ta.

english_verse: So I will give you the right to rule as kings, just as my Father has given me the right to rule as a king.
translation_verse: Ŋ Kuwo tɩya ŋ ŋmanɩɩ rɛ dɩ ŋ yaa kuworibal. Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ŋ mɛ sɩ tɩya ma ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ma yaa kuworibala.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Simon, listen to me! Satan has demanded the right to test each one of you, as a farmer does when he separates wheat from the husks.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya Siimɔŋ Piita a baa, “Siimɔŋ, gyegili nɩɩ daha! Sɩtaanɩ ko sʋla ŋmanɩɩ rɛ dɩ ʋ magɩsɩ ma, a tʋwasɩ ma niweliye a lɩɩ ma nɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ, anɩɩ ba aa kyaarɩ mɩya, a lɩɩ miŋbii aŋ ka mɩŋgyɔrʋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Peter, I tell you that before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.” Moneybags, Traveling Bags, and Swords
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Piita, ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ɩ rɛ, gyibala ha bɩ sɩ wɩɩ gyɩnaŋ tebine no, aŋka dɩ ɩ tʋwa naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋto anɩɩ ɩ bɩ gyɩma ŋ.” Yesu basɩ molbiye abee takoobiye wɩya

english_verse: Do this because the Scriptures say, ‘He was considered a criminal.’ This was written about me, and it will soon come true.”
translation_verse: Ba saba Wɩɩsɩ teŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ a baa, ‘Ba kana ʋ pɛ wɩbɔŋyaala tɩyaŋ nɛ’. Wɩya no buloŋ, ŋ wɩya rɛ ba sabɩ gɛɛ. Ba buloŋ sɩ yaa ŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, dɩ wɩya hʋ buloŋ ba aa saba ŋ wɩya kɔnɩ su ba bʋʋsɩ.”

english_verse: He said to them, “Why are you asleep? Wake up and pray that you won't be tested.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu ko pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Ma kɔnɩ pɩŋ kɩ pɩŋ doo rɛ? Ma sii a kɩ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ, dɩ Sɩtaanɩ ta wuwo mʋrɩ ma.” Ba kaŋ Yesu Matiwu

english_verse: “Father, if you will, please don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: “Ŋ Kuwo, dɩ ʋ ko yaa ɩ kyɛrɩ, lɩɩ ŋ tʋwara no aa ko tɩyaŋ. Amɛ mɩyaŋ kɛ kyɛrɩ daa, leŋ dɩ ɩ kyɛrɩ yaa.”

english_verse: Jesus went out to the Mount of Olives, as he often did, and his disciples went with him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ, a mʋ gyɩŋ Olivi dogimo hʋ nyuu, anɩɩ ʋ aa kɩ yaa gɛɛ saŋa buloŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ mɛ sii tɩŋa ʋ hal mʋ.

english_verse: When they got there, he told them, “Pray that you won't be tested.”
translation_verse: Ba aa mʋʋ pele doŋ, ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma kɩ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ, dɩ Sɩtaanɩ ta wuwo mʋrɩ ma.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way before he knelt down and prayed,
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ teŋ, ʋ parɩ ʋ hatɩnna hʋ mʋ sɩya mʋhʋ, a tuu gbinni a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ, a baa,

english_verse: Jesus was in great pain and prayed so sincerely that his sweat fell to the ground like drops of blood.
translation_verse: Ʋ tɩya gyɩ kyogo rɛ kɩŋkaŋ buloŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ faasɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ abee sɩfɩyasɩ. Ʋ suwolii paalɩ kɩ tuu taŋha gɛɛ anɩɩ kyal.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, a crowd came up. It was led by Judas, one of the twelve apostles. He went over to Jesus and greeted him with a kiss.
translation_verse: Yesu ha gyɩ aa basɩ wɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, dɩ Yesu hatɩnna fi abee balɩya hʋ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Gyudasɩ rɛ gyɩ mʋ laa nɩygamaa sɩya gɛɛ ba kɩ ko ʋ lee. Ba aa ko pele, Gyudasɩ vala a mʋ guwori Yesu.

english_verse: One of the disciples even struck at the high priest's servant with his sword and cut off the servant's right ear.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ teŋ kyuri takoobii, a ŋmaŋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ dɩya yoŋ noduu dɩgɩŋ kyɔl.

english_verse: Jesus asked Judas, “Are you betraying the Son of Man with a kiss?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Gyudasɩ, ɩ kɔnɩ ko guwori ŋ nɛ dɩ ɩ kpa Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye yallɩ koo?”

english_verse: When Jesus' disciples saw what was about to happen, they asked, “Lord, should we attack them with a sword?”
translation_verse: Yesu hatɩnna hʋ fa aa we doŋ a na wɩɩ hʋ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ yaa, ɛɛ rɛ ba pɩyɛsɩ Yesu a baa, “Á Tɩɩna, á kyuri á takoobiye koo?”

english_verse: “Enough of that!” Jesus said. Then he touched the servant's ear and healed it.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu baa, “Ʋ maga rɛ. Ma leŋ.” Ʋ basɩ gɛɛ rɛ, aŋ dige baal hʋ dɩgɩŋ, ʋ mɩɩgɩ taasɩ.

english_verse: Jesus spoke to the chief priests, the temple police, and the leaders who had come to arrest him. He said, “Why do you come out with swords and clubs and treat me like a criminal?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu pɩyɛsɩ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee nala hʋ aa pɔ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ nɩhɩyasɩ abee ba bee nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ aa ko dɩ ba kana ʋ, a baa, “Ŋ yaa biibɔŋ nɛ, ma sii kol takoobiye abee daŋgulo a kɩ ko dɩ ma kana ŋ?

english_verse: Peter said, “Woman, I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita baa, “Haŋtolibiye, ŋ paalɩ bɩ gyɩma ʋ mɛ gba.”

english_verse: A little later someone else saw Peter and said, “You are one of them!” “No, I'm not!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Mʋhʋ sʋwaa dɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ mɛ na ʋ a baa, “Ɩ mɛ yaa ba kɩdɩgɩ rɛ.” Ɛɛ rɛ Piita bɩl baa, “Ŋ bagyʋwa, ŋ kɛ daa.”

english_verse: Jesus was arrested and led away to the house of the high priest, while Peter followed at a distance.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba kaŋ Yesu, a kana ʋ kɩ mʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ dɩya, ka Piita mɛ daasɩ kɩ tɩŋ ba hal.

english_verse: At daybreak the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses got together and brought Jesus before their council.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩgballɩya, Gyuuma bee nɩhɩyasɩ abee Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla buloŋ laŋŋɩ, aŋ leŋ ba kaŋ Yesu ko ba sɩya.

english_verse: Peter replied, “I don't know what you are talking about!” Right then, while Peter was still speaking, a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita bɩl basɩ tɩya ʋ mɛ a baa, “Ŋ bagyʋwa, ŋ paalɩ bɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa basɩ tɩyaŋ.” Piita aa yaŋ basɩ gɛɛ, ʋ nyʋwa ha bɩ tele taŋha, dɩ gyibal wiye.

english_verse: The Lord turned and looked at Peter. And Peter remembered that the Lord had said, “Before a rooster crows tomorrow morning, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ á Tɩɩna Yesu galdaasɩ, a deŋ Piita sɩya tɩyaŋ. Doŋ nɛ ʋ liisi wɩɩ hʋ Yesu fa aa basɩ tɩya ʋ anɩɩ ʋ sɩ tʋwa naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋto a baa dɩ ʋ bɩ gyɩma ʋ, ka dɩ gyibal na wii.

english_verse: The men who were guarding Jesus made fun of him and beat him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala hʋ aa pɔ Yesu kɩ huuri ʋ, aŋ kɩ ŋmaa ʋ.

english_verse: They kept on insulting Jesus in many other ways. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ basɩ wɩya mɛ rɛ yʋga a tʋʋsɩ ʋ. Ba kaŋ Yesu mʋ bee-hɩyarɩ sɩya Matiwu

english_verse: And if I asked you a question, you wouldn't answer.
translation_verse: Dɩ mɩyaŋ nɛ yaŋ bɩl pɩyɛsɩ ma wɩɩ mɛ, ma bɩ sɩ lɩɩ ʋ sɩɩ tɩya ŋ.

english_verse: But from now on, the Son of Man will be seated at the right side of God All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: Amɛ a lɩɩ nyɛ a kaŋ kɩ mʋ, Mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye sɩ hɔŋ Wɩɩbal hʋ noduu logiŋ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: They replied, “Why do we need more witnesses? He said it himself!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala hʋ baa, “Á bɩl bee kyɛ daŋsɩya tɩɩna buloŋ. Á tɩɩ mɛ nɩɩ wɩɩ hʋ ʋ tɩɩ nyʋwa tɩyaŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: Herod and his soldiers made fun of Jesus and insulted him. They put a fine robe on him and sent him back to Pilate.
translation_verse: Hɛrɔtɩ bee ʋ laalyuwolo mɛ yaa Yesu sɩya weliŋ, aŋ leŋ ba kpa gawil kɩdɩgɩ laalɩ ʋ, aŋ mɩɩgɩ kana ʋ mʋ Pilato lee.

english_verse: He told them, “You brought Jesus to me and said he was a troublemaker. But I have questioned him here in front of you, and I have not found him guilty of anything that you say he has done.
translation_verse: “Ma kaŋ baal no ko ŋ lee rɛ, a basɩ tɩya ŋ anɩɩ ʋ aa mʋrɩ nala rɛ. Ŋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ wɩya rɛ ma buloŋ sɩɩ tɩyaŋ. Wɩya hʋ buloŋ ma aa baa dɩ ʋ yaa kyogi, ŋ kɛ bɩ naa ʋ wɩkyogii ba tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: But they all kept on saying, “He has been teaching and causing trouble all over Judea. He started in Galilee and has now come all the way here.” Jesus Is Brought before Herod
translation_verse: Amɛ nala hʋ ha hara a baa, “Ʋ daga nala Gyudɩya paalʋʋ buloŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ a leŋ ba kɩ laa taawɛɛ. Galili paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ piili wɩɩ no, aŋ sii kpa ko daha mɛ nyɛ.” Ba kaŋ Yesu mʋ Hɛrɔtɩ sɩya

english_verse: But the whole crowd shouted, “Kill Jesus! Give us Barabbas!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala hʋ buloŋ kyɩɩrɩ a baa, “Kpʋ Yesu aŋ lɩɩ Barabaasɩ tɩya ma.”

english_verse: Pilate asked Jesus, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words,” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pilato pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Ɩ rɛ yaa Gyuuma kuwori hʋ koo?” Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Na ɛɛ rɛ ɩ basɩ gɛɛ.”

english_verse: After Pilate learned that Jesus came from the region ruled by Herod, he sent him to Herod, who was in Jerusalem at that time.
translation_verse: Pilato aa ko gyɩma anɩɩ Galili paalʋʋ rɛ Yesu lɩɩ, ʋ leŋ ba kana ʋ mʋ Hɛrɔtɩ lee, beewɩya Hɛrɔtɩ rɛ gyɩ aa deŋ doŋ paalʋʋ. Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ Hɛrɔtɩ gyɩ we Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: For a long time Herod had wanted to see Jesus and was very happy because he finally had this chance. He had heard many things about Jesus and hoped to see him work a miracle.
translation_verse: Hɛrɔtɩ teŋ buloŋ gyɩ fɩyɛla rɛ weliŋ ʋ aa naa Yesu, beewɩya ʋ gyɩ nɩɩ ʋ wɩya rɛ faafaa buloŋ, a kɩ kyɛ dɩ ʋ na ʋ. Ʋ fa aa kyɛ dɩ Yesu yaa wɩmagɩl lɛ dɩ ʋ na.

english_verse: Herod asked him a lot of questions, but Jesus did not answer.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Hɛrɔtɩ pɩyɛsɩ Yesu wɩya yʋga, amɛ ʋ gyɩ bɩ laa wɩɩ buloŋ basɩ.

english_verse: Then the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses stood up and accused him of all kinds of bad things.
translation_verse: Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ sii tarɩsɩ mʋ Yesu tɩyaŋ, a daga wɩya yʋga ba aa bɩɩna anɩɩ ʋ yaa kyogi.

english_verse: That same day Herod and Pilate became friends, even though they had been enemies before this. The Death Sentence (Matthew
translation_verse: Hɛrɔtɩ bee Pilato fa bee na dɔmɔŋ sɩɩ. Haŋ kyɛɛ hʋ rɛ ba mɩɩgɩ bɩrɩmɩ kyaŋsɩ. Ba kpaa kyogisi tɩya Yesu aŋ baa dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ Matiwu

english_verse: As Jesus was being led away, some soldiers grabbed hold of a man named Simon who was from Cyrene. He was coming in from the fields, but they put the cross on him and made him carry it behind Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ laalyuwolo hʋ kaŋ Yesu lɩɩ dɩ ba kana ʋ mʋ kpaasɩ mal daagarɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba aa mʋʋ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ba kyeŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ aa lɩɩ Sariini ba kɩ yɩrɩ Siimɔŋ, dɩ ʋ lɩɩ baga a ko kɩ gyʋʋ Gyerusalɛm. Ɛɛ rɛ ba kana ʋ, a kpa daagarɩɩ hʋ tɩya ʋ ʋ baasɩ, a kɩ tɩŋ Yesu hal.

english_verse: Pilate wanted to set Jesus free, so he spoke again to the crowds.
translation_verse: Pilato fa aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ kpa Yesu ta rɛ, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ bɩl marɩ basɩ wɩya tɩya nɩgyamaa hʋ.

english_verse: Pilate spoke to them a third time, “But what crime has he done? I have not found him guilty of anything for which he should be put to death. I will have him beaten with a whip and set free.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pilato bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Bekiŋ wɩɩ rɛ baal no yaa kyogi? Ŋ kɛ bɩ naa wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa yaa kyogi a maga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ. Ɛɛwɩya, ŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ba vɩɩrɩ ʋ, aŋka dɩ ŋ kpa ʋ ta.”

english_verse: He freed the man who was in jail for rioting and murder, because he was the one the crowd wanted to be set free. Then Pilate handed Jesus over for them to do what they wanted with him. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pilato leŋ ba lɩɩ Barabaasɩ ta. Baal hʋ ba gyɩ aa kaŋ tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ akuu taawɛɛ aa siye bee hʋ tɩyaŋ ba kpʋ nala wɩya. Aŋ kpa Yesu mɛ tɩya ba dɩ ba kaŋ mʋ yaa gɛɛ hʋ buloŋ ba aa sɩ yaa. Ba kpaasɩ Yesu mal daagarɩɩ tɩyaŋ Matiwu

english_verse: If this can happen when the wood is green, what do you think will happen when it is dry?
translation_verse: Dɩ daasɔnɔ rɛ ko kɩ di diŋ, aŋnɛ rɛ bɩl sɩ kɩ basɩ daahɩlɩya mɛ wɩya?”

english_verse: Around noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until the middle of the afternoon.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ aa ko maga nyutʋtʋʋ saŋa gɛɛ, wɩɩsɩ pirigi nyʋgɩsɩ, ka lee buloŋ bɩl gɛɛ timm, a mʋ pele dɩdaana kerifi boto, ka lee bɩl na kyaanɩ.

english_verse: Two criminals were led out to be put to death with Jesus.
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ aa kaŋ Yesu kɩ mʋ dɩ ba kpʋ gɛɛ, wɩbɔŋyaala balɩya mɛ rɛ ba gyɩ kaŋ pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “Father, forgive these people! They don't know what they're doing.” While the crowd stood there watching Jesus, the soldiers gambled for his clothes. The leaders insulted him by saying, “He saved others. Now he should save himself, if he really is God's chosen Messiah!”
translation_verse: Nala badɔmɔŋ mɛ gyɩ sɩŋ doŋ a kɩ deŋ, aŋka Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ kɩ yaa ʋ sɩya, a baa, “Na ʋ baa ʋ rɛ yaa Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya, a laa nala kɩ ta, ma leŋ dɩ ʋ laa ʋ tɩɩ ta dɩ á na.”

english_verse: The soldiers made fun of Jesus and brought him some wine.
translation_verse: Laalyuwolo hʋ mɛ kɩ yaa ʋ sɩya, a yaa sɩkyaakyaa mʋ we ʋ nyʋwa tɩyaŋ dɩ ʋ nyʋwa,

english_verse: They said, “If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself!”
translation_verse: aŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Dɩ ɩ rɛ kɔnɩ yaa Gyuuma kuwori kɛ, laa ɩ tɩɩ ta dɩ á na.”

english_verse: But the other criminal told the first one off, “Don't you fear God? Aren't you getting the same punishment as this man?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ dɔŋtɩɩna wɩbɔŋyaal hʋ nɔsɩ ʋ a baa, “Ɩ bee fá Wɩɩsɩ koo? Á beel-ʋ buloŋ we tʋwarɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise that today you will be with me in paradise.” The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ɩ, ŋ beel-ɩ sɩ we wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ gyɩnaŋ.” Yesu sʋba Matiwu

english_verse: The sun stopped shining, and the curtain in the temple split down the middle.
translation_verse: Patɩɩsa hʋ fa aa bori Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ kɩyɛsɩ balɩya.

english_verse: A crowd had gathered to see the terrible sight. Then after they had seen it, they felt brokenhearted and went home.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ fa aa laŋŋa doŋ aa naa wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa, ba mɩɩgɩ mʋ dɩɩsɩ abee tɩkyogi.

english_verse: All of Jesus' close friends and the women who had come with him from Galilee stood at a distance and watched. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ fa aa gyɩŋ Yesu abee haana hʋ buloŋ fa aa tɩŋ ʋ hal a lɩɩ Galili ko, ba sɩŋ libolii rɛ aŋ kɩ deŋ wɩya hʋ buloŋ aa yaa. Ba hogo Yesu Matiwu

english_verse: There was a man named Joseph, who was from Arimathea in Judea. Joseph was a good and honest man, and he was eager for God's kingdom to come. He was also a member of the council, but he did not agree with what they had decided.
translation_verse: Gyuuma baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Gyosɛfʋ, a lɩɩ Arimatiya aa we Gyudɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ. Ʋ fa yaa nɩtɩɩ rɛ weliŋ, ka nala fa kɩ tɩya ʋ gyɩrɩma weliŋ. Ʋ fa aa gyegili Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ koyi rɛ, a fa yaa nala hʋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya kɩdɩgɩ. Amɛ saŋa hʋ ba fa aa vʋʋlɩ Yesu kpʋyɩ wɩya, ʋ kɛ fa bɩ sɩŋ ba kɩŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: He took the body down from the cross and wrapped it in fine cloth. Then he put it in a tomb that had been cut out of solid rock and had never been used.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ mʋ lallɩ Yesu daagarɩɩ hʋ nyuu tɩyaŋ, a we ʋ liŋe, aŋ kpa ʋ mʋ hogo bʋbʋwa kɩdɩgɩ ba aa lurigo tɩyaŋ. Ba ha gyɩ bɩ maakyiye hogo nal buloŋ doŋ.

english_verse: The women who had come with Jesus from Galilee followed Joseph and watched how Jesus' body was placed in the tomb.
translation_verse: Haana hʋ gyɩ aa tɩŋ Yesu hal a lɩɩ Galili paalʋʋ ko sii tɩŋ Gyosɛfʋ hal a mʋ dɩ ba na ɛɛ ba aa hogo Yesu bʋbʋwa hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James, and some other women were the ones who had gone to the tomb. When they returned, they told the eleven apostles and the others what had happened.
translation_verse: Haana hʋ gyɩ aa mʋʋ bil hʋ lee rɛ nyɛ: Mɛɛrɩ Magidalina, Gyuwana abee Gyemsi nɩɩna Mɛɛrɩ. Ba bee ba hadɔŋtɩŋsɩ hʋ gyɩ aa mʋʋ na wɩya hʋ rɛ gyɩ ko basɩ wɩya no tɩya Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ.

english_verse: When they found the stone rolled away from the entrance,
translation_verse: Ba mʋwa dɩ bʋyɩ hʋ ba fa aa kpaa tɔ bʋbʋwa hʋ nyʋwa suro.

english_verse: they went in. But they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba mʋ gyʋʋ bʋbʋwa hʋ, amɛ ba bɩ naa á Tɩɩna Yesu teŋbii.

english_verse: The women were afraid and bowed to the ground. But the men said, “Why are you looking in the place of the dead for someone who is alive?
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ kambɩŋ kaŋ haana hʋ, ba kaŋ ba nyuni gʋra gʋra. Ɛɛ rɛ baala hʋ pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ ma deŋ kɩ kyɛ nal hʋ aa weye nɩsʋʋnɩ tɩyaŋ?

english_verse: ‘The Son of Man will be handed over to sinners who will nail him to a cross. But three days later he will rise to life.’ ”
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ basɩ tɩya ma rɛ anɩɩ ba sɩ kpa ʋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye we wɩbɔŋyaala nosi tɩyaŋ dɩ ba kpaasɩ ʋ mal daagarɩɩ tɩyaŋ, dɩ ʋ sʋba, dɩ amɛ dɩ ʋ sɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ ʋ kyɛtoo kyɛɛ.”

english_verse: That same day two of Jesus' disciples were going to the village of Emmaus, which was about eleven kilometers from Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Haŋ kyɛɛ hʋ tɩɩ rɛ Yesu hatɩnna balɩya sii kɩ mʋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Imayosi. Imayosi bee Gyerusalɛm fa yaa anɩɩ meeldaasɩ bapɛ rɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What were you talking about as you walked along?” The two of them stood there looking sad and gloomy.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Bekine wɩya rɛ ma vala kɩ basɩ gɛɛ?” Ɛɛ rɛ ba mɩɩgɩ sɩŋ, ba tɩsɩ buloŋ kyogi.

english_verse: We had hoped that he would be the one to set Israel free! But it has already been three days since all this happened.
translation_verse: Amɛ á fa aa yɩyɛlɩ anɩɩ ʋ rɛ yaa nal hʋ aa ko dɩ ʋ laa Iziral tɩmma ta. Ka ba aa kpʋ ʋ, ʋ kyɛyɛ boto rɛ yaa gyɩnaŋ.

english_verse: Some men from our group went to the tomb and found it just as the women had said. But they didn't see Jesus either.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ á badɔmɔŋ mɛ sii mʋ bʋwa hʋ lee a na anɩɩ wɩɩ hʋ haana hʋ aa basa hʋ yaa wɩtɩɩ. Amɛ ba mɛ gyɩ bɩ naa Yesu.”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the two disciples, “Why can't you understand? How can you be so slow to believe all that the prophets said?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma yaa bambugo rɛ kɩŋkaŋ, a bɩ wuwo dɩ ma laa wɩya hʋ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala aa basa mɛ di.

english_verse: Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?”
translation_verse: Ma bɩ gyɩma anɩɩ ʋ maga dɩ Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya, na tʋwara rɛ aŋ na di ʋ gaŋdaa-feŋ Wɩɩsɩ lee tɩyaŋ koo?”

english_verse: They begged him, “Stay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.” So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba kana ʋ biŋ, aŋ baa dɩ lee bile rɛ, dɩ ʋ pɩŋ sɩya gballɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba beel-ʋ buloŋ kpa gyʋʋ dɩya.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down to eat, he took some bread. He blessed it and broke it. Then he gave it to them.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba hɔŋ dɩ ba di kɩna. Nyɛ rɛ Yesu kpa paanʋʋ, a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ aŋ kaŋ lɔgɔ lɔgɔ tɩya ba.

english_verse: They said to each other, “When he talked with us along the road and explained the Scriptures to us, didn't it warm our hearts?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba basɩ tɩya dɔmɔŋ a baa, “Saŋa hʋ ʋ fa aa basɩ wɩya kɩ tɩya ma aŋ daga Wɩɩsɩ teŋ wɩya hʋ memiye kɩ tɩya ma ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, á tenni fa fɩyɛla rɛ kɩŋkaŋ buloŋ.”

english_verse: So they got up and returned to Jerusalem. The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii lagɩ lagɩ, a mɩɩgɩ mʋ Gyerusalɛm, a na Yesu kpambɩsɩ fi abee kɩdɩgɩ hʋ, abee nɩkaalɩya hʋ buloŋ fa aa tɩŋ Yesu hal dɩ ba laŋŋa lɩdɩgɩ.

english_verse: And they learned from the group that the Lord was really alive and had appeared to Peter.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ fi abee kɩdɩgɩ hʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Á Tɩɩna Yesu kɔnɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, beewɩya, ʋ kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ ko daga Siimɔŋ nɛ!”

english_verse: Then the disciples from Emmaus told what happened on the road and how they knew he was the Lord when he broke the bread. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ baala hʋ balɩya mɛ basɩ wɩɩ hʋ aa yaa ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, abee gɛɛ ba aa ko gyɩma anɩɩ á Tɩɩna Yesu rɛ. Ba basɩ tɩya ba rɛ anɩɩ saŋa hʋ ʋ aa kaŋ paanʋʋ lɔgɔ a tɩya ba hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba gyɩma anɩɩ ʋ rɛ. Yesu kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ ko daga ʋ hatɩnna Matiwu

english_verse: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ kambɩŋ kaŋ ba, beewɩya ba kɛ fa bɩɩna anɩɩ nɩdʋma rɛ ba na gɛɛ.

english_verse: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
translation_verse: Ma deŋ ŋ nosi bee ŋ naasɩ na, ma sɩ gyɩma anɩɩ ŋ tɩɩ rɛ. Ma dige ŋ na weliŋ, ma sɩ gyɩma anɩɩ ŋ tɩɩ rɛ kɔnɩ, beewɩya nɩdʋma bɩ kaŋ hogo bee naŋpʋna anɩɩ ma aa naa dɩ ŋ kana nyɛ.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ ko teŋ, ʋ kaŋ ʋ nosi abee ʋ naasɩ buloŋ a daga ba.

english_verse: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
translation_verse: Wɩɩ no fa yaa ba wɩkperii rɛ, a bɩl yaa ba teŋfɩyɛlʋʋ mɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Amɛ ba ha fa bɩ laa ʋ di. Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu mɩɩgɩ pɩyɛsɩ ba a baa, “Ma kaŋ kɩdiiliye daha rɛ?”

english_verse: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba kpa fuwonaŋ-kyabɩɩ, a tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: He took it and ate it as they watched.
translation_verse: Ʋ laa kyaŋ ba sɩɩ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: He told them: The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death.
translation_verse: Aŋ bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Nyɛ rɛ ba sabɩ biŋ: Ʋ maga dɩ Krisita hʋ, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya na tʋwara rɛ, a sʋba aŋ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ ʋ kyɛtoo kyɛɛ.”

english_verse: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
