english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ mɛ pirigi lɩɩ tele ʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ doŋ, a sʋba. Bapʋwasɩya hʋ aa kpaa ʋ bala mʋ hogo ko kɩ gyʋʋ dɩ ʋ mɛ sʋba. Ɛɛ rɛ ba kpa ʋ mɛ a kaŋ lɩɩ hogo kpala ʋ bala hʋ.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di, aŋ aa ko kɩ laŋŋɩ, abee nala hʋ buloŋ mɛ gyɩ aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, kambɩŋ gyɩ gyʋʋ ba rɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Wɩkperiye bee wɩmagɩla

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ gyɩ yaa wɩmagɩla abee wɩkperiye rɛ yʋga nala tɩyaŋ. Nala hʋ buloŋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di, ba buloŋ nɛ gyɩ aa kɩ laŋŋɩ dɔmɔŋ abee nyʋʋdɩgɩ. Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ gyambarɩŋ ba aa yɩrɩ Solomɔn Gyambarɩŋ, ʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba gyɩ aa kɩ laŋŋɩ.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Akuu wɩya no buloŋ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ aa yaa wɩya, nala aa paa ba wɩɩla tɩmma bee ba kɩpɩnɩya rɛ, a ko lɩɩ biŋ ŋmaŋsɩ nyʋʋsɩ tɩyaŋ. Ba aa yaa gɛɛ rɛ dɩ Piita rɛ ko kɩ baŋ, dɩ ʋ daaliiŋ gyʋʋ ba tɩyaŋ. Beewɩya dɩ Piita daaliiŋ mɛ gba ko gyʋʋ ba tɩyaŋ, ba aa na laaŋfɩya rɛ.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Nɩgyamaa mɛ gyɩ lɩɩ tɔnɩ hʋ aa kpaga Gyerusalɛm nɛ, aa kaŋ wɩɩla tɩmma abee nala hʋ gyɩŋbɔmɔ aa kana, a kaŋ ko ba lee ba buloŋ na laaŋfɩya. Ba dɔgɩsɩ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ

english_verse: The high priest and all the other Sadducees who were with him became jealous.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ abee ʋ hatɩnna hʋ aa we Sadusiima paati hʋ tɩyaŋ, ba sii kpa baaŋ abee tɩhɔllɩ kyeŋ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ.

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba kasɩ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ a mʋ tɔ dɩyaŋ.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Amɛ haŋ kyɛɛ hʋ tɩɩ tebine rɛ malɩka kɩdɩgɩ ko kaŋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ dimbee suri, aŋ leŋ ba lɩɩ. Ka ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa,

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: “Ma mʋ gyʋʋ sɩŋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ, a basɩ wɩɩkyʋwal-ŋmaŋfalɩɩ no wɩya a tɩya nala.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ tɩŋa ʋ nyʋwa hʋ. Ʋ sɩgballɩnyʋwa, ba sii mʋ gyʋʋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ a piili kɩ daga nala. Nyɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ abee ʋ hatɩnna sii yɩrɩ Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ abee nala hʋ buloŋ dɩ ba ko laŋŋɩ vʋʋlɩ. Ka ba tɩma dɩ ba mʋ lɩɩ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ a kaŋ ba ko.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Laalyuwolo hʋ aa mʋwa ba bɩ naa Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ba mɩɩgɩ ko ba nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ lee a baa,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “Á aa mʋʋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ, nal buloŋ bɩ suro boro hʋ, ka nala hʋ aa pɔ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ mɛ sɩŋ boro hʋ nyʋwa. Amɛ á aa mʋʋ suri, á bɩ naa Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ.”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ aa pɔ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ nɩhɩyawʋ abee Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, ʋ yaa wɩkperii rɛ kɩŋkaŋ a tɩya ba. Ba bɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ aa puu ba.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, dɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ ko gyʋʋ gɛɛ, a basɩ tɩya ba anɩɩ ba naa nala hʋ ba aa kasa tɔ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, dɩ ba ko lɩɩ sɩŋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ a kɩ daga nala.

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ abee ʋ noŋ nala sii lɩɩ a mʋ kasɩ ba a mɩɩgɩ kaŋ ko. Amɛ ba gyɩ bɩ kpaa dee a kasɩ ba, beewɩya kambɩŋ fa kaŋ ba rɛ anɩɩ nala hʋ aa we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ sɩ paa bʋwa yaga ba.

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: “Á basɩ tɩya ma rɛ daha abee sɩfɩyasɩ anɩɩ ma ta bɩl ko maakyiye basɩ Yesu wɩya tɩya nal buloŋ, koo a yɩrɩ ʋ feŋ mɛ gba. Amɛ ma deŋ na, ma vɩya aŋ kaŋ wɩɩ hʋ gɔllɩ gyaasɩ Gyerusalɛm buloŋ nɛ, a kɩ kyɛ dɩ ma kpa baal no sʋʋ wɩya kyʋgɩ ma.”

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Á naabaala Wɩɩsɩ hʋ rɛ kyisi Yesu sʋʋ tɩyaŋ. Nal hʋ ma aa kaŋ kpaasɩ mal daagarɩɩ hʋ nyuu tɩyaŋ ʋ sʋba hʋ.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ kyisi ʋ rɛ, ʋ sii gyɩŋ hɔŋ ʋ noduu logiŋ, dɩ ʋ yaa sɩlaal abee laataal, a tɩya Iziral tɩmma ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ba bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ ba wɩbɔmɔ yayɩ tɩyaŋ, ka dɩ Wɩɩsɩ kpa ba wɩbɔmɔ kyɛ ba.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Wɩya no buloŋ á aa basɩ nyɛ, á bee Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ ʋ aa kpaa we nala hʋ aa tɩŋa ʋ nyʋwa, buloŋ yaa ba daŋsɩya rɛ.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Nala no aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, nyɛ rɛ ba na baaŋ kɩŋkaŋ aŋ kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba kpʋ ba.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Amɛ ba kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ, a lɩɩ Farasiima paati hʋ tɩyaŋ, ba kɩ yɩrɩ Gamaliyɛl. Ʋ fa yaa Wɩɩsɩ teŋ dɩdagɩl lɛ, nala fa kɩ tɩya gyɩrɩma weliŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ sii sɩŋ aŋ baa, “Ma leŋ dɩ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ lɩɩ lɩgyalɩɩ mʋhʋ.”

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yaŋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Mamaa Iziral tɩmma, ma fɩyɛlɩ ma sɩya abee wɩya hʋ ma aa kyɛ dɩ ma yaa nala no.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, wɩɩ no tɩyaŋ, ŋ kɛ aa naa wɩɩ rɛ nyɛ, ma ta yaa nala no wɩɩ buloŋ. Ma kɛ kpa ba ta. Dɩ ba wɩyaalɩya no ko lɩɩ nihuwobiŋ dee tɩyaŋ, tʋma hʋ sɩ nyʋgɩsɩ ba tɩɩ.

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: Amɛ, dɩ ʋ ko yaa Wɩɩsɩ tʋma, ma bɩ sɩ wuwo kaŋ tʋma no kyogi. Dɩ mamaa rɛ paalɩ kɩ kyɛ ma kyogi ba mɛ, ma sɩ na anɩɩ ma bee Wɩɩsɩ sɩ mɩɩgɩ kaŋ wɩɩ.”

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba gyegili nɩɩ baal hʋ nyʋwa hʋ. Doŋ nɛ ba yɩrɩ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ ba ko gyʋʋ, ba leŋ ba vɩɩrɩ ba, aŋ basɩ tɩya ba anɩɩ ba ta bɩl ko basɩ wɩya Yesu feŋ tɩyaŋ, aŋ kpa ba ta.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Kyɛɛ buloŋ ba aa mʋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ rɛ, abee nala dɩɩsɩ, a kɩ basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya aŋ kɩ daga nala Wɩɩsɩ wɩweliye hʋ a mʋ kɩ tile á tɩɩna Yesu Krisita tɩyaŋ, nal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya dɩ ʋ laa dʋnɩya nala ta.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ dɩ nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di gyɩ marɩ wala kɩ pɛ rɛ. Nyɛ rɛ ba tɩyaŋ Gyuuma hʋ aa basɩ Giriki taal abee Gyuuma hʋ aa basɩ Hiiburu taal piili kɩ kaa dɔmɔŋ. Beewɩya Gyuuma hʋ aa basɩ Giriki taal gyɩ baa dɩ Hiiburu tɩmma hʋ aa vɩya ba lohaana kɩ ta rɛ ba kɩna kpayɩ tɩyaŋ kyɛɛ buloŋ.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Yesu Kpambɩsɩ hʋ fi abee balɩya yɩrɩ nala hʋ buloŋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di a ko laŋŋɩ, a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ʋ bɩ maga dɩ á kɩ deŋ molbiye koo kɩdiiliye wɩkanɩya, aŋ yeŋŋi Wɩɩsɩ wɩya basɩɩ ta.

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ŋ naabalɩya, ma lɩɩ nala bapɛ ma tɩyaŋ. Ma lɩɩ nala hʋ aa kaŋ wɩgyʋŋ, nala kɩ tɩya ba gyɩrɩma, ka Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ mɛ we ba tɩyaŋ. Ma lɩɩ ba, dɩ á kpa á molbiye wɩkanɩya we ba nosi tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: Ka dɩ á mɛ kpa á nyuu buloŋ we Wɩɩsɩ kyʋwalɩɩ abee Wɩɩsɩ wɩya basɩɩ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba kaŋ nala no bapɛ ba aa lɩya a mʋ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ sɩya. Ba kpa ba nosi dɔbɔ ba nyuni tɩyaŋ, aŋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ba.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ka Wɩɩsɩ wɩbii hʋ marɩ pɩsa mʋ lee buloŋ. Nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di marɩ yʋga kɩ pɛ weliŋ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ. Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala mɛ gyɩ yʋga rɛ weliŋ, a ko laa Yesu wɩya di. Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ leŋ ba kaŋ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ noŋ gyɩ dɔbɔ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ʋ dee mɛ we ʋ tɩyaŋ yʋga, ʋ wuwo kɩ yaa wɩkperiye abee wɩmagɩla nala tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Amɛ ba fa bɩ wuwo basɩ wɩya lo ʋ wɩɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, beewɩya Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ fa we ʋ lee rɛ, ka ʋ kaŋ wɩgyʋŋ mɛ ʋ wɩbasɩɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba lʋwɔlɩ we nala badɔmɔŋ nyuu dɩ ba basɩ baa ba nɩɩ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ basɩ wɩya kɩ kyogi Moosi abee Wɩɩsɩ feŋ nɛ.

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Nala no aa basɩ wɩya no, ɛɛ rɛ bee hʋ nala abee ba nɩhɩyasɩ abee Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ buloŋ baaŋ sii Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ nyuu tɩyaŋ kɩŋkaŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ba kana ʋ mʋ hɩyarɩ sɩya.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Aŋ kaŋ nala mɛ ko dɩ ba ko nyɩya wɩya tɩya ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ nala nyɩya wɩya tɩya ʋ a baa, “Saŋa buloŋ, baal no aa basɩ wɩya rɛ a kɩ kyogi Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩwelii hʋ á aa saa, abee Wɩɩsɩ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ buloŋ ʋ aa leŋ Moosi sabɩ biŋ dɩ á kɩ tɩŋa.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Á nɩya dɩ ʋ baa Yesu no aa lɩɩ Nazarɛti sɩ yige Wɩɩsɩ dɩya no lo, aŋ kaŋ wɩya hʋ Moosi fa aa daga a naabaala dɩ ba kɩ tɩŋa hʋ mɛ a bɩrɩmɩ kyige.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ fa aa hɔŋ doŋ kuwoni ba sɩya pɔ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ tɩyaŋ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba na dɩ ʋ sɩya kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ malɩka sɩya.

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ baa, “Ŋ kuwoma abee ŋ naabalɩya, ma gyegili nɩɩ daha. Wɩɩbal hʋ, feŋbal hʋ tɩɩna gyɩ kaŋ ʋ tɩɩ ko daga á naabaa Abɩraham nɛ dɩ ʋ ha gyɩ hɔŋ Mesopotemiya taŋha tɩyaŋ. Haŋ saŋa hʋ dɩ ʋ ha gyɩ bɩ mʋʋ Haraŋ paalʋʋ.

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ sii leŋ ʋ dɩya nala buloŋ, aŋ lɩɩ ʋ paalʋʋ mɛ tɩyaŋ, a mʋ paalʋʋ hʋ ʋ aa sɩ daga ʋ.

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ sii lɩɩ Kaldɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a mʋ hɔŋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Haraŋ tɩyaŋ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ kuwo sʋba. Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ bɩl leŋ ʋ sii doŋ, a ko hɔŋ paalʋʋ no tɩyaŋ ma aa hɔnɔ gyɩnaŋ.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ, Wɩɩsɩ fa bɩ keri taŋha hʋ mʋhʋ mɛ a tɩya Abɩraham a pel naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa kɩdɩgɩ mɛ, dɩ ʋ tɩɩ kana. Amɛ ʋ gyɩ wee nyʋwa tɩya ʋ rɛ anɩɩ ʋ sɩ kpa paalʋʋ no tɩya ʋ bee ʋ doho nala. Saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa basɩ gɛɛ dɩ Abɩraham ha bɩ lʋla.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ, ‘Ɩ doho nala sɩ yaa nɩhʋwala paalʋʋ kɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ a yaa yosi. Ba sɩ dɔgɩsɩ ba kɩŋkaŋ bɩsɩ kɔɔsɩbanaa.

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Amɛ ŋ sɩ dɔgɩsɩ haŋ paalʋʋ hʋ nala aa kaŋ ba bɩrɩmɩ yosi ba tʋma kɩ tɩya ba hʋ. Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ, ba sɩ lɩɩ doŋ tɩyaŋ, a bɩl ko daha, a kɩ kyʋwalɩ mɩyaŋ Wɩɩsɩ.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ kpa ʋ nyʋwa tɩya Abɩraham. Ʋ nyʋwa hʋ magɩl lɛ yaa Abɩraham bee ʋ doho nala aa keri ba pene. Nyɛ rɛ Abɩraham lʋl Azɩkɩ. Ʋ kyɛyɛ kyori kyɛɛ, Abɩraham keri ʋ peŋ. Azɩkɩ mɛ sii lʋl Gyeekɔbɩ. Gyeekɔbɩ mɛ lʋl biisi fi abee balɩya. Ba aa rɛ ko yaa á naabaala.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: Ba tɩyaŋ kɩdɩgɩ feŋ nɛ fa yaa Gyosɛfʋ. Nyɛ rɛ nɩkaalɩya hʋ gyɩ haasɩ ʋ, ba tɩsɩ kɩ hɔllɩ ʋ. Ba kuu gɛɛ wɩya a kana ʋ mʋ yallɩ tɩya baala badɔmɔŋ. Ba mɛ kana ʋ mʋ Igyipiti, a mʋ yallɩ tɩya baal kɩdɩgɩ, ʋ yaa yoŋ. Amɛ Wɩɩsɩ ha gyɩ aa pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ weliŋ,

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: a lɩɩ ʋ tʋwara buloŋ tɩyaŋ ʋ gyɩ aa gyʋwa. Ʋ fa aa mʋʋ Igyipiti Kuwori Faaro sɩya, Wɩɩsɩ dee tɩyaŋ, ʋ wuwo kaŋ wɩgyʋŋ nɛ abee gyogiweliye. Ɛɛ rɛ Kuwori Faaro lɩɩ ʋ, ʋ yaa ʋ paalʋʋ nɩhɩyawʋ, a kɩ deŋ ʋ paalʋʋ abee ʋ dɩya buloŋ.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ losiŋbɔŋ kɩdɩgɩ gyɩ ko gyʋʋ Igyipiti paalʋʋ abee Keenaŋ paalʋʋ buloŋ, nala kɩ na sikii kɩŋkaŋ, á naabaala hʋ gyɩ kyɛ kɩdiilii buloŋ lɔlɔ dɩ ba di.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Gyeekɔbɩ nɩɩ anɩɩ kɩdiiliye we Igyipiti paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ʋ tɩma ʋ balɩya dɩ ba mʋ yɔbɔ ko. Ɛɛ rɛ ba mʋ yɔbɔ ko.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ bee ʋ balɩya buloŋ sʋba.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Ba mɩɩgɩ kaŋ ba mʋ Keenaŋ paalʋʋ, a hogi ba Sɛkɛm tɩyaŋ. Bʋbʋʋbal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ Abɩraham fa yɔbɔ Hamɔ nala lee. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba hogi ba.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ wee nyʋwa rɛ dɩ ʋ sɩ yaa wɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ tɩya Abɩraham. Ʋ gyɩ ko ka mʋhʋ rɛ ka dɩ Wɩɩsɩ yaa wɩɩ no. Haŋ saŋa hʋ dɩ Gyeekɔbɩ doho nala mɛ fa wala yʋga rɛ weliŋ Igyipiti paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ kɩ mʋrɩ á naabaala hʋ, a kɩ dɔgɩsɩ ba kɩŋkaŋ, aŋ fɩla ba dɩ ba paa ba biifɩllɩ lɩɩ ta dɩ ba sʋba.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: Haŋ saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba gyɩ lʋl Moosi. Ʋ fa yaa biiwelii nyɛ rɛ. Ɛɛ wɩya, ba kpa ʋ faŋa ʋ kuwo dɩya tɩyaŋ peŋsi boto, aŋ na kpa ʋ lɩɩ ta.

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: Ba aa kpa ʋ lɩɩ ta gɛɛ, Kuwori Faaro tɩɩ toluu ko kpa ʋ a dɩyɛsɩ ʋ anɩɩ ʋ biilʋlɩɩ.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Ba daga ʋ Igyipiti tɩmma wɩgyʋŋ buloŋ. Ʋ wɩyaalɩya abee ʋ wɩbasɩya buloŋ gyɩ daga anɩɩ ʋ yaa nɩbal lɛ.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ aa mʋʋ dɩ ʋ naa Igyipiti tɩmma hʋ kɩdɩgɩ dɩ ʋ aa dɔgɩsɩ Iziral tɩmma hʋ kɩdɩgɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ mʋ laa pɛ Iziral tɩɩna hʋ tɩyaŋ, a ŋmaa Igyipiti tɩɩna hʋ kpa lo ʋ sʋba.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Ʋ fa liise anɩɩ ʋ nala hʋ sɩ gyɩma anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ laa ba ta, amɛ ba bɩ wuwo gyɩma.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩgballɩya, ʋ bɩl mʋwa dɩ Iziral tɩmma balɩya aa kaa dɔmɔŋ. Ʋ mʋ dɩ ʋ laa ba ta, aŋ marɩ ba dɔmɔŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, ‘Ma yaa nɩmmabalɩya rɛ, bee rɛ tɩŋ ma mɩɩgɩ gul kɩ kaa dɔmɔŋ?’

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nal hʋ fa aa di dee ʋ dɔŋtɩɩna hʋ nyuu tɩyaŋ kaŋ Moosi yige ta aŋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, ‘Aŋnɛ rɛ lɩɩ ɩ dɩ ɩ kɩ deŋ á nyuu, a laa á wɩya kɩ basɩ?

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Koo ɩ aa kyɛ ɩ kpʋ ŋ mɛ rɛ anɩɩ ɩ dɩya aa kpʋʋ Igyipiti tɩɩna hʋ gɛɛ rɛ?’

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Moosi aa nɩɩ gɛɛ, ʋ lɩɩ fá Igyipiti paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a mʋ yaa nɩhʋwal Mɩdɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ hɔŋ a kpa haaŋ, a lʋl boyoŋbiisi balɩya.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Moosi aa naa gɛɛ, ʋ yaa ʋ wɩkperii. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ mʋ pele dɩ ʋ marɩ na tɩɩbiye hʋ weliŋ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ nɩɩ á Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ taal, dɩ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Mɩyaŋ nɛ yaa ɩ naabaala Abɩraham abee Azɩkɩ abee Gyeekɔbɩ Wɩɩsɩ’. Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ, Moosi sɩŋ kɩ kyele. Ʋ bɩl bɩ wuwo dɩ ʋ deŋ.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ bɩl basɩ baa, ‘Ɛɛ rɛ á Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ basɩ tɩya Moosi a baa, kuwoni ɩ naatɔbɔ ɩ naasɩ tɩyaŋ. Wɩɩsɩ we lee hʋ tɩyaŋ ɩ aa sɩna tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Ŋ naa ɛɛ hʋ ba aa dɔgɩsɩ ŋ nala rɛ Igyipiti paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a nɩɩ ba wiile mɛ. Ŋ ko tuu dɩ ŋ laa ba ta rɛ. Sii dɩ ŋ tɩma ɩ dɩ ɩ mɩɩgɩ mʋ Igyipiti.’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ bɩl marɩ kpa, a baa, ‘Moosi no rɛ Iziral tɩmma hʋ gyɩ vɩya aŋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ baa, Aŋnɛ rɛ baa dɩ ɩ kɩ deŋ ma a laa á wɩya kɩ basɩ?’ Ʋ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ yaŋ tɩma dɩ ʋ yaa ba nɩhɩyawʋ abee ba laataal. Wɩɩsɩ rɛ gyɩ tɩŋ ʋ malɩka, ʋ gyɩ ko Moosi lee a gyɩ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ tɩɩbiye rɛ aa di diŋ, a ko pɛ Moosi tɩyaŋ dɩ ʋ laa ʋ nala ta.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: Moosi no tɩɩ rɛ bɩl basɩ tɩya Iziral tɩmma a baa, ‘Wɩɩsɩ sɩ tɩŋ ʋ tɩŋdaal dɩ ʋ ko ma lee anɩɩ ʋ aa tɩma ŋ gɛɛ. Ʋ sɩ yaa ma nɩɩnabiye tɩɩ.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Moosi bee á naabaala gyɩ laŋŋa pogo tʋtʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Moosi gyɩ na malɩka dogiŋbal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Saana nyuu tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ malɩka hʋ kpa Wɩɩsɩ nyʋwa hʋ aa kaŋ mɩɩbol tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ laa basɩ tɩya ma.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka á naabaala hʋ gyɩ vɩya ʋ nyʋwa, aŋ kpa ba hal yɩya ʋ, ka ba tɩliise buloŋ fa kɩ tɩya ba anɩɩ ba mɩɩgɩ mʋ Igyipiti paalʋʋ.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii mɔ vʋgɩ kɩdɩgɩ ʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ nɔbiye. Nyɛ rɛ ba sii marɩ ʋ, ba tenni buloŋ fɩyɛlɩ kɩŋ hʋ ba tɩɩ aa mɔɔ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ kpa ʋ hal yɩya ba, a vɩya ba ta ba kɩ kyʋwalɩ kyɩŋwʋlɩya abee pene abee wɩɩpɔsɩɩ. Ba saba gɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ logiŋ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala aa saba tɩyaŋ. Ba kɩdɩgɩ saba rɛ anɩɩ dɩ Wɩɩsɩ baa, ‘Mamaa Iziral tɩmma, bɩsɩ mɔllɩbalɩya hʋ ma fa aa we pogo hʋ tɩyaŋ, ŋ daa ma fa kpa ma kɩna koo ma pʋsɩ kɩ sʋla.

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Amɛ ma tɔdʋŋ hʋ ma aa yɩrɩ Mɔlɔkɩ, ʋ rɛ ma marɩ taŋtɩ-dɩya we gɛɛ, abee ma kyɩŋwʋlɩya tɔdʋŋ hʋ ma aa yɩrɩ Rɛfaŋ, ba aa rɛ ma gyɩ kyʋŋ kɩ vala gɛɛ. Ma tɩɩ rɛ mɔɔ ba, a kɩ marɩ ba. Ɛɛwɩya, ŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ba kasɩ ma mʋ lee aa para Baabilɔŋ.’

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ bɩl basɩ baa, Á naabaala gyɩ aa we pogo hʋ tɩyaŋ, ba gyɩ marɩ taŋtɩ-dɩya rɛ doŋ a tɩya Wɩɩsɩ. Taŋtɩ-dɩya no rɛ gyɩ daga anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɔnɩ we ba lee rɛ. Wɩɩsɩ tɩɩ rɛ gyɩ daga Moosi ɛɛ ʋ aa sɩ marɩ dɩya no. Ɛɛ hʋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ aa kyɛ rɛ ʋ daga Moosi, ʋ mɛ leŋ ba marɩ ʋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ á naabaala laa taŋtɩ-dɩya no ba kuwoma lee. Saŋa hʋ Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ aa leŋ ba bee Gyoosʋwa mʋ yuwo paalʋʋ kɩdɩgɩ nala, a kil ba ta aŋ laa ba taŋha hɔŋ hʋ, dɩ ba ha kyʋŋ taŋtɩ-dɩya no rɛ. Taŋtɩ-dɩya no gyɩ we ba lee rɛ, a ko pele á naabaa Deviti saŋa.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ sɩŋ pɔ Deviti rɛ weliŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ Deviti gyɩ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ dɩ ʋ pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ dɩ ʋ wuwo saa dɩya tɩya ʋ Wɩɩsɩ hʋ á naabaa Gyeekɔbɩ fa aa kyʋwalɩ.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Amɛ Deviti gyɩ bɩ wuwo saa dɩya hʋ. Ʋ biibaal Solomɔn nɛ gyɩ mɩɩgɩ ko saa ʋ, a tɩya Wɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: Amɛ Wɩɩsɩ aa tee kɩŋ buloŋ, ʋ bee gyʋʋ dɩɩsɩ hʋ nihuwobiŋ aa saa dʋŋ. Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal kɩdɩgɩ gyɩ basɩ wɩɩ no wɩya rɛ, a baa dɩ

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ baa, ‘Wɩɩsɩbee tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ kuworikpasa we, taŋha daha rɛ yaa ŋ naasɩ libinii. Dɩɩgɛɛ rɛ ma sɩ wuwo saa tɩya ŋ? Lee rɛ ŋ liwiyesii we?

english_verse: I have made everything.”
translation_verse: Ŋ daa marɩ kɩna no buloŋ?’

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal buloŋ tuwo dɩ ma naabaala gyɩ bɩ tɩŋ ŋmanɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ a dɔgɩsɩ ʋ. Ba gyɩ kpʋʋ nala hʋ buloŋ aa laa sɩya basɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩpʋlʋŋ tʋŋtʋnnɩ hʋ koyi wɩya rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ ko, ma mɛ kpa ʋ yallɩ tɩya nala, ba kpʋ ʋ.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ leŋ ʋ malɩkasɩ dagɩ ma ʋ mɩrɩsɩ buloŋ nɛ, amɛ ma bɩ tɩŋa ʋ nyʋwa.”’ Ba yaga Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ kpʋ abee bʋwa

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ marɩ gyʋʋ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ yʋga. Ʋ wil deŋ wɩɩsɩnyuu a na Wɩɩbal hʋ gaŋdarɩ, a na Yesu mɛ dɩ ʋ sɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ noduu logiŋ.

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ basɩ baa, “Ma deŋ na, ŋ naa wɩɩsɩbee dimbeenyʋwa rɛ dɩ ʋ suro, ka Nihuwobiŋ Biye hʋ sɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ noduu logiŋ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ nala hʋ buloŋ kyɩɩrɩ yʋga, aŋ tɔ ba dɩgɩna, ka ba buloŋ teŋ sii giri mʋ kyige ʋ,

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: a kana ʋ yige ta ʋ lɩɩ bee hʋ samanɩ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba yaŋ paa bʋwa tɔ kana ʋ, a kɩ yaga lee lee buloŋ. Nala hʋ aa yaga Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ buloŋ gyɩ wuri ba gannɩ rɛ a kpa tɩya bapʋwasɩbiye kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Sɔɔl dɩ ʋ kɩ deŋ.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Ba aa yaga Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ gɛɛ rɛ, ka ʋ kɩ yɩrɩ á Tɩɩna Yesu a kɩ sʋla ʋ, “Ŋ Tɩɩna Yesu, laa ŋ dʋma mʋ ɩ lee.”

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Amɛ Sɔɔl gyɩ yaa siri rɛ dɩ ʋ yalmɩ nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di. Ʋ gyɩ aa sii gɔllɩ ba dɩɩsɩ rɛ, a tarɩ ba baala abee ba haana buloŋ ko lɩɩ, a leŋ ba kasɩ ba mʋ tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ. Ba basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩweliye hʋ Samaarɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Ka Yesu hatɩnna hʋ mɛ gyɩ aa lɩɩ fá Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ hʋ, ba mɛ gɔllɩ mʋ lee buloŋ, a kɩ basɩ Yesu Krisita wɩweliye hʋ.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Fɩlɩpɩ sii mʋ Samaarɩya paalʋʋ tɔbal kɩdɩgɩ, a basɩ Yesu Krisita, laataal hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa tɩma wɩya, a kɩ tɩya doŋ nala.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Gyamaa hʋ buloŋ gyɩ wee dɩgɩŋgyegil lɛ a kɩ nɩɩ wɩya hʋ ʋ gyɩ aa basɩ. Ba buloŋ gyɩ gyegile nɩɩ wɩya hʋ ʋ aa basa rɛ, a na wɩmagɩla hʋ buloŋ mɛ ʋ aa yaa.

english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, haŋ tɔɔ hʋ tɩmma buloŋ tenni gyɩ fɩyɛla rɛ kɩŋkaŋ.

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Haŋ tɔɔ hʋ tɩyaŋ, baal kɩdɩgɩ mɛ rɛ gyɩ we doŋ, ba kɩ yɩrɩ Siimɔŋ. Ʋ gyɩ kaŋ daaluliye rɛ weliŋ, a kɩ vʋga. Ʋ wɩyaalɩya buloŋ gyɩ yaa Samaarɩya paalʋʋ nala wɩkperii rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ʋ mɛ gyɩ aa kɩ daga ʋ tɩɩ anɩɩ ʋ bɩra rɛ.

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: Nɩhɩyasɩ bee biibiisi buloŋ gyɩ kpa ba nyuu we ʋ wɩyaalɩya tɩyaŋ nɛ a kɩ basɩ a baa, “Baal no rɛ yaa Wɩɩsɩ, ʋ kaŋ dee rɛ yʋga, dee hʋ ba aa yɩrɩ Wɩɩsɩ debal hʋ, ʋ rɛ ʋ kana.”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa yaa wɩkperiye no pɩɩsɩ rɛ, ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ nala na anɩɩ ʋ bɩra rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ba kpa ba nyuni we ʋ wɩyaalɩya tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Amɛ Fɩlɩpɩ gyɩ aa basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩweliye hʋ wɩya, a kɩ daga Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ wɩya, a basɩ Yesu Krisita wɩya mɛ, nala hʋ gyɩ laa wɩya hʋ di rɛ, ba fo ba wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ, baala abee haana buloŋ.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Siimɔŋ tɩɩ mɛ gyɩ ko laa Yesu wɩya hʋ di rɛ, ba fo ʋ mɛ wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ. Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ, ʋ kɩ mal Fɩlɩpɩ kɩŋkaŋ. Ʋ aa naa wɩkperiye hʋ bee wɩmagɩla hʋ Fɩlɩpɩ aa yaa, ʋ yaa ʋ wɩkperii rɛ.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ aa we Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ nɩɩ anɩɩ Samaarɩya paalʋʋ nala laa Wɩɩsɩ wɩya di rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba ta Piita bee Gyɔɔŋ dɩ ba mʋ doŋ.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita bee Gyɔɔŋ mʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ba dɩ Wɩɩsɩ leŋ ʋ Wiyesi Welii hʋ ko tuu gyʋʋ ba tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Beewɩya haŋ saŋa hʋ dɩ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ ha gyɩ bɩ gyʋʋ Samaarɩya paalʋʋ nal kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ. Á Tɩɩna Yesu Krisita feŋ dʋŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba fa fo ba wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ hʋ.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Piita bee Gyɔɔŋ kpa ba nosi dɔbɔ ba nyuni tɩyaŋ, a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ba. Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ ko tuu gyʋʋ ba tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Siimɔŋ aa naa dɩ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ kpa ba nosi dɔbɔ nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di tɩyaŋ, Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ gyʋʋ ba tɩyaŋ gɛɛ, ʋ sii kpa molbii a sʋl ba a baa,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: “Ma laa aŋ tɩya ŋ ma dee no dɩ ŋ mɛ ko kpa nosi dɔbɔ nal buloŋ tɩyaŋ, dɩ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ gyʋʋ ʋ tɩɩna tɩyaŋ.” Ɛɛ rɛ Piita basɩ tɩya ʋ, a baa,

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: “Ɩ liise ɩ sɩ wuwo kpa molbiye yɔbɔ Wɩɩsɩ aa kpaa kɩŋ hʋ tɩya nala ʋ bɔnyɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ koo? Wɩɩsɩ sɩ leŋ ɩ bee ɩ molbiye no buloŋ gyʋʋ diŋ.

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Ɩ noŋ tuwo á tʋma no tɩyaŋ, beewɩya ɩ bɩ pʋllɩ ɩ tɩya tɩya Wɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: Get rid of these evil thoughts and ask God to forgive you.
translation_verse: Mɩɩgɩ bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ ɩ wɩbɔmɔ no tɩyaŋ aŋ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ dɩ ʋ kpa ɩ tɩliisibɔmɔ no kyɛ ɩ.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Ŋ naa anɩɩ ɩ tɩya bile rɛ kɩŋkaŋ, ka ɩ bɩl yaa wɩbɔmɔ yoŋ mɛ.”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Siimɔŋ sʋla Piita bee Gyɔɔŋ dɩ ba kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ wɩya no buloŋ ba aa basa ta yaa ʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Ba aa basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya hʋ ko teŋ, a daga wɩya hʋ buloŋ ba aa gyɩma Yesu lugo tɩyaŋ a tɩya ba, ba sii doŋ nɛ, a mɩɩgɩ mʋ Gyerusalɛm. Saŋa hʋ ba aa mʋ Gyerusalɛm hʋ tɩyaŋ, ba basɩ Yesu Krisita wɩweliye hʋ rɛ Samaarɩya paalʋʋ tɔnɩ yʋga tɩyaŋ. Fɩlɩpɩ kyeŋ baal kɩdɩgɩ aa lɩɩ Itoopiya paalʋʋ

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka malɩka kɩdɩgɩ ko basɩ tɩya Fɩlɩpɩ a baa, “Sii, a mʋ wɩɩpɔsɩɩ noduu dɩɩlaŋ, a mʋ ŋmaŋbɩnɩɩ hʋ aa lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm kɩ mʋ Gasa.” Nyɛ rɛ Fɩlɩpɩ sii kpa mʋ.

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: Ʋ fa hɔŋ toriku hʋ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ rɛ, aŋ kɩ karɩmɩ Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ lee kɩdɩgɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal Azaaya aa saba.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ basɩ tɩya Fɩlɩpɩ dɩ ʋ vala mʋ puu toriku hʋ.

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Fɩlɩpɩ fá mʋ puu ʋ, dɩ ʋ nɩɩ baal hʋ aa karɩmɩ Wɩɩsɩ teŋ logiŋ hʋ Azaaya aa saba. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ dɩ ʋ gyɩŋ wɩya hʋ ʋ aa karɩmɩ hʋ memii rɛ?

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ baal hʋ baa, “Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ sɩ yaa gyɩŋ ba memii dɩ nal bɩ daga tɩya ŋ.” Aŋ yɩrɩ Fɩlɩpɩ dɩ ʋ ko gyɩŋ hɔŋ pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ teŋ lee hʋ ʋ fa aa karɩmɩ hʋ, wɩɩ no rɛ ba sabɩ doŋ: “Anɩɩ ba aa kaŋ piyesi kɩ mʋ dɩ ba kpʋ ka ʋ tallɩ ʋ nyʋwa gɛɛ, anɩɩ ba aa kɩ kʋsɩ pebiye pʋna ka ʋ nyʋwa bee suri gɛɛ, ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ʋ mɛ bɩ suro ʋ nyʋwa.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ yalmɩ ʋ rɛ weliŋ, ʋ kɩna gyɩ sʋma mɛ, ka ba vʋʋlɩ vɔmɔ ʋ, a kpa kyogisi tɩya ʋ. Ʋ mɩɩbol yaŋ aa tene taŋha no nyuu nyɛ, aŋnɛ rɛ sɩ wuwo tɩŋ ʋ doho nala naasɩ.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Baal hʋ aa karɩma Wɩɩsɩ wɩya hʋ teŋ, ʋ pɩyɛsɩ Fɩlɩpɩ, a baa “Aŋnɛ wɩya rɛ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal hʋ kɩ basɩ nyɛ? Ʋ tɩɩ wɩya rɛ koo, nɩdɩma kɩdɩgɩ wɩya rɛ?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Fɩlɩpɩ yaŋ piili wɩya no ʋ aa karɩma tɩyaŋ, a basɩ Yesu Wɩweliye hʋ buloŋ a tɩya baal hʋ.

english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Fɩlɩpɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Dɩ ɩ rɛ laa Yesu wɩya hʋ buloŋ di abee ɩ tɩya buloŋ, ŋ sɩ fo ɩ wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ.” Ɛɛ rɛ baal hʋ baa, “Ŋ laa di rɛ anɩɩ Yesu Krisita rɛ yaa Wɩɩsɩ biye.”

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ baal hʋ leŋ gyaagʋ hʋ fa aa tarɩ toriku hʋ sɩŋ, ka ʋ bee Fɩlɩpɩ buloŋ kpa tuu nɩɩ hʋ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Fɩlɩpɩ fo ʋ wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Ba aa ko dɩ ba lɩɩ nɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ ko kpa Fɩlɩpɩ dɩɩgɩ. Baal hʋ bɩrɩma dɩ ʋ bɩl bɩ naa Fɩlɩpɩ, aŋka ʋ kpa ʋ ŋmanɩɩ kɩ vala, ʋ teŋ buloŋ fɩyɛlɩ kɩŋkaŋ.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Sii mʋ gyʋʋ tɔɔ hʋ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba sɩ daga ɩ wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa sɩ yaa.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Mɩyaŋ nɛ sɩ daga ʋ tʋwara maga gɛɛ ʋ aa sɩ na ŋ wɩya.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Sɔɔl aa mʋʋ Gyerusalɛm, ʋ gyɩ aa kyɛ dɩ ʋ gyʋʋ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ aa we doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Amɛ ba gyɩ bɩ laa di anɩɩ ʋ mɛ laa Yesu wɩya di rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ gyɩ tɩŋ ba gyɩ kɩ fá ʋ.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ hʋ kyʋwalɩɩ tɩyaŋ, ʋ naa anɩɩ ɩ Ananiyasɩ rɛ ko gyʋʋ a kpa ɩ nosi dɔbɔ ʋ tɩyaŋ ʋ sɩya bɩl suri.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ, dɩ kɩna badɔmɔŋ aa kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ fuwonamɩya para pirige lɩɩ tele ʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ, ka ʋ bɩl wuwo kɩ na. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ sii, ba fo ʋ wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ.

english_verse: Saul kept on threatening to kill the Lord's followers. He even went to the high priest
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka Sɔɔl mɛ ha lɩɩ sɩɩ pɔ Yesu hatɩnna hʋ tɩyaŋ, a kɩ fugisi ba, a kɩ basɩ anɩɩ ʋ sɩ kpʋ ba.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɔɔl gyɩ sii lɩɩ kpa ŋmanɩɩ a kɩ mʋ Damaasiku. Ʋ gyɩ ko mʋ kpaga Damaasiku rɛ, dɩ kyaanʋʋ kɩdɩgɩ pirige lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu, a kyaanɩ we ʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Ʋ lɩɩ tele, aŋka ʋ nɩɩ nal taal dɩ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl, bee rɛ tɩŋ ɩ kɩ dɔgɩsɩ ŋ nyɛ.”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɔɔl pɩyɛsɩ, “Aŋnɛ rɛ yaa ɩ, ŋ Tɩɩna?” Ɛɛ rɛ taal hʋ baa, “Mɩyaŋ Yesu rɛ, nal hʋ ɩ aa dɔgɩsɩ hʋ.

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Sɔɔl bee nala hʋ fa aa val hʋ buloŋ mɩɩgɩ sɩŋ tuwori kɩ deŋ. Beewɩya ba mɛ nɩɩ taal hʋ rɛ, amɛ ba bɩ naa nal buloŋ.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɔɔl nyagɩ sii sɩŋ, a kaŋ ʋ sɩya suri. Ʋ sɩya gyɩ ta kyirigi waasʋ gɛɛ rɛ, ka ʋ bee na kɩŋ buloŋ. Ʋ noŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba gyɩ kana, a kana ʋ tarɩ mʋ pele Damaasiku.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Ʋ nyʋlɩma we doŋ gɛɛ rɛ ʋ kyɛyɛ boto, dɩ ʋ bɩ wuwo di kɩŋ buloŋ, a bɩ wuwo nyʋwa nɩɩ mɛ.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Yesu hatɩnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ mɛ gyɩ we Damaasiku tɩyaŋ, ba kɩ yɩrɩ Ananiyasɩ. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ duwosi dɩ á Tɩɩna Yesu yɩrɩ ʋ a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ananiyasɩ!” Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ saŋa a baa, “Mɩyaŋ nɛ nyɛ, Tɩɩna.”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ á Tɩɩna Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Sii kpa ŋmanɩɩ hʋ ba yɩrɩ ŋmaŋ-deginii hʋ, a mʋ baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Gyudasɩ dɩya. Dɩ ɩ ko mʋ, pɩyɛsɩ ʋ dɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ aa lɩɩ Taasu rɛ ɩ kɩ kyɛ. Ʋ feŋ nɛ yaa Sɔɔl. Ʋ rɛ ha yaŋ kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ gɛɛ.

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Ananiyasɩ basɩ baa, “Ŋ Tɩɩna, nala yʋga rɛ kɩŋkaŋ a basɩ tɩya ŋ anɩɩ baal no bɔmɔ rɛ weliŋ abee ɩ nala hʋ aa we Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ rɛ tɩya ʋ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ʋ ko Damaasiku daha, a kasɩ nala hʋ buloŋ aa kyʋwalɩ ɩ.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Á Tɩɩna Yesu basɩ tɩya Ananiyasɩ a baa, “Sii mʋ. Mɩyaŋ nɛ lɩɩ ʋ dɩ ʋ tuŋ ŋ tʋma. Ʋ sɩ mʋ basɩ ŋ wɩya tɩya nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma abee kuworo abee Iziral tɩmma mɛ.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Ananiyasɩ sii kpa mʋ dɩya hʋ Sɔɔl aa gyʋwa. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ gyʋʋ a kpa ʋ nosi dɔbɔ Sɔɔl tɩyaŋ aŋ baa, “Ŋ naabiye Sɔɔl, á Tɩɩna Yesu hʋ ɩ aa naa ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ dɩ ɩ aa ko daha, ʋ rɛ tɩma ŋ dɩ ŋ ko daha dɩ ɩ sɩya wuwo suri, ka dɩ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ mɛ gyʋʋ ɩ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: Ka ʋ di kɩna a bɩl kaŋ dee. Ʋ gyɩ we Damaasiku tɩyaŋ nɛ kyɛyɛ baŋmana, ʋ bee Yesu hatɩnna hʋ aa we doŋ. Sɔɔl basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya a tɩya nala Damaasiku tɩyaŋ

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa we doŋ gɛɛ rɛ, ʋ aa sii mʋ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩsɩ a piili kɩ basɩ Yesu wɩya a kɩ tɩya nala anɩɩ Yesu rɛ yaa Wɩɩsɩ biye hʋ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ gyɩ aa nɩɩ wɩya hʋ Sɔɔl aa basɩ, ʋ gyɩ yaa ba buloŋ wɩkperii rɛ. Ba kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ, a baa “Baal no daa fa we Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ a kɩ kpʋ nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di gɛɛ? Ka gɛɛ wɩya rɛ ʋ bɩl ko daha dɩ ʋ ko kasɩ ba mʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ lee?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ we doŋ pɩɩsɩ rɛ. Nyɛ rɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ, Gyuuma hʋ sii vʋʋlɩ dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ.

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Sɔɔl ko nɩɩ ba aa kyɛ dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ hʋ wɩya. Wɩɩhaa bee tebine buloŋ, ba aa sɩŋ ba bee pagɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ dɩ ba na ʋ a kpʋ.

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Yesu hatɩnna hʋ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Banabaasɩ rɛ gyɩ pɛɛ Sɔɔl tɩyaŋ, a kana ʋ mʋ Yesu kpambɩsɩ hʋ lee. Ʋ rɛ gyɩ basɩ tɩya ba ɛɛ hʋ Sɔɔl aa naa á tɩɩna Yesu ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ basɩ wɩya tɩya ʋ. Aŋ ha gyɩ bɩl basɩ tɩya ba ɛɛ hʋ Sɔɔl aa basɩ Yesu wɩya abee nyuduwo a tɩya nala Damaasiku tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba laa ʋ we ba tɩyaŋ, ba beel-ʋ buloŋ we doŋ, a kɩ gɔllɩ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ,

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Ʋ dɔŋtɩŋsɩ hʋ aa ko nɩɩ anɩɩ dɩ ba aa lʋga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ rɛ, ba sii kana ʋ a mʋ Sizaariya, ʋ sɩŋ doŋ kpa mʋ Taasu.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ, nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di Gyudɩya abee Galili abee Samaarɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, ba buloŋ yaŋ na wiyese, a marɩ laa Wɩɩsɩ wɩya di weliŋ, a kɩ tɩŋ Yesu á Tɩɩna, a kɩ fá ʋ mɛ. Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ mɛ pɛ ba tɩyaŋ, ba wala kɩ pɛ. Piita mʋʋ Liida abee Gyoopa

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Lee buloŋ nɛ Piita fa kɩ gɔllɩ. Kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ, ʋ sii kpa mʋ Liida dɩ ʋ na Wɩɩsɩ nala hʋ aa we doŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa mʋ doŋ, ʋ na baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Eniyasɩ. Ʋ fa gbarɩga rɛ, a pɩŋ dɩya tɩyaŋ bɩsɩ kyori, dɩ ʋ bɩ wuwo lɩɩ bee.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Eniyasɩ, Yesu Krisita tɩya ɩ laaŋfɩya rɛ. Sii kpa ɩ wasa.” Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Eniyasɩ pirigi nyagɩ sii sɩŋ abee dee.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ aa lɩɩ Liida abee Saarɔŋ aa naa gɛɛ, ba bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ ba wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ nɛ aŋ kɩ tɩŋ á Tɩɩna Yesu.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Haaŋ kɩdɩgɩ mɛ rɛ fa we Gyoopa tɩyaŋ, a mɛ fa yaa Yesu hatɩnnɩ. Ʋ feŋ nɛ fa yaa Tabiita Ʋ fa kaŋ bɔnyɛ rɛ weliŋ a kɩ pɛ nyaaba abee nɩtaaga tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Piita ha gyɩ aa we Liida tɩyaŋ gɛɛ rɛ ka haaŋ no sʋba Gyoopa tɩyaŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ba fo ʋ nɩɩ, a kana ʋ gyɩŋ biŋ salnyuu dɩya kɩdɩgɩ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Gyoopa bee Liida fa bɩ boliye. Nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di Gyoopa tɩyaŋ nɩɩ anɩɩ Piita we Liida tɩyaŋ nɛ. Nyɛ rɛ ba tɩŋ nala balɩya ʋ lee dɩ ba mʋ sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ fa ba wulo lagɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba mʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Piita sii tɩŋ ba hal a mʋ ko. Ʋ aa ko pele doŋ, ɛɛ rɛ ba kana ʋ gyɩŋ salnyuu dɩya hʋ. Ka lohaana badɔmɔŋ aa we doŋ buloŋ sɩŋ ʋ hal a kɩ wii, a paa galaalɩya abee gavʋwalɩya Dɔɔkasɩ aa mara, a ko lɩɩ kɩ daga Piita.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita leŋ ba buloŋ lɩɩ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba aa lɩɩ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ ko teŋ, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ tuu gbinni a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ. Ʋ aa kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ hʋ mʋ teŋ, ʋ mɩɩgɩ bɩrɩmɩ sʋʋ hʋ lee, a basɩ baa, “Tabiita, sii!” Nyɛ rɛ ʋ kaŋ ʋ sɩya suri. Ʋ aa naa Piita, ʋ sii hɔŋ.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Piita mʋ pele ʋ, a kaŋ ʋ noŋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ sii sɩŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ yɩrɩ Wɩɩsɩ nala hʋ abee lohaana hʋ buloŋ ko laŋŋɩ, a kpa Tabiita tɩya ba. Ba buloŋ na ʋ dɩ ʋ bɩl weye.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ wɩɩ no taalɩ Gyoopa buloŋ. Nala yʋga ko laa á Tɩɩna Yesu wɩya di.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Piita gyɩ we Gyoopa tɩyaŋ a pɩɩsɩ mʋhʋ rɛ. Baal kɩdɩgɩ gyɩ we doŋ a kɩ tɩgɩsɩ pulo, ba kɩ yɩrɩ Siimɔŋ. Ʋ dɩya rɛ Piita gyɩ gyʋʋ.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gyɩ we Sizaariya tɩyaŋ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Kɔniiliyo. Ʋ fa yaa laalyuwolo aa lɩɩ Itali paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɩhɩyawʋ rɛ, a fa kɩ laa ba kɔɔ sɩya.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Ʋ fa aa tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ ŋmanɩɩ rɛ weliŋ, ʋ bee ʋ dɩya nala buloŋ fa kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ. Ʋ gyɩ aa kɩ pɛ Gyuuma nɩtaaga tɩyaŋ nɛ, aŋ kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ mɛ saŋa buloŋ.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ wɩɩhaa anɩɩ kerifi boto saŋa, Kɔniiliyo na ʋ duwoso tɩyaŋ dɩ malɩka kɩdɩgɩ ko gyʋʋ yɩrɩ ʋ, “Kɔniiliyo.”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ kaŋ ʋ sɩya suri dɩ ʋ deŋ malɩka hʋ. Kambɩŋ kana ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ pɩyɛsɩ a baa, “Bekiŋ nɛ, ŋ Tɩɩna?” Nyɛ rɛ malɩka hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ nɩɩ ɛɛ hʋ ɩ aa kɩ kyʋwalɩ ʋ rɛ, a bɩl na ɛɛ hʋ ɩ aa yaa wɩweliye kɩ tɩya nɩtaaga mɛ. Nyɛ wɩya Wɩɩsɩ nɩɩ ɩ kyʋwala rɛ.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Leŋ dɩ nala mʋ Gyoopa lagɩlagɩ no, a kyɛ yɩrɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Siimɔŋ Piita dɩ ʋ ko ɩ lee.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Ʋ aa gyʋʋ pul-tɩgɩsɩl kɩdɩgɩ dɩya rɛ ba kɩ yɩrɩ ʋ mɛ Siimɔŋ. Ʋ dɩya sɩŋ kpaga mpʋwɔnʋʋ nyʋwa rɛ.”

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Kɔniiliyo basɩ wɩya hʋ buloŋ ʋ aa naa a tɩya ba, aŋ ta ba, ba kpa mʋ Gyoopa.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩgballɩya, wɩɩsɩ ko kɩ maga nyutʋtʋʋ gɛɛ, dɩ ba kpaga Gyoopa rɛ. Saŋa no tɩɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ Piita mɛ sii gyɩŋ salnyuu dɩ ʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: dɩ Wɩɩsɩ nyuu lɔgɔ suri. Ka kɩŋ kɩdɩgɩ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ wagyɛbal a lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu, a kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ nala rɛ kaŋ ganyʋʋsɩ hʋ banaa tɩyaŋ, a kaŋ ko kɩ tuu taŋha.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ nɩɩ nal taal dɩ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Piita, sii kpʋ kɩ kyaŋ.”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita baa, “Aayɩ, ŋ Tɩɩna, ŋ ha bɩ maakyiye kyaŋ kɩŋ buloŋ aa yaa bisiŋ kɩŋ, koo á sɩna.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ taal hʋ bɩl mɩɩgɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Kɩŋ kɛ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa baa ʋ weliye, ta basɩ baa ʋ yaa bisiŋ kɩŋ nɛ.”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Wɩya no buloŋ yaa gɛɛ rɛ naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋto, ka kɩna hʋ bɩl mɩɩgɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩsɩnyuu, aŋ bɩl bɩ ko tuu.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Wɩɩ hʋ yaa Piita wɩkperii. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ hɔŋ kɩ kyɛ wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa naa memii, dɩ nala hʋ Kɔniiliyo fa aa tɩma hʋ rɛ pɩyɛsa ko sɩŋ Siimɔŋ paganyʋwa tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
translation_verse: Sii tuu, a ta yaa gyegye, ka dɩ ɩ tɩŋ ba tɩyaŋ mʋ. Mɩyaŋ nɛ tɩm-ba ɩ lee”

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Piita sii ko tuu, a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Mɩyaŋ nɛ yaa nal hʋ ma aa kyɛ. Bekiŋ wɩɩ rɛ ma kaŋ kɩ kyɛ ŋ?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: ƐƐ rɛ ba baa, “Laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Kɔniiliyo, ʋ rɛ tɩm-ma. Ʋ yaa tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩɩna rɛ, a kɩ fá Wɩɩsɩ mɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Gyuuma mɛ buloŋ kɩ tɩya ʋ gyɩrɩma weliŋ. Malɩka kɩdɩgɩ rɛ ko basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ yɩrɩ ɩ, dɩ dɩ ɩ rɛ ko ʋ dɩya, dɩ ʋ sɩ nɩɩ wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa sɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita sʋl ba dɩ ba gyʋʋ pɩŋ sɩya gballɩ. Ʋ sɩya aa gballa, ʋ sii ba buloŋ kpa mʋ. Nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di Gyoopa tɩyaŋ, ba badɔmɔŋ mɛ sii tɩŋa ʋ mʋ.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Ba aa lɩɩ ŋmanɩɩ, ʋ kyɛlɩya kyɛɛ rɛ ba gyʋʋ Sizaariya. Bee no tɩyaŋ nɛ Kɔniiliyo fa we. Ba aa mʋ dɩ ʋ bee ʋ lʋlla abee ʋ kyaŋsɩ badɔmɔŋ ʋ aa yɩra, dɩ ba buloŋ hɔŋ kɩ gyegili ʋ.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Piita aa ko dɩ ʋ gyʋʋ, Kɔniiliyo sii mʋ kyem-ʋ, a tuu gbinni ʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ, a kyʋwalɩ ʋ.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Piita leŋ ʋ sii sɩŋ, ka ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ta yaa gɛɛ, beewɩya, ŋ mɛ yaa nihuwobiŋ nɛ anɩɩ ɩ.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Piita bee Kɔniiliyo basɩ wɩya kpa tɩŋ dɔmɔŋ kɩ gyʋʋ dɩya hʋ. Piita aa gyʋʋ dɩ nala bɩ kyalla a laŋŋɩ doŋ.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Piita basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma tɩɩtɩɩ gyɩma rɛ anɩɩ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal ŋmanɩɩ tɩyaŋ, ʋ yaa sɩna rɛ dɩ ba laŋŋɩ nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma tɩyaŋ. Ba paalɩ bee gyʋʋ ba dɩɩsɩ mɛ gba. Amɛ, ŋ kɛ, Wɩɩsɩ daga ŋ nɛ dɩ ŋ ta buloŋ kaŋ nal buloŋ kɩ yɩrɩ bisiŋ tɩɩna.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ba aa ko yɩrɩ ŋ, ŋ bɩ kaŋ vɩya. Ŋ aa ko nyɛ, yaŋ basɩ wɩɩ hʋ aa tɩŋ ɩ yɩrɩ ŋ?”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Kɔniiliyo basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Ʋ kyɛyɛ banaa rɛ nyɛ, anɩɩ saŋa no tɩɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ, wɩɩhaa kerifi boto saŋa, ŋ kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ ŋ dɩya tɩyaŋ. Dɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ gɛɛ, a ko sɩŋ ŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ. Ka ʋ gannɩ buloŋ kɩ tʋla.

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ŋ a baa, ‘Kɔniiliyo, Wɩɩsɩ nɩɩ ɩ aa kɩ kyʋwalɩ ʋ gɛɛ rɛ, a na ɩ aa kɩ pɛ nyaaba abee nɩtaaga mɛ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Sii tɩŋ nala dɩ ba mʋ Gyoopa, a kyɛ yɩrɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Siimɔŋ Piita dɩ ʋ ko. Ʋ aa gyʋʋ pul-tɩgɩsɩl kɩdɩgɩ dɩya rɛ, ba kɩ yɩrɩ ʋ mɛ Siimɔŋ. Ʋ dɩya sɩŋ kpaga mpʋwɔnʋʋ hʋ nyʋwa rɛ.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Kɔniiliyo bɩl basɩ baa, ‘Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ŋ ta nala lagɩlagɩ dɩ ba ko yɩrɩ ɩ. Ɩ mɛ yaa kuwokeri rɛ, ɩ aa wuwo ko nyɛ. Lagɩlagɩ no, á buloŋ laŋŋa rɛ Wɩɩsɩ sɩya tɩyaŋ, a kɩ gyegili dɩ á nɩɩ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa daga ɩ dɩ ɩ basɩ tɩya ma.”’ Piita wɩbasɩɩ

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Piita yaŋ kaŋ nyʋwa suri, a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ, nyɛ kɛ ŋ gyɩma anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɛ sɩya tɩyaŋ, nihuwobiŋ buloŋ yaa kɩdɩgɩ rɛ.

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: Nal kɛ buloŋ aa fá Wɩɩsɩ, aŋ kɩ yaa wɩya hʋ aa tɩŋ ŋmanɩɩ, Wɩɩsɩ aa laa ʋ rɛ. Beewɩya, Wɩɩsɩ bee lugisi nala a kuu ba aa yaa haŋ nihuwobiŋ iriŋ hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Na ma nɩɩ duwoso hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa kpaa tɩya Iziral tɩmma rɛ. Ʋ basɩ tɩya ba rɛ dɩ ba tɩŋ Yesu Krisita dɩ ba bee Wɩɩsɩ mɩɩgɩ marɩ dɔmɔŋ, beewɩya, ʋ rɛ yaa nihuwobiŋ buloŋ Tɩɩna.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: A piili saŋa hʋ Gyɔɔŋ gyɩ aa basɩ Wɩɩsɩ nɩɩfori hʋ wɩya Galili tɩyaŋ, ma buloŋ gyɩŋ wɩbal hʋ gyɩ aa yaa rɛ Iziral paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Wɩya hʋ buloŋ ʋ aa yaa Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ abee Gyuuma paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, ba buloŋ nɛ á na, a yaa ba daŋsɩya diile mɛ. Ba kana ʋ rɛ, a kpaasɩ ʋ mal daagarɩɩ hʋ nyuu tɩyaŋ, ʋ sʋba.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Amɛ ʋ kyɛyɛ boto kyɛɛ, Wɩɩsɩ bɩl kyisi ʋ sʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, a leŋ nala na ʋ.

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: Ka ʋ aa mɩɩgɩ sii hal tɩyaŋ, nal buloŋ daa bɩl na ʋ, see á nala hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya dɩ á yaa ʋ daŋsɩya diile. Á dʋŋ nɛ na ʋ. Ʋ aa sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ mɛ, á beel-ʋ buloŋ laŋŋa di kɩna rɛ.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka ʋ basɩ tɩya ma abee sɩfɩyasɩ dɩ á basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩweliye hʋ kɩ tɩya nala, a kɩ daga ba anɩɩ ʋ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ lɩɩ, dɩ ʋ yaa niweye abee sʋʋnɩ buloŋ sarɩdiil.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Piita ha aa sɩŋ kɩ basɩ wɩya hʋ gɛɛ, dɩ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ ko tuu gyʋʋ nala hʋ buloŋ fa aa gyegile kɩ nɩɩ wɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Ɛɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa yaŋ kpa ʋ Wiyesi Welii hʋ we á tɩyaŋ, ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ʋ kpa ʋ we nala no mɛ tɩyaŋ. Wɩbee rɛ yaŋ sɩ wuwo tɔ nala no dɩ ba ta foo ba wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ?”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka ʋ leŋ ba fo ba wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ Yesu Krisita feŋ tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ ba sʋla Piita, dɩ ʋ hɔŋ ba lee kyɛyɛ baŋmana.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka Piita mɛ mɩɩgɩ ko Gyerusalɛm. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Gyuuma badɔmɔŋ sii kɩ basɩ ʋ wɩya akuu wɩya hʋ ʋ aa yaa nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma tɩyaŋ wɩya.

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba pɩyɛsɩ Piita a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ ɩ mʋ nala hʋ aa bee keri ba pene lee, haalɩ, ɩ beel-ba gba laŋŋɩ di kɩna?”

english_verse: Then Peter told them exactly what had happened:
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita yaŋ piili a basɩ wɩya hʋ aa vala gɛɛ buloŋ a tɩya ba.

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: Ʋ baa, “Ŋ gyɩ we Gyoopa tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ, ŋ hɔŋ kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ. Wɩɩsɩ hʋ kyʋwalɩɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ ŋ aa duwosi, ka ŋ na kɩŋ kɩdɩgɩ dɩ ʋ kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ wagyɛbal aa lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu ko kɩ tuu, ka ba kaŋ ʋ nyʋʋsɩ hʋ banaa tɩyaŋ, a kaŋ ko tuu biŋ ŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka ŋ nɩɩ nal basɩ tɩya ŋ a baa, ‘Piita, sii kpʋ kɩ kyaŋ.’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ baa, ‘Aayɩ, ŋ Tɩɩna, ŋ ha bɩ maakyiye di bisiŋ kɩŋ buloŋ.”’

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ taal hʋ bɩl lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu, a basɩ tɩya ŋ a baa, “Kɩŋ kɛ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa baa ʋ weliye, ta baa dɩ ʋ yaa bisiŋ kɩŋ nɛ.

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Wɩɩ no yaa gɛɛ rɛ naaŋsɩɩnyʋwa bʋto, ka kɩna hʋ bɩl mɩɩgɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩsɩnyuu.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: Haŋ saŋa hʋ tɩɩ rɛ baala boto mɛ ba aa tɩma ŋ lee, ba lɩɩ Sizaariya, dɩ ba mɛ yaŋ ko kɩ pele dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ ŋ fa aa gyʋʋ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ basɩ tɩya ŋ dɩ ŋ ta yaa gyegye, akuu ba aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma wɩya, ka dɩ ŋ tɩŋ ba hal mʋ. Nyɛ rɛ ŋ bee ŋ naabalɩya hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di Gyoopa tɩyaŋ nala badʋ sii kpa mʋ Sizaariya, a mʋ gyʋʋ Kɔniiliyo dɩya.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ma ɛɛ hʋ ʋ aa naa malɩka dɩ ʋ sɩŋ ʋ dɩya tɩyaŋ, a basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ tɩŋ nala dɩ ba mʋ Gyoopa a yɩrɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Siimɔŋ Piita dɩ ʋ ko.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: Dɩ ʋ sɩ basɩ wɩya tɩya ba, aŋ daga ba wɩya hʋ aa sɩ laa ʋ bee ʋ dɩya nala buloŋ ta.

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ŋ aa piili kɩ basɩ wɩya hʋ, Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ ko tuu gyʋʋ ba tɩyaŋ nɛ anɩɩ ʋ gyɩ aa laa sɩya ko gyʋʋ á tɩyaŋ piili hʋ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Wɩɩ no aa ko yaa tɩyaŋ nɛ, ŋ liisi wɩɩ hʋ á Tɩɩna Yesu fa aa basa. Ʋ fa baa, ‘Nɩɩ dʋŋ nɛ Gyɔɔŋ kaŋ kɩ fo nala wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ, amɛ ŋ kɛ sɩ kpa Wiyesi Welii hʋ fo ma wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ.’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Ɛɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ aa yaŋ kpa ʋ Wiyesi Welii hʋ we á tɩyaŋ saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ á aa laa á Tɩɩna Yesu Krisita wɩya di, ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ ʋ kpa ʋ we ba tɩyaŋ mɛ. Mɩyaŋ nɛ yaŋ yaa aŋnɛ, ŋ aa sɩ tɔ Wɩɩsɩ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ʋ ta yaa ʋ wɩkyɛɛlɩɩ.”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Ba aa nɩɩ wɩya no, ba bɩl bɩ wuwo basɩ wɩɩ buloŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ba dannɩ Wɩɩsɩ a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ tɩya nala mɛ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma ŋmanɩɩ rɛ dɩ ba mɛ wuwo bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ ba wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ, aŋ kaŋ mɩɩbol hʋ aa bɩ kaŋ tenii.” Nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di aŋ kɩ laŋŋɩ Aŋtiyɔki tɩyaŋ

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ba aa kpʋʋ Sɩtɩɩvɩŋ hal tɩyaŋ, ɛɛ hʋ ba gyɩ aa dɔgɩsɩ nala hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ gyɩ leŋ ba gyeniŋ buloŋ lɩɩ fá pɩsa mʋ leye rɛ. Ba tɩyaŋ, badɔmɔŋ gyɩ mʋʋ pele Fɔniisɩya abee Saapʋrɔsɩ paalʋʋ rɛ, abee tɔɔ hʋ ba aa yɩrɩ Aŋtiyɔki. Doŋ tɩyaŋ ba basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya a kɩ tɩya Gyuuma hʋ dʋŋ aa we doŋ.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Amɛ nala hʋ badɔmɔŋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di Saapʋrɔsɩ abee Sariini tɩyaŋ sii mʋ Aŋtiyɔki, a basɩ á Tɩɩna Yesu wɩweliye hʋ a kɩ tɩya nala hʋ mɛ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ pɛɛ ba tɩyaŋ nɛ, a sɩŋ ba hal mɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ nala yʋga gyɩ laa Yesu wɩya di a bɩrɩmɩ kɩ tɩŋa ʋ.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ wɩɩ no duwoso gyɩ pele Yesu hatɩnna hʋ aa we Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ. Ba sii tɩŋ Banabaasɩ dɩ ʋ mʋ Aŋtiyɔki na.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ aa mʋwa, ʋ naa ɛɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa pɛɛ nala hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ʋ teŋ buloŋ gyɩ fɩyɛlɩ. Ʋ basɩ wɩya kyagɩlɩ ba weliŋ aŋ baa dɩ ba sɩŋ á Tɩɩna Yesu laadii tɩyaŋ abee ba tɩsɩ buloŋ aŋ ta ko kpa kɩ ta.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Banabaasɩ fa yaa tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩɩna rɛ, a fa laa Wɩɩsɩ di weliŋ. Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ mɛ fa we ʋ tɩyaŋ weliŋ. Ɛɛ nyuniŋ, nala gyɩ yʋga rɛ a ko laa Yesu wɩya di.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Banabaasɩ sii mʋ Taasu, dɩ ʋ kyɛ Sɔɔl lee.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa mʋʋ na ʋ, ʋ kana ʋ, ba buloŋ mɩɩgɩ ko Aŋtiyɔki. Banabaasɩ bee Sɔɔl buloŋ gyɩ hɔŋ doŋ nɛ bɩŋpiliŋ. Ba bee nɩgyamaa hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di aa vala kɩ laŋŋɩ saŋa buloŋ, ba kɩ daga nala hʋ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya. Aŋtiyɔki tɩyaŋ nɛ ba gyɩ piili kaŋ Yesu hatɩnna kɩ yɩrɩ Krisitabiisi.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ nɛ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala badɔmɔŋ mɛ lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm a ko Aŋtiyɔki.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Ba kɩdɩgɩ feŋ nɛ Agabu. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ sii sɩŋ a tɩya Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdarɩ, a baa dɩ losiŋbɔŋ sɩ gyʋʋ dʋnɩya buloŋ.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba kɩyɛsɩ molbiye no ko pɛ dɔmɔŋ, a kpa tɩya Banabaasɩ bee Sɔɔl dɩ ba kaŋ mʋ tɩya Krisitabiisi nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ aa we Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ, Gyuuma paalʋʋ kuworibal feŋ nɛ fa yaa Hɛrɔtɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ mɛ sii piili a kɩ dɔgɩsɩ Krisitabiisi hʋ badɔmɔŋ.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ leŋ ba kaŋ Gyɔɔŋ maana Gyemsi rɛ, a keri ʋ nyuu abee takoobii.

english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ aa na dɩ Gyuuma hʋ teŋ fɩyɛla abee wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa yaa tɩyaŋ, ʋ bɩl leŋ ba kaŋ Piita mɛ tɔ dɩyaŋ. .

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Piita gyɩ we nɩtɔdɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ka Krisitabiisi hʋ aa laŋŋɩ saŋa buloŋ, a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ kɩ tɩya ʋ sɩfɩyaŋ abee ba tɩsɩ buloŋ. Malɩka lɩɩ Piita nɩtɔdɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ tebine, Piita gyɩ pɩŋ laalyuwol hʋ aa deŋ ʋ balɩya tʋtʋʋ, ka ba kaŋ kyɔrʋmɔ balɩya vʋwa ʋ a mʋ mal ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ. Ka laalyuwolo mɛ ha sɩŋ pɔ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ dimbeenyʋwa. Ʋ sɩya rɛ fa sɩ sii gballɩ dɩ Hɛrɔtɩ lɩɩ ʋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ a tɩya bee hʋ nala dɩ ba di ʋ sarɩya.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ malɩka kɩdɩgɩ pirigi ko sɩŋ Piita lee. Nɩtɔdɩya hʋ buloŋ kyaanɩ. Nyɛ rɛ malɩka hʋ ko pɩyɛsɩ Piita vaaŋ tɩyaŋ, a kyisi ʋ, a basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ sii lagɩ. Nyɛ rɛ kyɔrʋmɔ hʋ pirigi yɔrɩmɩ lɩɩ Piita nosi tɩyaŋ a tuu tele.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ malɩka hʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Sii paa ɩ kɩna laalɩ aŋ paa ɩ naatɔbɔ mɛ we. Doŋ nɛ Piita yaa gɛɛ. Malɩka hʋ bɩl basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, ‘Kpa ɩ wagyɛ tɔ, a kɩ tɩŋa ŋ hal.”’

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Ba mʋ gallɩ laalyuwolo hʋ aa sɩŋ sɩya a kɩ deŋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ a bɩl mʋ gallɩ laalyuwolo hʋ badɔmɔŋ mɛ aa sɩŋ hal a mɛ ha kɩ deŋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ, a mʋ pele hɔgɩ boro hʋ aa tɔɔ pagɩbal hʋ aa gyʋʋ bee hʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ boro hʋ suri ʋ tɩɩ tɩyaŋ ba mʋ lɩɩ. Ba lɩɩ kpa ŋmanɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ a vala kɩ mʋ. Ba aa kpa vala mʋhʋ malɩka hʋ leŋ Piita ta doŋ, aŋ mʋ.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Piita hakɩla yaŋ ko, ʋ gyɩma anɩɩ wɩya hʋ aa yaa hʋ yaa wɩtɩɩ rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ baa, “Nyɛ kɛ, ŋ gyɩma anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ rɛ tɩŋ ʋ malɩka dɩ ʋ ko laa ŋ ta Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ nosi tɩyaŋ abee wɩbɔmɔ hʋ buloŋ Gyuuma hʋ fa aa liisi dɩ ba yaa ŋ.”

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Piita aa ko gyɩŋ nyɛ tɩyaŋ, ʋ kpa mʋ Gyɔɔŋ Maakɩ nɩɩna Mɛɛrɩ dɩya. Ʋ mʋʋ doŋ dɩ nɩgyamaa laŋŋa a kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa ko nɩɩ tɩɩna taal, ʋ gyɩma anɩɩ Piita rɛ. Ʋ teŋ buloŋ fɩyɛlɩ kɩŋkaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ bɩl bɩ suro boro hʋ, aŋ mɩɩgɩ fá gyʋʋ basɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa we dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ anɩɩ Piita rɛ sɩŋ gyaŋhala tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba mɩɩgɩ basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ aa yaarɩ rɛ. Ka ʋ ha vɩya aŋ baa dɩ wɩtɩɩ rɛ ʋ kɩ basɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba baa dɩ gɛɛ kɛ Piita dʋma rɛ ʋ na gɛɛ.

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Ba aa basɩ wɩya no buloŋ gɛɛ, ka Piita mɛ ha sɩŋ kɩ ŋmaa boro hʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba ko kaŋ boro hʋ suri dɩ ʋ rɛ sɩna gɛɛ, ba buloŋ nyʋʋsɩ fɩyɛlɩ kɩŋkaŋ.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Piita kaŋ ʋ noŋ viri ba dɩ ba leŋ kyagɩŋsɩ, aŋ basɩ tɩya ba ɛɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩɩ ʋ nɩtɔdɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ʋ baa dɩ ba basɩ wɩɩ no tɩya Gyemsi abee ba nɩkaalɩya hʋ mɛ aa laa Yesu wɩya di. Ʋ aa basɩ wɩya no buloŋ ko teŋ ʋ leŋ ba aŋ mʋ lee kɩdɩgɩ.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩya aa gballa kyɩkyʋwala ba kyɛ Piita lɔlɔ. Ɛɛ rɛ nyʋʋtaaduwo gyʋʋ ba, ba hakɩllɩ buloŋ vʋgɩmɩ, ba kɩ pɩyɛsɩ dɔmɔŋ anɩɩ lee rɛ Piita we?

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ baa dɩ ba kyɛ kaŋ Piita ko. Nyɛ rɛ ba kyɛ ʋ lɔlɔ. Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ marɩ pɩyɛsɩ laalyuwolo hʋ dɩ ʋ gyɩŋ ɛɛ hʋ wɩya hʋ aa vala, aŋ baa dɩ ba kpʋ ba. Ɛɛ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ sii Gyudɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a kpa mʋ Sizaariya, a hɔŋ doŋ. Ɛɛ kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ aa sʋba

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ, Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ gyɩ naa baaŋ nɛ kɩŋkaŋ Taaya bee Siidɔŋ tɩmma nyuu tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii yaa nyʋʋdɩgɩ mʋ dɩ ba na Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ. Nyɛ rɛ ba laa sɩya mʋ na Bilasitu, nal hʋ aa deŋ Kuwori hʋ dɩya, dɩ ʋ pɛ ba tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ laa nyʋwa dɩ ʋ sɩ pɛ ba tɩyaŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ba kpa mʋ Hɛrɔtɩ lee, a sʋla ʋ dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ ba beel-ʋ mɩɩgɩ marɩ dɔmɔŋ. Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ paalʋʋ rɛ Taaya bee Siidɔŋ tɩmma kɩdiiliye fa aa lɩɩ kɩ ko. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ba gyɩ ko dɩ ba beel-ʋ mɩɩgɩ marɩ dɔmɔŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ tɩya ba kyɛɛ dɩ ba ko. Kyɛɛ hʋ aa ko pele, Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ sii laalɩ ʋ kuwori gannɩ a lɩɩ hɔŋ ʋ kuworikpasa nyuu tɩyaŋ a basɩ wɩya kɩ tɩya nala hʋ.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala hʋ buloŋ kyɩɩrɩ yʋga, a kɩ basɩ, “Nihuwobiŋ wɩbasɩɩ daa nyɛ, Wɩɩsɩ rɛ aa basɩ.”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ aa basɩ gɛɛ, Kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ gyɩ bɩ basɩ tɩya ba anɩɩ ʋ bɩ yaa Wɩɩsɩ, aŋ mɩɩgɩ laa Wɩɩsɩ yohuŋ tɩya ʋ tɩɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ, haŋ lagɩlagɩbiye hʋ, malɩka ko tuu ŋmaa ʋ biŋ wɩɩla, kyaaŋkyʋwana gyʋʋ kpa ʋ tɩya buloŋ di ʋ sʋba.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ, dɩ Banabaasɩ bee Sɔɔl mɛ gyɩ teŋ wɩɩ hʋ ba aa ko Gyerusalɛm dɩ ba yaa rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba kaŋ Gyɔɔŋ Maakɩ pɛ ba tɩyaŋ, a mɩɩgɩ mʋ Aŋtiyɔki.

english_verse: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
translation_verse: Krisitabiisi hʋ aa we Aŋtiyɔki tɩyaŋ, ba badɔmɔŋ fa yaa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala rɛ, badɔmɔŋ mɛ fa yaa nala hʋ aa daga Yesu hatɩnna hʋ. Ba fene rɛ nyɛ: Banabaasɩ abee Simiyɔŋ , abee Lusɩya aa lɩɩ Sariini paalʋʋ, abee Sɔɔl, a yaŋ bee Meniyaŋ. .

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ kyɛɛ kɩdɩgɩ, nala no sii vʋwa nyʋwa, a kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ. Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma lɩɩ Banabaasɩ bee Sɔɔl tɩya ŋ. Ŋ lɩɩ ba rɛ dɩ ba tʋŋ ŋ tʋma tɩya ŋ.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba vʋwa nyʋwa, a kpa nosi dɔbɔ ba nyuni tɩyaŋ, a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ba, aŋ na ta ba ba lɩɩ ŋmanɩɩ. Banabaasɩ bee Sɔɔl mʋʋ Saapʋrɔsɩ

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ aa tɩŋ Banabaasɩ bee Sɔɔl gɛɛ, ba sii kpa tuu mʋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ aa sɩŋ mpʋwɔnʋʋ nyʋwa, ba kɩ yɩrɩ Selusɩya. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba sɩŋ kpa nɩɩduworiboribal, a mʋ Saapʋrɔsɩ paalʋʋ. Nɩɩ fa gol Saapʋrɔsɩ paalʋʋ buloŋ nɛ ko baŋ kyeme.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba gɔllɩ Saapʋrɔsɩ paalʋʋ buloŋ, a mʋ pele tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Paafɔsɩ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba gyɩ na daaluliye tɩɩna kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Baa-Yesu. . Ʋ fa yaa Gyuu rɛ, a gyɩ kɩ mʋrɩ nala anɩɩ ʋ yaa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal lɛ.
