translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ma kɩ deŋ ma tɩɩ nyuu weliŋ, aŋ pɛ kɩ deŋ nala hʋ mɛ ʋ Wiyesi Welii hʋ aa kpaa we ma nosi tɩyaŋ. Ma kɩ deŋ Krisitabiisi mɛ nyuu weliŋ anɩɩ pedaal aa kɩ deŋ ʋ piyese nyuu tɩyaŋ gɛɛ. Wɩɩsɩ rɛ kpaa ʋ tɩɩ biye kyal yɔbɔ ma.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ŋ basɩ baa ma kɩ deŋ ma tɩɩ ma nyuu tɩyaŋ gɛɛ. Ma gyɩma anɩɩ ŋ bɩsɩ boto rɛ nyɛ ŋ aa daga ma kɩdɩgɩ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ wɩɩhaa abee tebine buloŋ, a wɩɩ sɩnɩɩl mɛ weliŋ ma wɩya.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: Nyɛ kɛ, ŋ kpaa ma we Wɩɩsɩ nosi tɩyaŋ nɛ. Duwoso hʋ aa daga bɔnyɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa kana abeel-ma, ʋ rɛ sɩ pɛ ma tɩyaŋ dɩ ma kaŋ dee a wuwo sɩŋ ma laadii tɩyaŋ. Ʋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ma na wɩweliye hʋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa yaa biŋ a gbala ʋ nala buloŋ.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Ma tɩɩ gyɩma rɛ anɩɩ ŋ tʋma rɛ weliŋ abee ŋ nosi no, aŋ na na ŋ bee ŋ kyaŋsɩ kɩkyɛɛlɩya buloŋ.

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl aa basɩ wɩya no buloŋ ko teŋ, ʋ beel-ba buloŋ tuu gbinni, ʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ba buloŋ.

english_verse: Everyone cried and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba buloŋ ko guwori ʋ, aŋ kɩ wɩɩ sɩnɩɩl.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: Ba tɩsɩ buloŋ gyɩ kyogo rɛ weliŋ, beewɩya ʋ fa basɩ tɩya ba rɛ anɩɩ ba bɩl bɩ sɩ maakyiye na ʋ. Nyɛ rɛ ka ba buloŋ kpa tɩŋa ʋ tɩyaŋ a mʋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ lee.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ á ta dɔmɔŋ aŋ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ, a vala sɩdeŋ a mʋ lɩɩ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Kɔsɩ. Ʋ sɩgballɩya á bɩl sii doŋ a kpa mʋ Rodisi, a bɩl lɩɩ doŋ mɛ a kpa mʋ Patara.

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ á na nɩɩduworiboribal dɩ ʋ aa mʋ Fɔniisɩya paalʋʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ á gyʋʋ ʋ mʋ.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Á aa mʋʋ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, a na tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Saapʋrɔsɩ. Nyɛ rɛ á gallɩ doŋ a kpa tɩŋ wɩɩpɔsɩɩ noduu dɩɩlaŋ, a mʋ pele Siiriya paalʋʋ, a mʋ sɩŋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Taaya. Doŋ nɛ nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ gyɩ maga dɩ ʋ sɩŋ dɩ ba kuwoni kyʋgɩsɩ hʋ aa we ʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Kyɛyɛ bapɛ no hal tɩyaŋ nɛ, á bɩl lɩɩ doŋ a kpa á ŋmanɩɩ. Baala abee ba haana abee ba biisi kaŋ ma ko lɩɩ dɩ ba we ŋmanɩɩ tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ á buloŋ tuu gbinni nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa tɩyaŋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ.

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ á ta dɔmɔŋ aŋ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ ka ba mɛ mɩɩgɩ mʋ dɩya.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Á bɩl lɩɩ Taaya tɩyaŋ mɛ a kpa á ŋmanɩɩ a mʋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Pitolimiiyasɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ á mʋ kyʋwalɩ Krisitabiisi hʋ aa we doŋ aŋ pɩŋ ba lee.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ á we kyɛyɛ baŋmana, dɩ baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Agabu mɛ lɩɩ Gyudɩya paalʋʋ ko. Ʋ mɛ fa yaa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal lɛ.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ ko á lee, a laa Pɔɔl tɛpɩɩŋbii a vʋwa ʋ tɩɩ naasɩ abee ʋ nosi buloŋ aŋ basɩ baa, Nyɛ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ basɩ, “Gyuuma sɩ kaŋ tɛpɩɩŋbii no tɩɩna a vʋwa ʋ nyɛ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ aŋ kpa ʋ we nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma nosi tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Á aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no, á bee nala hʋ buloŋ fa aa we doŋ sʋla Pɔɔl dɩ ʋ ta mʋ Gyerusalɛm.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl basɩ tɩya ma a baa, “Bee rɛ tɩŋ ma kɩ wii, a leŋ ŋ tɩya buloŋ kyogi nyɛ? Dɩ ba aa rɛ sɩ kana ŋ vʋwa, koo dɩ ba aa rɛ kpʋ ŋ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ mɛ, akuu ŋ Tɩɩna Yesu wɩya, ŋ kɛ laa rɛ.”

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ ko teŋ, á na anɩɩ á bɩ sɩ wuwo tɔ ʋ ŋmanɩɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ á mɛ leŋ aŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ Wɩɩsɩ sɩ leŋ dɩ wɩɩ kɛ buloŋ ʋ aa kyɛ ʋ yaa, kɔnɩ yaa.

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ka á mɛ yaa siri a sii mʋ Gyerusalɛm.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Nala hʋ badɔmɔŋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di a lɩɩ Sizaariya mɛ tɩŋ á hal mʋ, a kaŋ ma mʋ gyʋʋ baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Mineesiŋ a lɩɩ Saapʋrɔsɩ dɩya. Saŋa hʋ ba fa aa piile kɩ basɩ Yesu wɩya kɩ tɩya nala hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ gyɩ laa Yesu wɩya di. Pɔɔl mʋʋ dɩ ʋ na Gyemsi

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: Á gyɩ aa ko pele Gyerusalɛm, Krisitabiisi hʋ gyɩ kaŋ ma weliŋ nɛ.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩgballɩya, á bee Pɔɔl sii mʋ dɩ á na Gyemsi. Á mʋwa dɩ Krisitabiisi nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ buloŋ laŋŋa doŋ.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Pɔɔl kyʋwalɩ ba aŋ basɩ tɩya ba ɛɛ hʋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa pɛɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ ʋ wuwo tʋŋ tʋma nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Ba aa nɩɩ wɩɩ no ko teŋ, ba dannɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋ basɩ tɩya Pɔɔl a baa, “Á nihiyabiye Pɔɔl, ɩ kɛ deŋ na Gyuuma aa maga gɛɛ a laa Yesu wɩya di. Ba pele nala tusi tusi, ka ba buloŋ wuwo kɩ tɩŋ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ buloŋ Moosi fa aa saba biŋ dɩ nala kɩ tɩŋa.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Ba nɩɩ ɩ wɩya rɛ. Ba baa dɩ ɩ aa kɩ daga Gyuuma hʋ buloŋ aa we nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma tɩyaŋ nɛ, aŋ baa dɩ ba vɩya mɩrɩsɩ hʋ buloŋ Moosi fa aa saba biŋ dɩ nala kɩ tɩŋa hʋ mɛ. Aŋ bɩl baa dɩ ɩ baa ba ta kɩ keri ba biisi pene, aŋ ta bɩl kɩ tɩŋ Gyuuma naabaala lesiri hʋ mɛ.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Ba sɩ nɩɩ anɩɩ ɩ ko daha rɛ. Wɩbee rɛ á yaŋ sɩ yaa?

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Wɩɩ kɛ buloŋ á aa sɩ baa ɩ yaa, tɩŋa á nyʋwa. Baala banaa rɛ we daha. Ba dii nyʋwa rɛ Wɩɩsɩ lee aŋ kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba lɩɩ nyʋwa hʋ.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Kaŋ ba dɩ ma buloŋ mʋ dɩ ɩ pɩɩsɩ ɩ bisiŋ ta, aŋ tuŋ molbiye dɩ ba mɛ pɩɩsɩ ba bisiŋ ta aŋ fʋŋ ba nyuni mɛ. Dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, nal buloŋ sɩ tɩŋ gɛɛ tɩyaŋ a gyɩma anɩɩ wɩya hʋ buloŋ ba aa basɩ a mʋ kɩ tile ɩ tɩyaŋ bɩ yaa wɩtɩɩ. Ba sɩ na anɩɩ ɩ tɩɩ mɛ gba aa tɩŋ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ Moosi fa aa saba biŋ dɩ nala kɩ tɩŋa rɛ.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Amɛ Krisitabiisi hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma, á laa sɩya sabɩ teŋ a basɩ tɩya ba rɛ, dɩ ba laa nyʋwa anɩɩ ba bɩl bɩ sɩ kɩ di vʋga kɩna, a ta bɩl kɩ kyaŋ pʋsɩ kyal mɛ, a ta kɩ kyaŋ kɩna aa sʋba pimmiliŋ mɛ, aŋ lɩɩ ba tɩɩ mɛ sɔŋsɔŋ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Pɔɔl kyaŋsɩ hʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ, ʋ sɩya gballɩ, Pɔɔl kaŋ baala hʋ banaa mʋ, ba buloŋ pɩɩsɩ ba bisiŋ ta aŋ gyʋʋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ. Ka ʋ daga Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala kyɛyɛ mɔɔgɛɛ ba aa sɩ pɩɩsɩ ba bisiŋ ta, ka dɩ ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ na kpa kɩna sʋla Wɩɩsɩ. Kyɛyɛ bapɛ rɛ ʋ daga ba. Ba kaŋ Pɔɔl Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Kyɛyɛ bapɛ hʋ aa ko dɩ ʋ pele, Gyuuma badɔmɔŋ aa lɩɩ Esɩya paalʋʋ na Pɔɔl dɩ ʋ we Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba mʋrɩ nɩgyamaa we ba mʋ kaŋ Pɔɔl.

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Wɩɩ hʋ aa tɩŋ ba basɩ gɛɛ rɛ nyɛ: Ba fa naa Ɛfiso tɩmma baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Turofimɔsɩ rɛ dɩ ʋ aa tɩŋ Pɔɔl hal bee hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba kɛ fa liise anɩɩ Pɔɔl kana ʋ gyʋʋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ rɛ.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ bee hʋ nala buloŋ laa taawɛɛ, nala hʋ buloŋ sii tɩŋ dɔmɔŋ a kaŋ Pɔɔl tarɩ lɩɩ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ, aŋ kaŋ dimbeye hʋ tɔ haŋ lagɩlagɩbiye hʋ.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Ba aa lʋga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ gɛɛ rɛ, Roma laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ nɩɩ anɩɩ taawɛɛ rɛ sii Gyerusalɛm buloŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sii lɩɩ laalyuwolo hʋ badɔmɔŋ abee ba nɩhɩyasɩ haŋ lagɩlagɩbiye hʋ a pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ, ba fá mʋ lee hʋ ba aa yaa taawɛɛ hʋ. Nala hʋ aa naa dɩ ʋ bee laalyuwolo hʋ aa ko gɛɛ, ba bɩl bɩ ŋmaa Pɔɔl.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ mʋ kaŋ Pɔɔl, a leŋ ba kaŋ kyɔrʋmɔ balɩya vʋwa ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ pɩyɛsɩ nala hʋ a baa, “Kɩbee rɛ yaa nal no tɩɩna? Bekiŋ wɩɩ rɛ ʋ yaa kyogi?”

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba buloŋ kɩ yaa kyagɩŋsɩ, ba badɔmɔŋ baa nyɛ rɛ, badɔmɔŋ mɛ baa dɩ ɛɛ daa, dɩ nyɛ rɛ. Nala hʋ fa aa yaa kyagɩŋsɩ gɛɛ, laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ bɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ aa paalɩ tɩŋ ba kɩ yaa gɛɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ leŋ ʋ nala hʋ kaŋ Pɔɔl mʋ we laalyuwolo dɩɩduwo hʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Ba aa mʋ pele dɩya hʋ lɩtɩŋgyɩnɩɩ, nala hʋ kaŋ Pɔɔl kɩ tarɩ a kɩ lʋga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ laalyuwolo hʋ kpa ʋ kyʋŋ.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Nala hʋ buloŋ tɩŋa ʋ hal aŋ yaa kyagɩŋsɩ kɩ basɩ, “Ma kpʋ ʋ.” Pɔɔl basɩ wɩya dɩ ʋ laa ʋ tɩɩ

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Saŋa hʋ ba aa gyɩŋ sal hʋ a kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba gyʋʋ dɩya hʋ, Pɔɔl sʋl laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ a baa dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ ʋ basɩ wɩɩ tɩya ʋ. Nyɛ rɛ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ pɩyɛsɩ Pɔɔl a baa, “Ɛɛ kɛ ɩ aa nɩɩ Giriki taal lɛ?

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl baa, “Aayɩ, ŋ yaa gyuu rɛ a lɩɩ Taasu, Silisɩya paalʋʋ tɔbal hʋ. Ŋ aa sʋla ɩ rɛ dɩ ɩ leŋ dɩ ŋ basɩ wɩya tɩya nala no.”

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ tɩya ʋ ŋmanɩɩ. Pɔɔl sɩŋ dɩya hʋ saŋgyɩŋ hʋ tɩyaŋ, aŋ kaŋ ʋ nosi viri dɩ nala hʋ buloŋ leŋ kyagɩŋsɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba buloŋ yaa fʋɩɩ, ka Pɔɔl piili kɩ basɩ wɩya Gyuuma taal tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Ba aa nɩɩ dɩ Pɔɔl basɩ wɩya ba tɩɩ taal tɩyaŋ gɛɛ, ba buloŋ yaa fʋɩɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl basɩ tɩya ba a baa,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Ŋ yaa gyuu rɛ. Taasu aa we Silisɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba lʋla ŋ. Ŋ gyɩ yaa haŋbiye rɛ ka ba kana ŋ ko Gyerusalɛm daha, ŋ waa daha tɩyaŋ. Gamaliyɛl rɛ dɩyɛsɩ ŋ. Ʋ gyɩ daga ŋ á naabaala lesiri abee ba ŋmaŋsɩ buloŋ nɛ abee sɩfɩyasɩ. Wɩya hʋ ʋ aa daga ŋ, ŋ mɛ gyɩ kpaa ba tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ rɛ abee ŋ tɩya buloŋ, anɩɩ ma kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ aa we daha gyɩnaŋ.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Ŋ fa tɩŋ naasɩ dɔgɩsɩ nala hʋ aa tɩŋ Yesu ŋmanɩɩ hʋ rɛ kɩŋkaŋ, a gba kpʋ ba badɔmɔŋ. Ba baala abee haana buloŋ nɛ ŋ leŋ ba kasɩ vʋwa, aŋ kasɩ ba tɔ dɩɩsɩ tɩyaŋ mɛ.

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl bɩl baa, “Ŋ aa lɩɩ kpa Damaasiku ŋmanɩɩ kɩ mʋ, a ko kpaga bee hʋ anɩɩ wɩɩhaa saŋa gɛɛ sek, pʋlʋŋbal kɩdɩgɩ pirigi lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu a kyaanɩ we ŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Ŋ tuu tele taŋha aŋ nɩɩ nal taal dɩ ʋ aa basɩ wɩya ŋ lee a baa, ‘Sɔɔl, Sɔɔl! Bee rɛ tɩŋ ɩ kɩ dɔgɩsɩ ŋ nyɛ?”’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ mɛ pɩyɛsɩ, “Kɩbee rɛ yaa ɩ, ŋ Tɩɩna?” Taal hʋ basɩ baa, “Mɩyaŋ nɛ yaa Yesu aa lɩɩ Nazarɛti, nal hʋ ɩ aa dɔgɩsɩ hʋ.”

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Ŋ bee nala hʋ fa aa val hʋ, ba gyɩ naa kyaanʋʋ hʋ rɛ aŋ fa bɩ nɩɩ nal hʋ aa basɩ wɩya ŋ lee taal.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ pɩyɛsɩ, “Ŋ Tɩɩna, ŋ yaa bee?” Ŋ Tɩɩna bɩl basɩ baa, “Sii mʋ Damaasiku. Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba sɩ daga ɩ wɩɩ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩɩ ɩ dɩ ɩ yaa.”

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Pʋlʋŋ hʋ aa kyʋʋlɩ ŋ sɩya gɛɛ, ŋ bɩl bɩ wuwo dɩ ŋ na. Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ bee nala hʋ fa aa val hʋ kaŋ ŋ noŋ tɩyaŋ a tarɩ ŋ mʋ Damaasiku.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: Baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ fa we doŋ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Ananiyasɩ. Ʋ fa aa fá Wɩɩsɩ rɛ kɩŋkaŋ aŋ fa kɩ tɩŋ mɩrɩsɩ hʋ Moosi fa aa saba biŋ dɩ nala kɩ tɩŋa mɛ. Gyuuma hʋ buloŋ aa we Damaasiku tɩyaŋ fa aa tɩya ʋ gyɩrɩma rɛ weliŋ.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ ko sɩŋ ŋ logiŋ tɩyaŋ a basɩ tɩya ŋ a baa, “Ŋ naabiye Sɔɔl, kaŋ ɩ sɩya suri! Haŋ lagɩlagɩbiye hʋ ŋ sɩya pirigi suri, ŋ wuwo na ʋ.”

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Ananiyasɩ basɩ tɩya ŋ a baa, “Wɩɩsɩ hʋ á bee á naabaala aa tɩŋ, ʋ rɛ lɩɩ ɩ dɩ ɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa kyɛ dɩ ɩ yaa. Ʋ leŋ ɩ na Wɩɩsɩ Tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩɩna hʋ rɛ a nɩɩ ʋ taal mɛ ʋ aa basɩ wɩya tɩya ɩ.

english_verse: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
translation_verse: Ɩ sɩ yaa ʋ daŋsɩya tɩɩna, a basɩ tɩya nala wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa nɩya aŋ pɛ na mɛ.

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ mɩɩgɩ mʋ Gyerusalɛm a gyʋʋ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya a kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ. Saŋa hʋ ŋ aa kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ,

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: ŋ na á Tɩɩna Yesu anɩɩ duwoso tɩyaŋ gɛɛ. Ʋ basɩ tɩya ŋ a baa, “Sii lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ lagɩ, beewɩya nala hʋ aa we daha bɩ sɩ laa nyʋwa laa ŋ wɩya hʋ ɩ aa sɩ basɩ tɩya ba di.”

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ŋ Tɩɩna basɩ tɩyaŋ a baa, “Sii daha, ŋ sɩ tɩma ɩ dɩ ɩ mʋ libolii, nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma lee.”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Ka ba kɩ yaa kyagɩŋsɩ, aŋ kaŋ ba gannɩ kɩ pigisi aŋ yaa taŋha kɩ yuwo.

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Roma laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ basɩ baa dɩ laalyuwolo hʋ kaŋ Pɔɔl gyʋʋ laalyuwolo dɩɩduwo hʋ. Ʋ baa dɩ ba vɩɩrɩ ʋ aŋ pɩyɛsɩ na wɩɩ hʋ aa tɩŋ nala no buloŋ lɩɩ ʋ hal aŋ kɩ yaa kyagɩŋsɩ nyɛ.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba vʋwa ʋ dɩ ba vɩɩrɩ. Saŋa hʋ ba aa yaa siri dɩ ba vɩɩrɩ Pɔɔl hʋ rɛ ʋ pɩyɛsɩ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ fa aa sɩŋ doŋ a baa, “Ʋ yaa ŋmanɩɩ rɛ dɩ ba vɩɩrɩ nal hʋ aa yaa Roma tɩɩna rɛ, dɩ ba bɩ dii ʋ sarɩya a daga ʋ wɩkyogii koo?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Ʋ aa nɩɩ wɩɩ hʋ Pɔɔl aa basa, ɛɛ rɛ ʋ mɛ mɩɩgɩ mʋ ʋ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ lee a basɩ tɩya ʋ baa, “Deŋ na wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa kyɛ dɩ ɩ yaa, baal hʋ yaa Roma tɩɩna rɛ.”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ tɩɩ sii kpa mʋ Pɔɔl lee a pɩyɛsɩ ʋ, “Ɩ yaa Roma tɩɩna rɛ? Basɩ tɩya ŋ.” Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl baa dɩ waa.

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ mɛ basɩ baa, “Ŋ mɛ yaa kɩdɩgɩ rɛ. Ŋ kɛ ta tuŋ molbiye rɛ yʋga aŋ na ko yaa Roma tɩɩna.” Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl baa, “Ba paalɩ lʋla ŋ ŋ yaa Roma tɩɩna rɛ.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ nala hʋ fa aa kyɛ dɩ ba vɩɩrɩ Pɔɔl aŋ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ wɩya hʋ buloŋ mɩɩgɩ fasɩ kɩ lɩɩ hal mʋhʋ mʋhʋ. Laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ aa ko gyɩma anɩɩ Pɔɔl yaa Roma tɩɩna rɛ, ka ʋ leŋ ba kaŋ kyɔrʋmɔ vʋwa ʋ gɛɛ, kambɩŋ mɩɩgɩ kana ʋ mɛ tɩɩ rɛ. Ba kaŋ Pɔɔl mʋ bee hɩyarɩ sɩya

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ kɩ kyɛ dɩ ʋ kɔnɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ aa tɩŋ Gyuuma hʋ kɩ basɩ Pɔɔl wɩya. Ʋ sɩya aa gballa, ʋ leŋ ba kana ʋ puri, a leŋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya hʋ buloŋ ko laŋŋɩ. Ba aa laŋŋa ko teŋ, ʋ kaŋ Pɔɔl lɩɩ sɩgɩ ba sɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ ba aa yɩrɩ Ananiyasɩ basɩ tɩya nala hʋ aa sɩŋ kpaga Pɔɔl, a baa dɩ ba ŋmaa ʋ kyaŋkpaŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Ba aa ŋmaa ʋ gɛɛ, Pɔɔl mɛ tʋʋsɩ ʋ a baa, “Na ʋ aa kɩɩ gɛɛ anɩɩ gyee ba aa yaa tʋwɔl bʋgɩ! Ɩ aa yaŋ leŋ ba ŋmaa ŋ nyɛ, ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ mɛ sɩ ŋmaa ɩ. Na ɩ hɔnɔ dɩ ɩ kpa mɩrɩsɩ hʋ Moosi aa saba biŋ dɩ á kɩ tɩŋa rɛ, a di ŋ sarɩya, aŋka ɩ baa dɩ ba ŋmaa ŋ. Wɩya no tɩɩ daa ɩ kɩ kyogi gɛɛ?”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Nala hʋ aa sɩŋ kpaga Pɔɔl pɩyɛsɩ ʋ a baa, “Wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ daa ɩ kɩ tʋʋsɩ gɛɛ?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Pɔɔl bɩl baa, “Ŋ naabalɩya, ma kɩna sʋma rɛ. Ba saba Wɩɩsɩ tenni tɩyaŋ nɛ anɩɩ ʋ bɩ maga dɩ á basɩ wɩbɔmɔ a kɩ tɩya nal hʋ aa deŋ nala. Ŋ bɩ gyɩma anɩɩ ʋ rɛ yaa á wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ.”

english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl aa deŋ nala hʋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ na anɩɩ ba badɔmɔŋ yaa nala hʋ ba aa yɩrɩ Sadusiima rɛ. Ba badɔmɔŋ mɛ yaa Farasiima. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ faasɩ basɩ tɩya nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya hʋ a baa, “Ŋ naabalɩya, ŋ yaa Farasii rɛ, ŋ kuwo mɛ yaa Farasii. Ŋ laa di rɛ anɩɩ nala hʋ aa sʋba sɩ mɩɩgɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ a kaŋ mɩɩbol. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ba kɩ di ŋ sarɩya gɛɛ.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ wɩɩ no, nyʋʋtaaduwo gyʋʋ Farasiima hʋ abee Sadusiima hʋ, ba porigi dɔmɔŋ.

english_verse: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
translation_verse: Sadusiima baa dɩ dɩ nal lɛ sʋba, ʋ bɩl bɩ sɩ wuwo sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ a kaŋ mɩɩbol, aŋ bɩl basɩ baa dɩ malɩkasɩ mɛ tuwo, dɩ nɩdʋŋsɩ mɛ tuwo. Amɛ Farasiima hʋ kɛ baa dɩ wɩya no buloŋ we doŋ nɛ.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ nala hʋ buloŋ sii a faasɩ kɩ yaa kyagɩŋsɩ. Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla hʋ badɔmɔŋ aa we Farasiima paati hʋ tɩyaŋ sii a faasɩ kaŋ wɩya hʋ kɩ tarɩ a baa, “Á kɛ bɩ naa wɩɩ hʋ baal no aa yaa kyogi. Dɩ nɩdɩma koo malɩka rɛ kɔnɩ basɩ wɩya hʋ tɩya ʋ, á bɩ sɩ wuwo gyɩma.”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba marɩ kaŋ wɩɩ hʋ kɩ kpa dɔmɔŋ, lee hʋ buloŋ nɩma. Kambɩŋ kaŋ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ anɩɩ ba sɩ kaŋ Pɔɔl tarɩ pʋ̃ʋ̃. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ leŋ ʋ laalyuwolo hʋ gyʋʋ nala hʋ tɩyaŋ a lorigi laa Pɔɔl, a kana ʋ kaŋ mʋ laalyuwolo dɩɩduwo hʋ.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Lee aa ko bɩl, haŋ tebine hʋ tɩɩ á Tɩɩna Yesu ko Pɔɔl lee, a sɩŋ ʋ logiŋ, aŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Kana ɩ tɩɩ. Ʋ maga dɩ ɩ di ŋ daŋsɩya rɛ a basɩ ŋ wɩya Roma tɩyaŋ anɩɩ ɩ aa basɩ ŋ wɩya Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.” Gyuuma aa vʋʋlɩ dɩ ba kpʋ Pɔɔl

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii mʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ abee bee hʋ nɩhɩyasɩ lee a basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Á ŋmɩyɛsa rɛ anɩɩ dɩ ámaa rɛ ko bɩ kpʋʋ Pɔɔl, á bɩ sɩ di kɩna koo a nyʋwa nɩɩ mɛ.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ma bee nala hʋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya nɩhɩyasɩ, ma tɩma dɩ ba mʋ basɩ tɩya Roma laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ Pɔɔl ko ma lee. Ma fugo baa dɩ ma aa kyɛ dɩ ma marɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ weliŋ nɛ, a gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ ʋ kɔnɩ aa yaa. Á sɩ yaa siri, dɩ ʋ rɛ leŋ dɩ ʋ kɩ ko, ʋ bɩ sɩ pele ma lee, ka dɩ á kpʋ ʋ ŋmanɩɩ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl dɩya toluu biibaal mɛ nɩɩ ba aa vʋʋlɩ wɩɩ hʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sii mʋ gyʋʋ laalyuwolo dɩɩduwo hʋ, a basɩ wɩɩ hʋ tɩya Pɔɔl.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Pɔɔl yɩrɩ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ kɩdɩgɩ a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Kaŋ bapʋwasɩbiye no mʋ ma nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ lee, ʋ kaŋ wɩɩ rɛ dɩ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ kana ʋ mʋ ba nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ lee a basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Pɔɔl, nal hʋ ba aa kaŋ tɔ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ yɩrɩ ŋ a basɩ tɩya ŋ baa dɩ ŋ kaŋ bapʋwasɩbiye no ko ɩ lee, dɩ ʋ kaŋ wɩɩ rɛ dɩ ʋ basɩ tɩya ɩ.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ kaŋ biye hʋ noŋ tɩyaŋ, ba fasɩ lɩɩ mʋhʋ, ʋ fɩyɛlɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ dɩ wɩbee rɛ ʋ kana dɩ ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ?

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ baa, “Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ vʋʋla rɛ dɩ ba basɩ tɩya ɩ dɩ ɩ kyiye leŋ dɩ Pɔɔl ko nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya lee. Ba sɩ fugo baa dɩ ba aa kyɛ dɩ ba marɩ pɩyɛsɩ ʋ weliŋ nɛ a gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ ʋ kɔnɩ aa yaa.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Ʋ aa basɩ gɛɛ ko teŋ, nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ basɩ tɩya bapʋwasɩbiye hʋ baa, “Ta ko basɩ wɩɩ no ɩ aa basɩ tɩya ŋ, a tɩya nal buloŋ.” Ɛɛ rɛ ka ʋ ta biye hʋ, ʋ mʋ. Ba kaŋ Pɔɔl mʋ Roma nɩhɩyawʋ Fɛlisi sɩya

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Baal no ŋ aa leŋ ba kaŋ kɩ ko ɩ lee nyɛ, Gyuuma rɛ fa kana ʋ dɩ ba kpʋ. Ba aa kyɛ dɩ ba yaa ɛɛ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, ŋ nɩɩ anɩɩ ʋ yaa Roma tɩɩna rɛ. Nyɛ rɛ ŋ kaŋ ŋ laalyuwolo mʋ, á lorigi laa ʋ ba nosi tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Ŋ fa aa kyɛ dɩ ŋ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa yaa kyogi rɛ. Nyɛ rɛ ŋ kana ʋ mʋ ba nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ aa hɔŋ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya lee.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Ba aa pɩyɛsɩ ʋ wɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ, ŋ na anɩɩ ʋ bɩ kyogo wɩɩ buloŋ a maga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ, koo dɩ ba kana ʋ tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ. Wɩɩ hʋ ba aa baa dɩ ʋ yaa kyogi mɩɩgɩ mʋ kɩ tile ba tɩɩ sɩŋsɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Ba basɩ tɩya ŋ anɩɩ Gyuuma badɔmɔŋ vʋʋla rɛ dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ŋ baa dɩ ba kana ʋ ko tɩya ɩ lagɩ. Ka ŋ yaŋ basɩ tɩya ba anɩɩ ba ko ɩ lee, a daga wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa yaa kyogi ba, a tɩya ɩ.”

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩya aa gballa, laalyuwolo hʋ aa val naasɩ hʋ, mɩɩgɩ ko ba dɩɩduwo hʋ, aŋka gyagyɩnna hʋ kana ʋ mʋ Sizaariya.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Ba aa mʋʋ pele doŋ, ba kpa Pɔɔl tɩya ba paalʋʋ hʋ didenni hʋ aŋ kpa teŋ hʋ mɛ pɛ tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Fɛlisi karɩmɩ teŋ hʋ ko teŋ, aŋ mɩɩgɩ pɩyɛsɩ Pɔɔl lee hʋ ʋ aa lɩya. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ baa dɩ ʋ lɩɩ Silisɩya paalʋʋ rɛ. Ʋ aa nɩɩ gɛɛ,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: ʋ basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Dɩ ɩ nala hʋ rɛ ko ko, ŋ sɩ nɩɩ wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa kana dɩ ɩ basɩ, aŋka dɩ ŋ na di ɩ sarɩya.” Ɛɛ rɛ ka ʋ leŋ ba kaŋ Pɔɔl mʋ we kuwori Hɛrɔtɩ aa saa ʋ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ, a leŋ laalyuwolo kɩ pɔ ʋ doŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Ʋ kyɛyɛ bɔnɔŋ hal tɩyaŋ nɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyawʋ ba aa yɩrɩ Ananiyasɩ abee bee hʋ nɩhɩyasɩ badɔmɔŋ abee sarɩdiil kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Tɛɛtulusi, sii kpa mʋ Sizaariya, dɩ ba na Roma nɩhɩyawʋ Fɛlisi. Ba mʋ kpa wɩya hʋ ba aa baa dɩ Pɔɔl kyogo, a biŋ Fɛlisi sɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Tɛɛtulusi rɛ ba gyɩ laa sɩya yɩrɩ dɩ ʋ ko lɩɩ daga wɩɩ hʋ Pɔɔl aa kyogo. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ piili a baa, “Ŋ nɩhɩyawʋ, ɩ wɩgyʋŋ nyuniŋ, ɩ wuwo kɩ deŋ á paalʋʋ no weliŋ nɛ, a leŋ á hɔŋ abee laaŋfɩya. Ɩ bɩl marɩ wɩya mɛ rɛ yʋga á paalʋʋ no tɩyaŋ, ba yaa wɩweliye tɩya ma.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: Á naa dɩ baal no yaa wɩweel lɛ. Gyuuma aa we dʋnɩya logiŋ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, ʋ aa mʋ doŋ nɛ, a kaŋ ba we ba kɩ kaa kaasɩ. Ʋ mɛ rɛ yaa Nazarɛti tɩmma paati hʋ sɩlaal.

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ paalʋʋ hʋ didenni hʋ tɩya Pɔɔl mɛ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ʋ mɛ ko lɩɩ basɩ wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa kana. Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl baa, “Ŋ tɩɩ gyɩma rɛ anɩɩ ɩ aa di sarɩya taŋha no tɩyaŋ ʋ bɩsɩ bɩ kpaga daha. Ŋ teŋ yaŋ fɩyɛla rɛ abee ŋ aa sɩ basɩ wɩya a laa ŋ tɩɩ ɩ sɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Ɩ tɩɩ sɩ wuwo pɩyɛsɩ na, anɩɩ saŋa hʋ ŋ aa ko mʋ Gyerusalɛm dɩ ŋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ, ʋ ha bɩ para kyɛyɛ fi abee balɩya.

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Gyuuma bɩ naa dɩ ŋ bee ba bee nal buloŋ kaŋ wɩya kɩ kpa dɔmɔŋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba mɛ bɩl bɩ naa dɩ ŋ we kaasɩ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩsɩ tɩyaŋ mɛ koo lee kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Wɩɩ buloŋ tuwo ba aa sɩ wuwo kpa daga anɩɩ wɩya hʋ ba aa baa dɩ ŋ yaa kyogi mɛ kɔnɩ yaa wɩtɩɩ rɛ.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Wɩɩ hʋ ŋ aa sɩ basɩ tɩya ɩ anɩɩ ʋ yaa wɩtɩɩ rɛ nyɛ: Ŋ kɛ gyɩma anɩɩ ŋmanɩɩ no ŋ aa tɩŋ nyɛ, á naabaala Wɩɩsɩ hʋ rɛ ŋ kɩ kyʋwalɩ gɛɛ. Beewɩya, mɩrɩsɩ hʋ buloŋ Moosi aa biŋ tɩya ma, abee wɩya hʋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala aa saba, ŋ mɛ laa ba buloŋ dɩ rɛ. Amɛ ba kɛ baa dɩ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ ŋ aa tɩŋ hʋ yaa wɩnyɩyɛl lɛ.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: Ŋ mɛ laa di rɛ anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kyisi niweliye abee nɩbɔmɔ buloŋ sʋʋ tɩyaŋ anɩɩ nala no buloŋ aa sɩŋ daha mɛ aa laa di gɛɛ.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Ŋ aa lʋgɩ rɛ saŋa buloŋ dɩ ŋ gyɩma ŋ hakɩla tɩyaŋ anɩɩ ŋ bɩ kaŋ wɩkyogii buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ abee nihuwobiŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Ŋ lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ pɩɩsɩ rɛ, a pel bɩŋgyamaa, aŋ na mɩɩgɩ mʋ doŋ, dɩ ŋ kaŋ kɩna mʋ tɩya ŋ nala ka dɩ ŋ pɛ kpʋ pʋsɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ mɛ.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ tɩyaŋ nɛ ba na ŋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ tɩyaŋ. Saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ ba aa naa ŋ dɩ ŋ pɩɩsɩ ŋ bisiŋ teŋ nɛ. Nɩgyamaa mɛ daa ŋ kaŋ gyʋʋ doŋ. Ŋ bɩ wee kaasɩ buloŋ mɛ.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Gyuuma badɔmɔŋ mɛ aa lɩɩ Esɩya paalʋʋ mɛ rɛ fa we doŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ fa kaŋ wɩɩ buloŋ abeel-ŋ, ʋ fa maga dɩ ba tɩɩ ko basɩ rɛ daha tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Dɩ ɛɛ mɛ ko daa, leŋ dɩ nala no aa we daha basɩ wɩɩ hʋ ba mɛ aa naa dɩ ŋ yaa kyogi, saŋa hʋ ba aa kana ŋ mʋ nɩhɩyasɩ hʋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya sɩya.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ ŋ aa yaa rɛ nyɛ: Ba sɩya tɩyaŋ, ŋ fa nɔsɔ basɩ tɩya ba rɛ a baa, ‘Gyɩnaŋ ma aa di ŋ sarɩya rɛ, dɩ ma kpa kyogisi tɩya ŋ, akuu ŋ aa laa di anɩɩ sʋʋnɩ sɩ mɩɩgɩ sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ a kaŋ mɩɩbol wɩya.”’

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Fɛlisi aa nɩɩ gɛɛ, ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ma sii. See dɩ ŋ leŋ dɩ Lisiyasɩ, laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ ko, ka dɩ ŋ na dɩ wɩɩ no sarɩya.” Fɛlisi fa gyɩŋ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ Pɔɔl aa tɩŋ wɩya rɛ weliŋ, ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ʋ basɩ gɛɛ.

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Ʋ leŋ laalyuwolo hʋ kɩdɩgɩ kɩ deŋ Pɔɔl, aŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ dɩ ʋ kana ʋ tɔ aŋ leŋ dɩ ʋ kɩ na wiyese, dɩ ʋ tɩya ʋ kyaŋsɩ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ba ko kɩ na ʋ. Dɩ ʋ rɛ aa kyɛ kɩŋ mɛ, dɩ ba wuwo pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ abee haŋ kɩbalɩɩ hʋ. Pɔɔl sɩŋ Fɛlisi bee Durusila sɩya tɩyaŋ

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ bɩ pɩɩsa ka Fɛlisi bee ʋ haaŋ Durusila mɛ ko doŋ. Durusila fa yaa Gyuu rɛ. Nyɛ rɛ Fɛlisi tɩma yɩrɩ Pɔɔl dɩ ʋ ko. Pɔɔl aa ko, ʋ leŋ ʋ basɩ Yesu Krisita laadii memii a tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl gyɩ basɩ wɩya mɛ rɛ a mʋ kɩ tile wɩweliye yayɩ tɩyaŋ, a bee ɛɛ ɩ aa sɩ wuwo kaŋ ɩ tɩɩ, a bɩl basɩ kyɛɛ hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa sɩ di dʋnɩya nala sarɩya wɩya mɛ. Ʋ aa basɩ wɩya no, kambɩŋ buloŋ kaŋ Fɛlisi. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ baa dɩ ʋ leŋ mɔɔgɛɛ aŋ kɩ mʋ, dɩ amɛ dɩ ʋ rɛ ko na sɩnɩɩ, dɩ ʋ sɩ bɩl yɩrɩ ʋ.

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Ʋ kɛ fa bɩɩna anɩɩ Pɔɔl sɩ kaŋ molbiye ko kpala ʋ rɛ. Ɛɛwɩya, ʋ gyɩ aa kɩ yɩrɩ ʋ bʋlɩya bʋlɩya, dɩ ba kɩ basɩ wɩya.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Ʋ bɩsɩ balɩya hal tɩyaŋ, baal kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Pɔɔkɩyɔsɩ Fɛsitusi rɛ fa mɩɩgɩ ko laa Fɛlisi nyuu, a kɩ deŋ paalʋʋ hʋ. Fɛlisi fa aa kyɛ dɩ Gyuuma hʋ kyo ʋ rɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ʋ gyɩ leŋ ba ha leŋ Pɔɔl nɩtɔdɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Fɛsitusi aa ko laa paalʋʋ hʋ, ʋ hɔŋ Sizaariya tɩyaŋ kyɛyɛ boto rɛ aŋ sii kpa mʋ Gyerusalɛm.

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ vʋʋla rɛ dɩ ba kyeŋ kpʋ Pɔɔl ŋmanɩɩ tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ba gyɩ kɩ sʋla Fɛsitusi dɩ ʋ leŋ dɩ Pɔɔl ko Gyerusalɛm gɛɛ.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Fɛsitusi mɩɩgɩ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Sizaariya tɩyaŋ nɛ ba kaŋ Pɔɔl tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ. Ŋ mɛ sɩ mɩɩgɩ mʋ doŋ kyɛɛ no buloŋ.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ma leŋ dɩ ma nɩhɩyasɩ tɩŋ ŋ hal dɩ á buloŋ mʋ Sizaariya. Wɩɩ hʋ ma aa liise anɩɩ baal hʋ yaa kyogi, dɩ ba basɩ tɩya ŋ doŋ, dɩ ŋ nɩɩ.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Fɛsitusi gyɩ we Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ kyɛyɛ kyori koo fi rɛ aŋ na mɩɩgɩ mʋ Sizaariya. Ʋ sɩgballɩya, ʋ mʋ hɔŋ sarɩya hʋ lidiilii tɩyaŋ aŋ leŋ ba kaŋ Pɔɔl ko.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl aa ko, Gyuuma hʋ aa lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm ko buloŋ ko sɩŋ gol ʋ kyige aŋ baa dɩ wɩya hʋ Pɔɔl aa kyogo yʋga rɛ kɩŋkaŋ. Amɛ ba gyɩ bɩ wuwo daga anɩɩ wɩya hʋ ba aa baa dɩ ʋ kyogo yaa wɩtɩɩ rɛ.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl mɛ basɩ wɩya dɩ ʋ laa ʋ tɩɩ, a baa, “Ŋ bɩ kyogi wɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ Gyuuma naabaala ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ŋ bɩ kyogi Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩbal hʋ sɩna buloŋ mɛ. Ŋ bɩl bɩ yaa wɩɩ buloŋ mɛ kyogi Roma paalʋʋ buloŋ kuworibal hʋ.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl baa, “Aayɩ, paalʋʋ hʋ buloŋ kuworibal sarɩya lidiilii tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ sɩŋ nyɛ. Ɛɛwɩya, daha rɛ maga dɩ ma di ŋ sarɩya. Ɩ tɩɩtɩɩ gyɩma rɛ weliŋ anɩɩ ŋ bɩ yaa wɩɩ buloŋ kyogi Gyuuma.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Dɩ mɩyaŋ nɛ fa yaa wɩɩ kyogi, a maga dɩ ba kpʋ ŋ, ŋ fa aa laa rɛ, amɛ dɩ wɩya hʋ buloŋ ba aa basa yaa wɩnyɩyɛl kɛ, nal buloŋ bɩ kaŋ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ʋ kpa ŋ we ba nosi tɩyaŋ dɩ ba kpʋ ŋ. Ŋ aa sʋl lɛ, dɩ ɩ leŋ dɩ ŋ mʋ kuworibal hʋ lee dɩ ʋ di ŋ sarɩya.”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Fɛsitusi bee ʋ nɩhɩyasɩ na dɔmɔŋ. Ka ʋ basɩ tɩya Pɔɔl a baa, “Na ɩ baa ɩ sɩ mʋ kuworibal hʋ lee dɩ ʋ di ɩ sarɩya. Ŋ mɛ kɔnɩ yaŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ɩ mʋ.” Fɛsitusi basɩ Pɔɔl wɩya tɩya Kuwori Agɩrɩpa abee Bɛɛnisi

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Kyɛyɛ baŋmana hal tɩyaŋ, kuwori kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Agɩrɩpa bee ʋ dɩya toluu kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Bɛɛnisi ko dɩ ba kyʋwalɩ Fɛsitusi Sizaariya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Ba aa we doŋ kyɛyɛ baŋmana, Fɛsitusi basɩ Pɔɔl wɩya buloŋ tɩya ba a baa, “Baal kɩdɩgɩ rɛ we daha. Fɛlisi rɛ fa leŋ ba kana ʋ tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ. Ʋ ha we doŋ.

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: Ŋ aa mʋ Gyerusalɛm, Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ ko basɩ wɩya hʋ ba aa baa dɩ ʋ yaa kyogi ba rɛ, a tɩya ŋ, aŋ baa dɩ ŋ di ʋ sarɩya a kpa kyogisi tɩya ʋ.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ŋ basɩ tɩya ba anɩɩ á Roma tɩmma bɩ sɩ laa nyʋwa kpa kyogisi tɩya nal, aŋ kpa tɩɩna we ʋ dɔmɔ nosi tɩyaŋ, see dɩ á buloŋ hɔnɔ, dɩ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ basɩ ʋ nyʋwa.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba sii kpa ko daha. Ba aa ko, ŋ bɩ kaŋ wɩɩ hʋ pɩɩsɩ. Ʋ sɩgballɩya buloŋ nɛ, ŋ sii mʋ sarɩya hʋ lidiilii aŋ leŋ ba kaŋ baal hʋ ko lɩɩ.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ bee Pɔɔl fa aa kaŋ wɩya kɩ kpa dɔmɔŋ hʋ, sii sɩŋ dɩ ba daga wɩya hʋ ba aa baa dɩ Pɔɔl yaa kyogi. Wɩbɔmɔ hʋ ŋ kɛ fa aa bɩɩna anɩɩ ba sɩ baa dɩ ʋ yaa, ba bɩ basa ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Ŋ fa bɩ gyɩŋ ɛɛ hʋ buloŋ ŋ aa sɩ yaa, a gyɩŋ wɩɩ no naasɩ. Nyɛ rɛ ŋ pɩyɛsɩ Pɔɔl anɩɩ ʋ sɩ laa nyʋwa a mʋ Gyerusalɛm dɩ ba di ʋ sarɩya doŋ tɩyaŋ?

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Amɛ Pɔɔl bɩ laa nyʋwa, aŋ sʋla ŋ dɩ ŋ leŋ dɩ ba deŋ ʋ a kana ʋ mʋ kuworibal hʋ lee, dɩ ʋ di ʋ sarɩya. Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ leŋ ba kɩ deŋ ʋ. Dɩ mɩyaŋ nɛ ko na ŋmanɩɩ ŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ba kana ʋ mʋ kuworibal hʋ lee dɩ ʋ di ʋ sarɩya.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ kuwori Agɩrɩpa basɩ tɩya Fɛsitusi anɩɩ ʋ tɩɩ kyo dɩ ʋ mɛ nɩɩ baal hʋ nyʋwa rɛ. Nyɛ rɛ Fɛsitusi basɩ tɩya ʋ anɩɩ ʋ kyiye sɩ nɩɩ ʋ wɩbasɩɩ. Ba kaŋ Pɔɔl mʋ Kuwori Agɩrɩpa bee Bɛɛnisi sɩya

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩya aa gballa, Kuwori Agɩrɩpa bee Bɛɛnisi sii mʋ sarɩya hʋ lidiilii. Ba gyɩ laalɩ ba kuwori gannɩ rɛ, ba sii ŋmaa kuwori kɩna tɩŋ ba hal, ba mʋ gyʋʋ dɩɩbal hʋ. Ba bee laalyuwolo nɩhɩyasɩ abee bee hʋ nɩhɩyasɩ buloŋ ko gyʋʋ hɔŋ dɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba buloŋ aa hɔnɔ ko teŋ, Fɛsitusi leŋ ba kaŋ Pɔɔl ko gyʋʋ.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Fɛsitusi baa, “Kuwori Agɩrɩpa, ɩ bee nala hʋ buloŋ aa we daha, ma na baal no. Ʋ wɩya rɛ tɩŋ Gyuuma hʋ aa we daha abee Gyuuma hʋ buloŋ aa lɩɩ Gyerusalɛm, ko kana ŋ bagɩna tɩyaŋ, a kɩ yaa kyagɩŋsɩ a baa dɩ ʋ bɩ maga dɩ ʋ we ʋ mɩɩbol tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Ŋ kɛ bɩ naa wɩɩ hʋ ʋ aa yaa kyogi, a maga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ. Amɛ ʋ tɩɩ rɛ sʋla ŋ anɩɩ ʋ sɩ mʋ kuworibal hʋ lee dɩ ʋ di ʋ sarɩya. Ŋ mɛ yaŋ sɩ leŋ dɩ ʋ mʋ.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Amɛ ŋ paalɩ bɩ kyaasɩ gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ buloŋ ŋ aa sɩ sabɩ tɩya kuworibal hʋ baal no wɩya. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ŋ kana ʋ ko ma lee dɩ ma mɛ nɩɩ ʋ nyʋwa. Ɩ kuwori Agɩrɩpa lee rɛ ŋ yaŋ paalɩ kana ʋ ko. Ŋ bɩɩna anɩɩ dɩ ɩ mɛ gyʋʋ wɩɩ no tɩyaŋ, ɩ sɩ wuwo na wɩɩ dɩ ŋ wuwo sabɩ teŋ tɩya kuworibal hʋ.

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Kuwori Agɩrɩpa, wɩya hʋ ŋ aa bɩ yaa, ba aa rɛ Gyuuma baa dɩ ŋ yaa rɛ. Ɛɛwɩya, ŋ sɩ baa dɩ ŋ nyuu sʋma rɛ gyɩnaŋ, ŋ aa sɩ wuwo basɩ wɩya ɩ sɩya tɩyaŋ a laa ŋ tɩɩ,

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: beewɩya ɩ kɛ paalɩ gyɩŋ Gyuuma lesiri buloŋ nɛ abee ba wɩkaŋtarɩya buloŋ. Ŋ yaŋ aa sʋla ɩ rɛ dɩ ɩ di kenyiri aŋ gyegili nɩɩ ŋ nyʋwa.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Dɩ ba aa rɛ fa sɩ laa nyʋwa basɩ wɩtɩɩ, ba tɩɩ gyɩma rɛ anɩɩ piilii hʋ tɩyaŋ buloŋ, Farasiima paati hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ŋ we. Paati no nala rɛ fa aa tɩŋ á wɩɩkyʋwal ŋmanɩɩ hʋ abee wɩtɩɩ a te nɩkaalɩya hʋ buloŋ.

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: Ŋ kuwo kpɩyana, á Iziral tɩmma dimbeye fi abee balɩya hʋ buloŋ laa di rɛ anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ sɩ yaa wɩɩ no tɩya ma. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ á yaa sɩfɩyaŋ a kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩɩhaa bee tebine buloŋ. Ŋ mɛ aa laa wɩya no dɩ wɩya rɛ tɩŋ, Gyuuma baa dɩ ŋ kyogo rɛ, aŋ kɩ basɩ ŋ wɩya gɛɛ.

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Bee rɛ tɩŋ ma kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bɩ sɩ wuwo laa di anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kyisi nala hʋ aa sʋba dɩ ba sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ?

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ yaa Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ. Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ fa tɩya ŋ ŋmanɩɩ rɛ, ŋ kasɩ Wɩɩsɩ nala yʋga a tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ. Dɩ ba aa rɛ fa ko kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba kpʋ ba mɛ, ŋ aa laa nyʋwa rɛ ba kpʋ ba.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Ŋ vala dɔgɩsɩ ba rɛ yʋga Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwaldɩɩsɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, a kɩ lʋga dɩ ba basɩ baa ba bɩl bɩ laa Yesu Krisita wɩya di. Ŋ baaŋ gyɩ siye rɛ kɩŋkaŋ ba nyuu tɩyaŋ. Ŋ paalɩ kile ba lugo mʋ tɔnɩ hʋ badɔmɔŋ aa tuwo Gyuuma paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a dɔgɩsɩ ba doŋ tɩyaŋ mɛ.” Pɔɔl daga ɛɛ aa yaa ʋ ko laa Yesu wɩya di Kpambɩsɩ

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Wɩɩ no tɩɩ wɩya, ŋ gyɩ sii mʋ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ lee rɛ, ba tɩya ŋ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ŋ mʋ Damaasiku.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Ŋ kuwo kpɩyana, ŋ aa mʋ ŋmanɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, anɩɩ wɩɩhaa keserige saŋa gɛɛ, ŋ na pʋlʋŋ dɩ ʋ lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu a kyaanɩ we ŋ bee nala hʋ fa aa val tɩyaŋ. Ʋ gyɩ kyaana te wɩɩpɔsɩɩ.

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ŋ pɩyɛsɩ, ‘Aŋnɛ rɛ, Tɩɩna?’ Ɛɛ rɛ Tɩɩna hʋ baa, ‘Mɩyaŋ Yesu rɛ, nal hʋ ɩ aa tɩŋ naasɩ kɩ dɔgɩsɩ hʋ.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: Amɛ sii sɩŋ. Ŋ kaŋ ŋ tɩɩ ko daga ɩ rɛ, dɩ ŋ lɩɩ ɩ, dɩ ɩ tʋŋ tʋma kɩ tɩya ŋ, a yaa ŋ daŋsɩya tɩɩna, a basɩ kɩ tɩya nala, ɩ aa naa ŋ nyɛ gyɩnaŋ, abee wɩya hʋ ŋ aa sɩ maakyiye kpa daga ɩ.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Ŋ sɩ tɩma ɩ, dɩ ɩ mʋ Iziral tɩmma lee abee nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma buloŋ lee. Dɩ ba aa rɛ ko kɩ dɔgɩsɩ ɩ, ŋ sɩ laa ɩ ta ba nosi tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: Ŋ sɩ tɩma ɩ dɩ ɩ mʋ leŋ dɩ ba sɩya suri, a kaŋ ba dɩ ba bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ bilhuu tɩyaŋ a ko gyʋʋ pʋlʋŋ tɩyaŋ, a leŋ dɩ ba lɩɩ Sɩtaanɩ nosi tɩyaŋ a ko Wɩɩsɩ lee. Dɩ ba aa rɛ yaa gɛɛ, aŋ laa ŋ wɩya di, Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kpa ba wɩbɔmɔ buloŋ kyɛ ba, ka dɩ ba wuwo na lee nala hʋ Wɩɩsɩ aa lɩya tɩyaŋ.”’ Pɔɔl basɩ ʋ tʋma wɩya

english_verse: King Agrippa, I obeyed this vision from heaven.
translation_verse: “Ɛɛ wɩya kuwori, ŋ bɩ vɩya taal hʋ aa lɩɩ wɩɩsɩnyuu.

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Doŋ nɛ ŋ piili basɩ Wɩɩsɩ wɩya Damaasiku tɩyaŋ abee Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ abee Gyudɩya paalʋʋ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, abee nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma tɩyaŋ mɛ. Ŋ basɩ tɩya ba rɛ dɩ ba bɩrɩmɩ lɩɩ ba wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ aŋ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ, aŋ kɩ yaa wɩya hʋ aa daga anɩɩ ba kɔnɩ bɩrɩma lɩɩ ba wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Wɩɩ no wɩya rɛ tɩŋ Gyuuma kana ŋ Wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ a kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba kpʋ ŋ gɛɛ.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Amɛ abee gyɩnaŋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ ha aa pɛ ŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ŋ wuwo sɩŋ daha a kɩ basɩ wɩya no buloŋ ŋ aa yaa daŋsɩya, a kɩ tɩya ma, ma nɩbala abee ma nɩbiisi buloŋ. Wɩɩ no ŋ aa basɩ nyɛ, ʋ tɩɩtɩɩ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala abee Moosi buloŋ fa basɩ anɩɩ ʋ kɔnɩ sɩ yaa.

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nala no fa basa rɛ anɩɩ Krisita hʋ sɩ na tʋwara, a sʋba, aŋ laa sɩya sii sʋʋ tɩyaŋ nala hʋ buloŋ aa sʋba tɩyaŋ. A bɩl baa dɩ Krisita no tɩyaŋ nɛ Gyuuma abee nala hʋ aa bɩ yaa Gyuuma buloŋ sɩ tɩŋa a na Wɩɩsɩ kyaanʋʋ hʋ aa sɩ laa ba ta.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Pɔɔl aa basɩ wɩya dɩ laa ʋ tɩɩ gɛɛ, Fɛsitusi nɔsɩ ʋ, aŋ basɩ tɩya ʋ baa, “Pɔɔl, ɩ aa yaarɩ rɛ dʋ? Ɩ wɩgyʋŋ kɩŋkaŋ hʋ rɛ leŋ ɩ kɩ yaarɩ gɛɛ.”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Pɔɔl baa, “Ŋ kuwo kpɩyana, ŋ kɛ paalɩ bee yaarɩ. Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ kɩ basɩ.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Kuwori Agɩrɩpa, ɩ gyɩŋ wɩya no buloŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ ŋ wuwo kɩ basɩ ba abee nyuduwo gɛɛ. Ŋ gyɩma rɛ anɩɩ wɩya no buloŋ tɩyaŋ wɩɩ buloŋ tuwo ɩ aa bɩ gyɩma, beewɩya wɩya no aa yaa kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bɩ yaa wɩfaŋɩɩ.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Kuwori Agɩrɩpa, ɩ laa Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala hʋ wɩbasɩya dɩ rɛ? Ŋ gyɩma anɩɩ ɩ laa di rɛ.”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ kuwori Agɩrɩpa pɩyɛsɩ Pɔɔl a baa, “Ɩ liise anɩɩ lagɩlagɩbiye no ɩ teŋ sɩ wuwo leŋ dɩ ŋ laa Yesu wɩya di a kɩ tɩŋa ʋ koo?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl baa, “Dɩ lagɩlagɩbiye no rɛ, koo dɩ lagɩlagɩbiye no daa, ŋ kɛ wɩɩsʋlɩɩ rɛ yaa dɩ ɩ bee nɩkaalɩya hʋ buloŋ aa gyegile kɩ nɩɩ ŋ taal no gyɩnaŋ, dɩ ma ko laa Yesu wɩya di anɩɩ ŋ aa laa ʋ wɩya di gɛɛ. Amɛ ŋ bee sʋla Wɩɩsɩ dɩ ba kaŋ ma kɛ we kyɔrʋmɔ tɩyaŋ anɩɩ mɩyaŋ nyɛ.”

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: Ba aa lɩɩ lɩgyalɩɩ ko teŋ, ba basɩ kɩ tɩya dɔmɔŋ a baa, “Baal no bɩ kyogo wɩɩ buloŋ a maga dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ koo dɩ ba kana ʋ tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Kuwori Agɩrɩpa basɩ tɩya Fɛsitusi a baa, “Dɩ baal no fa bɩ sʋla ŋmanɩɩ anɩɩ ʋ sɩ mʋ kuworibal hʋ lee dɩ ʋ di ʋ sarɩya rɛ, ŋ kɛ fa aa baa ɩ kpa ʋ ta rɛ.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba vʋʋlɩ dɩ á gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro a mʋ Itali paalʋʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ba kpa Pɔɔl bee nɩtɔdɩya nala hʋ badɔmɔŋ a we laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Gyulɩyɔsɩ noŋ tɩyaŋ. Gyulɩyɔsɩ fa yaa nɩhɩyawʋ rɛ Roma paalʋʋ kuworibal hʋ laalyuwolo tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Á mʋ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboribal kɩdɩgɩ aa lɩɩ Adɩramɩtɩyam, dɩ ʋ mɛ yaŋ kɩ yaa siri dɩ ʋ mʋ tɔnɩ hʋ aa we mpʋwɔnʋʋ hʋ nyʋwa Esɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ. Baal kɩdɩgɩ mɛ rɛ fa we á tɩyaŋ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Arisitaakusi. Ʋ fa lɩɩ Tesaloniika aa we Masidooniya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩgballɩya rɛ á mʋ pele tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Siidɔŋ. Gyulɩyɔsɩ fa kaŋ bɔnyɛ bee Pɔɔl lɛ weliŋ, a tɩya ʋ ŋmanɩɩ ʋ mʋ kɩ na ʋ kyaŋsɩ, dɩ ʋ rɛ aa kyɛ kɩŋ, dɩ ʋ kyaŋsɩ hʋ wuwo pɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ abee haŋ kɩŋ hʋ.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ á kpala tɩŋ Silisɩya abee Pamfilɩya paalʋʋ nyʋwa, a mʋ pele lee kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Maara, a we Liisɩya paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Doŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ na nɩɩduworiboribal kɩdɩgɩ mɛ dɩ ʋ lɩɩ Alizaŋdiriya a kɩ mʋ Itali. Ʋ leŋ á gyʋʋ ʋ.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Á fɩyɛlɩ sɩya duwori gɛɛ rɛ deŋdeŋ kyɛgyamaa, a na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ, aŋ yaŋ na ko kpaga tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Kinidusi. Puwo hʋ gyɩ bɩ laa di á tɩŋ doŋ. Nyɛ rɛ á bɩl kpala tɩŋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ nɩɩ aa golo baŋ kyeme, ba kɩ yɩrɩ Kireti, a ko pele lee kɩdɩgɩ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Salmooni, a kpala tɩŋ tɔɔ hʋ logiŋ puwo aa tuwo.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: Á aa kpala tɩŋ nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa gɛɛ, a naa tʋwara rɛ weliŋ aŋ na ko sɩŋ lee kɩdɩgɩ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Nɩɩduworiborisi Lɩsɩŋwelii. Doŋ fa kpaga tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Laasɩya rɛ.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Á hɔŋ doŋ nɛ a pɩɩsɩ mʋhʋ, Gyuuma kyɛbal hʋ ba aa kpa pʋsɩ lɩɩ wɩɩsʋlɩɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ dɩ ʋ kpa ba wɩbɔmɔ kyɛ ba hʋ ko parɩ baŋ. Haŋ saŋa hʋ mɛ nɩɩ duworii aa hayɛ rɛ weliŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl basɩ wɩya kyagɩlɩ ba a baa,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Ŋ naabalɩya, ŋ naa anɩɩ á ŋmanɩɩ hʋ valɩɩ sɩ kperi ma. Ma leŋ dɩ á hɔŋ daha. Dɩ ámaa rɛ baa á sɩ mʋ, á nɩɩduworiboro hʋ bee á kyʋgɩsɩ hʋ buloŋ sɩ kyogi, ka dɩ á badɔmɔŋ mɛ sʋba.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Amɛ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ fa bɩ gyegile nɩɩ Pɔɔl nyʋwa hʋ, aŋ gyegili nɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ nɩhɩyawʋ abee nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩɩna nyʋwa.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba baa dɩ lee hʋ bɩ weliye abee ba aa sɩ we doŋ yʋʋ no tɩyaŋ. Ba gyeniŋ buloŋ fa kyo dɩ ba gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ rɛ, a ta kɩ lʋga dɩ ba wuwo mʋ pele tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Finiisi a we doŋ yʋʋ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Finiisi fa yaa nɩɩduworiborisi lɩsɩnɩɩ rɛ a we Kiriti paalʋʋ tɩyaŋ. Nɩɩduworiborisi lɩsɩnɩɩ no fa yaa fuwobal goloŋ nɛ a kyaasɩ wɩɩpɔsɩɩ noduu dɩɩlaŋ abee wɩɩgyʋʋyɩ nogɔbɔ dɩɩlaŋ. Puwo luro fuwobal hʋ tɩyaŋ

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ puwo fʋʋfʋʋbiye kɩdɩgɩ luri a lɩɩ wɩɩpɔsɩɩ noduu dɩɩlaŋ. Nala hʋ kɛ fa bɩɩna anɩɩ ba sɩ na ŋmanɩɩ a vala anɩɩ gɛɛ ba fa aa liisi ba tɩsɩ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Nyɛ rɛ ba kaŋ hɔgɩbal hʋ ba aa kaŋ kyaga nɩɩduworiboro hʋ sɩgɩ, a lɩɩ we nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ aŋ duwori kpala ko tɩŋ Kiriti bee nyʋwa gɛɛ baŋ.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ á tɩŋ bee kɩdɩgɩ ba aa yɩrɩ Kawʋda, doŋ mɛ buloŋ nɛ nɩɩ fa gol a ko baŋ kyeme. Doŋ puwo kɛ fa siyese mʋhʋ rɛ. Doŋ tɩyaŋ mɛ, á gyɩ naa tʋwara rɛ kɩŋkaŋ aŋ na wuwo kaŋ nɩɩduworiboribiye hʋ á fa aa kpaa vʋwa nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ hal.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba tarɩ nɩɩduworiboribiye hʋ ko gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ tɩyaŋ a kaŋ ŋmasɩ vʋwa ʋ mal nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ tɩyaŋ kpaŋkpaŋ, aŋ vʋwa nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ mɛ dɩ ʋ ta ko yɔrɩmɩ. Kambɩŋ fa kaŋ ba rɛ, beewɩya ba kɛ fa bɩɩna anɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ sɩ yɩ taŋha Libiya mpʋwɔnʋʋ hʋ nyʋwa tɩyaŋ nɛ, beewɩya doŋ nɩɩ bɩ luŋiye. Nyɛ rɛ ba kaŋ gannɩ hʋ ba fa aa kaŋ vʋwa nɩɩduworiboro hʋ nyuu puri ta ba tuu, aŋ leŋ puwo hʋ kaŋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ kɩ vala.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Puwo hʋ gyɩ faasɩ dɔŋ nɛ weliŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ sɩgballɩya ba piili kuwoni kyʋgɩsɩ hʋ aa we nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ a yuwo kɩ ta.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩya bɩl aa gballa, ba kuwoni nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩɩ kɩna badɔmɔŋ mɛ a yuwo ta.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Á gyɩ naa kyɛgyamaa rɛ dɩ wɩɩsɩ bɩ pɔsɔ, kyɩŋwʋlɩya mɛ bɩ lɩya. Ka puwo hʋ mɛ ha marɩ kɩ dɔŋ kɩŋkaŋ. Ɛɛ hʋ puwo hʋ gyɩ aa marɩ kɩ dɔŋ hʋ, á gyɩ bɩl bɩ liise anɩɩ á sɩ waa.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ gyɩ pɩɩsa dɩ ba bɩ dii kɩna. Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ŋ naabalɩya, ʋ fa maga dɩ ma gyegili nɩɩ ŋ nyʋwa hʋ rɛ. Dɩ mamaa rɛ fa gyegile nɩɩ ŋ nyʋwa, dɩ á fa hɔŋ Kiriti tɩyaŋ, á kɩna no buloŋ aa kyogo nyɛ, ba fa bɩ sɩ kyogi. Kɩna hʋ mɛ buloŋ aa nyʋgɩsa mɛ fa bɩ sɩ nyʋgɩsɩ.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Amɛ ŋ aa sʋl ma, ma kaŋ ma tɩɩ aŋ kaŋ bembii mɛ. Á nɩɩduworiboro hʋ dʋŋ nɛ sɩ kyogi, amɛ ma kɛ kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ bɩ sɩ sʋba.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ hʋ aa tɩŋa ŋ, ŋ mɛ kɩ kyʋwalɩ ʋ, ʋ malɩka dɩya ko ŋ lee rɛ tebine,

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: a basɩ tɩya ŋ a baa, ‘Pɔɔl, ta leŋ dɩ kambɩŋ kana ɩ. Ɩ sɩ mʋ sɩŋ kuworibal hʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ. Wɩɩsɩ sɩ kuu ɩ wɩya a leŋ ɩ bee nala hʋ buloŋ aa we nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ buloŋ kaŋ mɩɩbol.’

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Amɛ puwo no sɩ yigi ma mʋ ta taŋha biye kɩdɩgɩ nɩɩ aa golo ko baŋ tɩyaŋ.”

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba keri hɔgɩbala hʋ buloŋ ba fa aa kaŋ kyaga boro hʋ a lo we nɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ba kaŋ ŋmasɩ hʋ mɛ ba fa aa kaŋ kɩ vʋwa kuunyiŋdaabiisi hʋ ba aa kaŋ kɩ kuwosi boro hʋ mɛ puri. Ba bɩl marɩ kaŋ gannɩ hʋ mɛ ba aa kaŋ vʋwa boro hʋ sɩya tɩyaŋ ta ba gyɩŋ vʋwa, dɩ puwo wuwo kaŋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ vala mʋ nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ nala hʋ fa aa duwori boro hʋ lɩɩ kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba fá, aŋ vɩya nɩkaalɩya hʋ bee boro hʋ ta. Ba lɩɩ nɩɩduworiboribiye hʋ a we nɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ, a fugo kɩ yaa gɛɛ anɩɩ ba aa kyɛ dɩ ba lɩɩ hɔgɩnɩ hʋ ba aa kaŋ kyaga nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ sɩya rɛ.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Kambɩŋ fa kaŋ ba rɛ anɩɩ ba aa sɩ mʋ ŋmaa bʋyɩ wɩya. Ɛɛ rɛ ba kuwoni hɔgɩnɩ hʋ ba aa kaŋ kyaga nɩɩduworiboro hʋ sɩgɩ. Ba banaa rɛ ba kuwoni nɩɩduworiboro hʋ hal a kyaga ʋ sɩgɩ, aŋ kɩ sʋla Wɩɩsɩ dɩ sɩya gballɩ dɩ ba na pʋlʋŋ.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl basɩ tɩya laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ abee laalyuwolo hʋ a baa, “Dɩ nala hʋ aa duwori boro hʋ ko lɩɩ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ, ma kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ mɛ bɩ sɩ waa.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl aa basɩ gɛɛ, laalyuwolo hʋ keri ŋmasɩ hʋ ba fa aa kaŋ vʋwa nɩɩduworiboribiye hʋ a leŋ nɩɩ hʋ kpa ʋ kɩ mʋ.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ŋ aa sʋla ma rɛ dɩ ma di kɩna. Dɩ mamaa rɛ dii kɩna, ma sɩ kaŋ dee a wuwo laa ma tɩɩ a kaŋ mɩɩbol. Wɩɩ buloŋ bɩ sɩ yaa ma kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl aa basɩ gɛɛ ko teŋ, ʋ kpa paanʋʋ, a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ ba buloŋ sɩya tɩyaŋ aŋ lɔgɔ kɩ kyaŋ.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ ba buloŋ kaŋ ba tɩɩ aŋ di kɩdiiliye hʋ.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Ba buloŋ aa dii kɩna hʋ vɔgɔ, ba kuwoni mɩya hʋ fa aa we nɩɩduworiboro hʋ tɩyaŋ a ta, ka dɩ boro hʋ fʋŋɩsɩ. Nɩɩduworiboribal hʋ wara

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Ʋ sɩgballɩya, á ko pele dogimo ka nala hʋ aa duwori boro hʋ bɩ gyɩŋ lee hʋ buloŋ ba aa wee. Ba na nɩɩ-goloŋ kɩdɩgɩ, ka nɩɩ hʋ mɛ aa we doŋ bɩ gyalɩya, ka nɩɩ hʋ mɛ buloŋ bɩ luŋiye. Ba fa bɩɩna ba sɩ wuwo kaŋ nɩɩduworiboro hʋ lɩɩ nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa rɛ.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Amɛ boro hʋ fa fáá mʋ gyʋʋ bumbugulo badɔmɔŋ nɩɩ aa kulo biŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ sɩya mʋʋrɩ gyʋʋ, ʋ bɩ wuwo dɩ ʋ vala, aŋ ka puwobal hʋ leŋ nɩɩyigil ko ŋmaa boro hʋ hal warɩ tala tala.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ laalyuwolo hʋ vʋʋlɩ dɩ ba kpʋ nɩtɔdɩya nala hʋ buloŋ, dɩ ba kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ ta ko duwori kyol fá nyʋgɩsɩ.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Amɛ laalyuwolo nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ fa bee kyɛ dɩ ba kpʋ Pɔɔl. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba baa dɩ ba ta yaa gɛɛ. Aŋ baa dɩ nala hʋ buloŋ aa we boro hʋ tɩyaŋ a gyɩŋ nɩɩ duworii, dɩ ba laa sɩya gyaa tuu duwori mʋ nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa,

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: aŋka dɩ nɩkaalɩya hʋ mɛ tuu paa daaboriye hʋ bee daawarɩya hʋ, dɩ a mɛ kaŋ duwori kyol. Nyɛ rɛ á buloŋ yaa a lɩɩ nɩɩ hʋ nyʋwa abee laaŋfɩya.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl mʋ paa daasɩ a ko we diŋ hʋ tɩyaŋ, dɩ dʋŋ we daasɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ. Ɛɛ rɛ nʋŋ kaŋ dʋŋ hʋ, ʋ ko lɩɩ a dʋŋ-pɔ Pɔɔl noŋ tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Nala hʋ aa we doŋ na dɩ dʋŋ hʋ dʋŋ-pɔ Pɔɔl noŋ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ, ba basɩ kɩ tɩya dɔmɔŋ a baa, “Ʋ kɩɩ nɩɩ baal no yaa nɩkpʋʋl lɛ. Ʋ wuwo lɩɩ nɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ aŋ ha kaŋ mɩɩbol, amɛ ʋ wɩbɔmɔ rɛ ha aa dɩ ʋ gɛɛ.”

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Ba gyɩ aa gyegile rɛ dɩ ba na, dʋŋ hʋ aa dʋŋ Pɔɔl gɛɛ dɩ doŋ fa sɩ ŋmala, koo dɩ Pɔɔl fa sɩ lɩɩ tele a sʋba ba sii tɩyaŋ. Ba gyegili lɔlɔ, aŋ mɩɩgɩ baa dɩ Pɔɔl yaa tɔdʋŋ nɛ.

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Bee hʋ kuwori feŋ nɛ fa yaa Pubiliyɔsɩ. Ʋ bagɩsɩ mɛ fa kpaga doŋ nɛ. Ʋ mɛ gyɩ kaŋ ma nɩhɔrɩ weliŋ kyɛyɛ boto, ʋ dɩya tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Dɩ Kuwori hʋ kuwo mɛ fa aa wɩɩlɩ, a pɩŋ dɩya tɩyaŋ. Fɩyɛlʋʋ abee hamɔlʋŋ nɛ fa kana ʋ. Ɛɛ rɛ Pɔɔl gyʋʋ na ʋ dɩya tɩyaŋ a kpa ʋ nosi dɔbɔ ʋ tɩyaŋ a kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ tɩya ʋ, ʋ na laaŋfɩya.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl fa aa yaa gɛɛ, wɩɩla tɩmma hʋ buloŋ fa aa we doŋ ko Pɔɔl lee rɛ ʋ tɩɩbɩ ba buloŋ, ba na laaŋfɩya.

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Á gyɩ we doŋ nɛ peŋsi boto aŋ na sii lɩɩ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro kɩdɩgɩ aa lɩɩ Alizaŋdiriya. Nɩɩduworiboro no feŋ nɛ fa yaa Tɔdʋŋ-daalɩyanɩ. Boro no gyɩ sɩŋ Mɔlta fuwo hʋ nyʋwa tɩyaŋ nɛ, a lɩɩ yʋʋ saŋa a mʋ pele puwo saŋa.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Á bɩl sii lɩɩ doŋ a mʋ gyʋʋ nɩɩduworiboro, a duwori mʋ tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Riigyiyɔm. Ʋ sɩya aa gballa, puwo bɩl piili kɩ dɔŋ, a lɩɩ wɩɩpɔsɩɩ noduu dɩɩlaŋ. Ʋ kyɛyɛ balɩya kyɛɛ rɛ á ko pele tɔɔ kɩdɩgɩ ba kɩ yɩrɩ Putiyoli.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Á gyɩ na Krisitabiisi badɔmɔŋ nɛ doŋ. Ba baa dɩ á hɔŋ ba lee kyɛyɛ bapɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ á hɔŋ. Nyɛ rɛ á vala pɩŋ ŋmanɩɩ tɩyaŋ gɛɛ, a mʋ pele Roma.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Á nɩmmabalɩya hʋ aa laa Yesu wɩya di Roma tɩyaŋ nɩɩ á wɩya anɩɩ á aa ko rɛ. Ba ko kyeŋ ma libolii tɩyaŋ. Ba badɔmɔŋ gyɩ kyeŋ ma Apiya Yɔbɔ hʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ. Ka ba badɔmɔŋ mɛ kyeŋ ma lee hʋ ba aa yɩrɩ Dɩɩsɩ boto nɩhʋwala aa pɩŋ doŋ. Pɔɔl aa naa ba, ʋ teŋ buloŋ gyɩ pollo rɛ, ʋ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ. Pɔɔl we Roma tɩyaŋ

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Á aa mʋʋ pele Roma, ba tɩya Pɔɔl ŋmanɩɩ rɛ dɩ ʋ kɩ gyʋʋ ʋ dʋŋ dɩya, ka ba leŋ laalyuwolo hʋ kɩdɩgɩ kɩ deŋ ʋ.

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Ʋ kyɛyɛ boto kyɛɛ, Pɔɔl yɩrɩ Gyuuma nɩhɩyasɩ aa we bee hʋ tɩyaŋ ba ko laŋŋɩ. Ba aa laŋŋa gɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Ŋ naabalɩya Iziral tɩmma, ŋ bɩ yaa wɩɩ buloŋ kyogi á nala. Ŋ bɩ kyogo á naabaala sɩŋsɩ mɛ. Ka ba kana ŋ tɔ dɩya tɩyaŋ Gyerusalɛm tɩyaŋ, a bɩl kpa ŋ we Roma tɩmma mɛ nosi tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ Roma tɩmma di ŋ sarɩya, a na dɩ ŋ bɩ kyogo wɩɩ buloŋ a maga dɩ ba kpʋ ŋ. Ɛɛwɩya, ba fa kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba kpa ŋ ta.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Nyɛ rɛ tɩŋ ŋ baa ŋ sɩ na ma dɩ á basɩ wɩya gɛɛ. Nal hʋ wɩya Iziral tɩmma aa kaŋ bayɩyɛla anɩɩ ʋ sɩ ko, ʋ wɩya rɛ tɩŋ ba kaŋ kyɔrʋmɔ no vʋwa ŋ nyɛ.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl aa basɩ gɛɛ, ba basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Á bɩ laa teŋ buloŋ a lɩɩ Gyudɩya paalʋʋ a mʋ kɩ tile ɩ tɩyaŋ. Á nɩmmabalɩya kɩdɩgɩ mɛ bɩ lɩɩ doŋ ko a basɩ ɩ wɩbɔŋ.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Amɛ á gyɩma anɩɩ lee kɛ buloŋ nala aa kɩ basɩ wɩbɔmɔ rɛ a mʋ kɩ tile paati hʋ tɩyaŋ ɩ aa wee. Ɛɛ rɛ tɩŋ á kɩ kyɛ dɩ á gyɩŋ wɩɩ hʋ ɩ aa kana.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Ɛɛ rɛ ba tɩya Pɔɔl kyɛɛ. Kyɛɛ hʋ aa ko pele, nala yʋga sii ko lee hʋ Pɔɔl aa gyʋʋ. A yaŋ piili ʋ kyɩkyʋwala buloŋ a kaŋ kɩ mʋ ʋ dɩdaana, Pɔɔl basɩ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ wɩya gɛɛ rɛ a tɩya nala abee sɩfɩyasɩ. Ʋ gyɩ kpaa wɩya hʋ Moosi aa bine abee wɩya hʋ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaala aa saba Wɩɩsɩ teŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ, a daga nala hʋ anɩɩ Yesu Krisita wɩya hʋ ʋ aa basɩ yaa wɩtɩɩ rɛ. Aŋ kɩ lʋga dɩ ba laa wɩya hʋ di.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Nala hʋ aa nɩɩ wɩya hʋ ʋ aa basɩ, ba badɔmɔŋ laa di, ka ba badɔmɔŋ mɛ bɩ laa di.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Amɛ wɩdɩgɩ rɛ Pɔɔl gyɩ ko basɩ, nyʋʋtaaduwo gyʋʋ ba, ba sii pɩsa. Wɩɩ hʋ ʋ gyɩ aa basa rɛ nyɛ. Ʋ baa, “Wɩya hʋ Wɩɩsɩ Wiyese Welii hʋ aa tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋdaal Azaaya tɩyaŋ a basɩ tɩya ma naabaala hʋ yaa wɩtɩɩ rɛ.

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: Ʋ fa basɩ baa, ‘Mʋ nala no lee a basɩ tɩya ba baa, Ma sɩ gyegili nɩɩ wɩya abee sɩfɩyasɩ, aŋ bɩ sɩ gyɩŋ ba memiye. Ma sɩ kyirigi ma sɩya aŋ bɩ sɩ na kɩŋ buloŋ mɛ,

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Pɔɔl gyɩ hɔŋ Roma tɩyaŋ nɛ bɩsɩ balɩya, a laa dɩya kɩ gyʋʋ, aŋ kɩ tuŋ. Nal buloŋ aa ko ko dɩ ʋ na ʋ, ʋ aa kaŋ ʋ tɩɩna weliŋ nɛ.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Ʋ gyɩ basɩ Wɩɩsɩ koro hʋ wɩya kɩ tɩya nala rɛ, a kɩ daga á Tɩɩna Yesu Krisita wɩya mɛ abee nyuduwo. Ka nal buloŋ mɛ bɩ tɔɔ ʋ ŋmanɩɩ dɩ ʋ ta basɩ Yesu wɩya doŋ.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Mɩyaŋ Pɔɔl aa yaa Yesu Krisita tʋŋtʋnnɩ rɛ sabɩ teŋ no a kɩ tɩya mamaa Roma tɩmma. Wɩɩsɩ rɛ yɩrɩ ŋ dɩ ŋ yaa ʋ kpambɩya aŋ bɩl lɩɩ ŋ dɩ ŋ basɩ ʋ wɩweliye hʋ a kɩ tɩya nala.

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: Ba saba Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ tɩyaŋ nɛ anɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ tɩŋ ʋ tɩŋdaala tɩyaŋ nɛ faafaa buloŋ, a we nyʋwa wɩweliye no wɩya.

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: Amɛ ʋ aa lɩɩ Wɩɩsɩ lee a yaa tɩpʋlʋŋ tɩɩna lugo tɩyaŋ kɛ, Wɩɩsɩ gyɩ kpa ʋ debal lɛ a kyisi ʋ sʋʋ tɩyaŋ, a daga anɩɩ ʋ yaa ʋ biye rɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Ʋ tɩyaŋ nɛ Wɩɩsɩ tɩŋa a lɩɩ ma abee bɔnyɛ, a leŋ á yaa ʋ kpambɩsɩ a basɩ ʋ wɩweliye hʋ kɩ tɩya dʋnɩya nala buloŋ dɩ ba laa ʋ wɩya di, a kɩ tɩŋa ʋ nyʋwa dɩ ʋ feŋ yɩrɩ.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Ɛɛwɩya, ŋ sabɩ teŋ no kɩ tɩya ma nala hʋ buloŋ Wɩɩsɩ aa kyo, a yɩrɩ ma, ma yaa ʋ nala Roma tɩyaŋ nɛ. Á kuwo Wɩɩsɩ abee á Tɩɩna Yesu Krisita bɔnyɛ abee ba fɩyɛlʋʋ sɩ we ma lee. Pɔɔl kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Ka dɩ ŋ na basɩ wɩɩ buloŋ, ŋ tɩŋ Yesu Krisita tɩyaŋ nɛ a kɩ kyʋwalɩ Wɩɩsɩ aŋgyʋwasʋ ma buloŋ wɩya, beewɩya dʋnɩya nala buloŋ aa nɩɩ gɛɛ hʋ ma aa laa Wɩɩsɩ wɩya di rɛ.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Wɩɩsɩ hʋ ŋ aa tɩŋ abee ŋ tɩdɩgɩ, a kɩ basɩ ʋ Biye wɩweliye hʋ, ʋ rɛ yaa ŋ daŋsɩya tɩɩna anɩɩ saŋa kɛ buloŋ dɩ mɩyaŋ nɛ ko kɩ kyʋwalɩ ʋ, ŋ aa kɩ liisi ma wɩya rɛ.

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: Ŋ aa sʋla ʋ rɛ anɩɩ dɩ ʋ ko yaa ʋ kyɛrɩ, nyɛ kɛ ʋ leŋ dɩ ŋ na ŋmanɩɩ a ko na ma.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Ŋ kyɛrɩ rɛ yaa dɩ ŋ ko na ma a pɛ ma tɩyaŋ abee Wɩɩsɩ Wiyesi Welii hʋ bɔnyɛ kɩna, dɩ ɛɛ wuwo leŋ dɩ ma marɩ sɩŋ weliŋ ma Wɩɩsɩ laadii tɩyaŋ.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Wɩɩ hʋ ŋ aa basɩ memii rɛ nyɛ: Dɩ mɩyaŋ nɛ ko ma lee ŋ Wɩɩsɩ laadii sɩ leŋ dɩ ma tenni polli, dɩ ma Wɩɩsɩ laadii mɛ leŋ dɩ ŋ teŋ mɛ polli.
